items.description: These Regulations disapply regulation 12 of the Motorways Traffic (England and Wales) Regulations 1982 (which prohibits certain vehicles from using the right hand or off side lane of a motorway carriageway with 3 or more lanes) in relation to the length of the northbound carriageway of the M621 Motorway defined in regulation 2.

items.title: The Education (Lower Primary Class Sizes) (Scotland) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations impose a limit on class sizes for Primary 1, 2 and 3 classes at schools managed by education authorities in Scotland.

items.title: The Scotland Act 1998 (Transitory and Transitional Provisions) (Grants to Members and Officeholders) Order 1999
items.description: This Order makes transitional provision for the payment by the Parliamentary corporation of resettlement grants and ill-health retirement grants to members of the Scottish Parliament, and of severance grants to holders of certain offices.

items.title: The Scotland Act 1998 (Transitory and Transitional Provisions) (Scottish Parliamentary Pension Scheme) Order 1999
items.description: This Order makes transitional provision for pensions for members and office holders of the Scottish Parliament.

items.title: The National Assembly for Wales (Assembly Members) (Salaries) (Limitation) Order 1999
items.description: Section 17 of the Government of Wales Act 1998 (“the Act”) empowers the Secretary of State by order to limit the salary payable to a member of the National Assembly for Wales (“Assembly member”) where that Assembly member is also in receipt of a salary from certain other public bodies.

items.title: The Local Authorities (Direct Labour Organisations) (Competition) (Wales) (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: Part III of the Local Government, Planning and Land Act 1980 (“the 1980 Act”) requires local authorities (including authorities who are treated as local authorities for that purpose) and development bodies, before undertaking construction or maintenance work through their direct labour organisations (DLO's), to have satisfied certain requirements as respects competitive tendering.

items.title: The Fees in the Registers of Scotland Amendment Order 1999
items.description: This Order amends, with effect from 21st April 1999, the Fees in the Registers of Scotland Order 1995 (“the 1995 Order”), so as to:

items.title: The Local Authorities (Members' Allowances) (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Local Authorities (Members' Allowances) Regulations 1991.

items.title: The Local Authorities (Members' Allowances) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1999
The Social Security (Incapacity, Earnings and Work Trials) Pilot Schemes Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations provide for two pilot schemes which modify the Social Security (Incapacity for Work) (General) Regulations 1995 (S.I. 1995/311) so that claimants of benefits which are dependent on incapacity for work and who live in the pilot scheme areas—

items.title: The Plant Breeders' Rights (Fees) (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations amend the Plant Breeders' Rights (Fees) Regulations 1998 so as to prescribe revised fees in respect of various matters relating to plant breeders' rights arising under the Plant Varieties Act 1997 or the Plant Breeders' Rights Regulations 1998 (regulation 2 and the Schedule).

items.title: The Seeds (National Lists of Varieties) (Fees) (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations amend the Seeds (National Lists of Varieties) (Fees) Regulations 1994 (as amended) so as to prescribe revised fees in respect of matters arising under the Seeds (National Lists of Varieties) Regulations 1982 (as amended) (regulation 2 and the Schedule).

items.title: The A13 Trunk Road (Tower Hamlets) Red Route Experimental Traffic Order 1998 Experimental Variation Order 1999
items.description: This version of the The A13 Trunk Road (Tower Hamlets) Red Route Experimental Traffic Order 1998 Experimental Variation Order 1999 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Patents (Amendment) Rules 1999
items.description: This version of the The Patents (Amendment) Rules 1999 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Patents (Fees) (Amendment) Rules 1999
items.description: This version of the The Patents (Fees) (Amendment) Rules 1999 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Scottish Parliamentary Elections (Returning Officers' Charges) Order 1999
items.description: This Order makes provision for the payment of constituency and regional returning officers at elections for membership of the Scottish Parliament in respect of their services and expenses in connection therewith.

items.title: The Scotland Act 1998 (Transitory and Transitional Provisions)(Standing Orders and Parliamentary Publications) Order 1999
items.description: This Order makes transitory and transitional provisions by virtue of section 129(1) of the Scotland Act 1998 (“the Act”) regarding the standing orders of the Scottish Parliament and the publication of statements made under the authority of the Parliament.

items.title: The Scotland Act 1998 (Transitory and Transitional Provisions) (Statutory Instruments) Order 1999
items.description: This Order makes transitory and transitional provisions in relation to Scottish statutory instruments (as construed in accordance with article 4(2)) and other statutory instruments or documents which are required by any enactment to be laid before the Scottish Parliament.

items.title: The Scotland Act 1998 (Transitory and Transitional Provisions)(Salaries and Allowances) Order 1999
items.description: This is an Order making provision for transitory and transitional purposes in connection with the coming into force of section 81(1) and (2) of the Scotland Act 1998 (c. 46) (“the Act”). The Order makes provision, to have effect until the coming into force of any provision made by the Scottish Parliament in accordance with section 81(1) of the Act, for the payment of salaries to–

items.title: The Scotland Act 1998 (Transitory and Transitional Provisions) (Administration of the Parliament) Order 1999
items.description: This Order makes provision for transitory and transitional purposes in connection with the coming into force, on 6th May 1999, of sections 20 (Clerk of the Parliament) and 21 (Scottish Parliamentary Corporate Body (“SPCB”)) of the Scotland Act 1998 (c. 46) (“the 1998 Act”).

items.title: The Education (Nursery Education and Early Years Development) (Wales) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations, which are made under sections 118(1), 120(1) and (3) and 121(1) and (9) of the School Standards and Framework Act 1998 and apply only in Wales, prescribe for the purposes of the new duty imposed by section 118 of the Act (under which a local education authority must secure sufficient provision of nursery education for their area), the minimum age in relation to which that new duty applies (regulation 2).

items.title: The Medway National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Amendment Order 1999
items.description: This Order amends the Order (“the Establishment Order”) which established the Medway National Health Service Trust.

items.title: The Welsh Language Schemes (Public Bodies) Order 1999
items.description: Under Part II of the Welsh Language Act 1993 (“the Act”) the Welsh Language Board may give a notice in writing to any public body to produce a Welsh Language scheme. Section 6 of the Act lists various public bodies for the purposes of Part II of the Act.

items.title: The National Assembly for Wales (Oath of Allegiance in Welsh) Order 1999
items.description: Section 20 of the Government of Wales Act 1998 provides that a member of the National Assembly for Wales shall (as soon as may be after he is returned as an Assembly member) take the oath of allegiance set out in section 2 of the Promissory Oaths Act 1868, or make the corresponding affirmation.

items.title: The Sex Discrimination (Gender Reassignment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations, which are made under section 2(2) of the European Communities Act 1972, extend the Sex Discrimination Act 1975 (“the 1975 Act”) to cover discrimination on grounds of gender reassignment in employment and vocational training, following the judgment of the European Court of Justice in Case No. C-13/94 P v S and Cornwall County Council. They come into force on .

items.title: The Bovines and Bovine Products (Trade) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations replace the Bovines and Bovine Products (Trade) Regulations 1998 (S.I. 1998 No. 1135). They give effect in Great Britain to Commission Decisions 98/692/EC (OJ No. L328, 4.12.98, p. 28) and 98/564/EC (OJ No. L273, 9.10.98, p. 37) which amended Council Decision 98/256/EC (OJ No. L113, 15.4.98, p. 32) concerning emergency measures to protect against bovine spongiform encephalopathy in relation to the despatch to third countries and member States of bovine animals and embryos and meat and other products and by-products derived from bovine animals.

items.title: The Transfer of Property etc. (Scottish Ministers) Order 1999
items.description: This Order provides for the transfer of certain property and liabilities of Ministers of the Crown and government departments to the Scottish Ministers in connection with the transfer to them of functions (referred to as “transferred functions”) under sections 53 (general transfer of functions), 63 (power to transfer functions) and 89 (power to adapt etc. cross-border public authorities) of the Scotland Act 1998 (c. 46) and also in connection with the functions (referred to as “non-ministerial functions”) of certain other office-holders who will form part of the Scottish Administration.

items.title: The Transfer of Property etc. (Lord Advocate) Order 1999
items.description: This Order provides for the transfer of certain property and liabilities of Ministers of the Crown and government departments to the Lord Advocate in connection with his retained functions under sections 52(6)(a) of the Scotland Act 1998 (c. 46) and also in connection with the functions of procurators fiscal and procurators fiscal depute who will form part of the Scottish Administration.

items.title: The Transfer of Property etc. (Scottish Parliamentary Corporate Body) Order 1999
items.description: This Order provides for the transfer of certain property and liabilities of Ministers of the Crown and government departments to the Scottish Parliamentary Corporate Body (SPCB) established by section 21 of the Scotland Act 1998 (c. 46) for the purposes of the Scottish Parliament or otherwise in connection with SPCB’s functions.

items.title: The Environment Act 1995 (Consequential Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations make an additional amendment to the Environmental Protection Act 1990 (“the 1990 Act”) as a result of the coming into force of provisions of the Environment Act 1995 which repeal section 17 (powers of entry) of the 1990 Act.

items.title: The Pesticides (Maximum Residue Levels in Crops, Food and Feeding Stuffs) (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations, which do not extend to Northern Ireland, are made under section 2(2) of the European Communities Act 1972 and amend the Pesticides (Maximum Residue Levels in Crops, Food and Feeding Stuffs) Regulations 1994 (“the principal Regulations”) (S.I. 1994/1985, amended by S.I. 1995/1483, 1996/1487, 1997/567 and 1998/2922).

items.title: The Fish Producers' Organisations (Formation Grants) Regulations 1999 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations give effect in the United Kingdom to the relevant provisions of Council Regulation 3759/92/EEC on the common organisation of the market in fishery and aquaculture products (OJ No. L388, 31.12.92, p. 1), Council Regulation 3140/82/EEC on granting and financing aid granted by Member States to producers' organisations in the fishery products sector (OJ No. L331, 26.11.82, p. 7), Commission Regulation 1452/83/EEC defining the administrative expenses of producers' organisations in the fishery products sector (OJ No. L149, 7.6.83, p. 5) and Council Regulation 2468/98/EC laying down the criteria and arrangements regarding Community structural assistance in the fisheries and aquaculture sector and the processing and marketing of its products (OJ No. L312, 20.11.98, p. 19), which together govern the payment of formation grants to fish producers' organisations. The Regulations enable the appropriate fisheries Minister to pay to a recognised fish or, for the first time, aquaculture producers' organisation an annual grant in respect of the administrative expenses incurred on its formation and during its operation for a period of three years following the date on which that organisation was recognised (regulation 3).

items.title: The District Salmon Fishery Boards Order 1999
items.description: This Order varies Schedule 2 to the Salmon Act 1986 by–

items.title: The Road Traffic (Permitted Parking Area and Special Parking Area) (County of East Sussex) (Borough of Hastings) Order 1999
items.description: This Order designates the Borough of Hastings, excluding a short section of the A21 trunk road 500 metres in length, as both a permitted parking area and a special parking area in accordance with Schedule 3 to the Road Traffic Act 1991 and applies with modifications various provisions of Part II of that Act to the designated area. The Order also modifies the Road Traffic Regulation Act 1984 in relation to the designated area.

items.title: The Civil Legal Aid (General) (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: This version of the The Civil Legal Aid (General) (Amendment) Regulations 1999 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Argyll and Clyde Acute Hospitals National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Amendment Order 1999
items.description: This Order substitutes a new article 3 in the Argyll and Clyde Acute Hospitals National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Order 1998 to empower the trust to build a new hospital at Larkfield, Greenock.

items.title: The Parole Board (Scotland) Amendment Rules 1999
items.description: These rules amend the Parole Board (Scotland) Rules 1993 in consequence of the Crime and Disorder Act 1998. That Act amended the Criminal Procedure (Scotland) Act 1995 so as to enable the courts to impose extended sentences on persons who have been convicted on indictment of sexual offences, or violent offences of four years or more. An extended sentence is the aggregate of the term of imprisonment which the court would otherwise have passed on the offender together with a further period for which the offender is to be subject to a licence.

items.title: The London Cab Order 1999
items.description: This Order increases the fares payable for the hiring of a motor cab licensed in the Metropolitan Police District and the City of London in respect of all journeys beginning and ending there. It also increases certain extra charges payable for hirings on public holidays, at weekends and at night.

items.title: The Cardiff and District Community National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Order 1999
items.description: This Order establishes the Cardiff and District Community National Health Service Trust, an NHS trust provided for in section 5 of the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990. It also provides for the functions of the trust (article 3). It specifies the operational date and accounting date of the trust (article 5). It specifies the value of assets in excess of which the Secretary of State is to consider the disposal of the asset (article 6).

items.title: The University Hospital of Wales and Llandough Hospital National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Order 1999
items.description: This Order establishes the University Hospital of Wales and Llandough Hospital National Health Service Trust, an NHS trust provided for in section 5 of the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990. It also provides for the functions of the trust (article 3). It specifies the operational date and accounting date of the trust (article 5). It specifies the value of assets in excess of which the Secretary of State is to consider the disposal of the asset (article 6).

items.title: The National Health Service Trusts (Wales) (Dissolution) Order 1999
items.description: This Order provides for the dissolution of the National Health Service Trusts set out in Schedule 1 and for the revocation of the relevant establishment orders and related amendment orders set out in Schedule 2.

items.title: The Education (Chief Inspector of Schools in England) Order 1999
items.description: This Order re-appoints Christopher Anthony Woodhead to be Her Majesty’s Chief Inspector of Schools in England for a period of four years from 1st September 1999.

items.title: The Education (Inspectors of Schools in England) Order 1999
items.description: This Order appoints the persons named in the Schedule as Her Majesty’s Inspectors of Schools in England.

items.title: The Air Navigation (Fourth Amendment) Order 1999
items.description: This Order further amends the Air Navigation (No. 2) Order 1995. In addition to minor and drafting amendments, the following changes are made:

items.title: The Consular Relations (Merchant Shipping) (Union of Soviet Socialist Republics) (Revocation) Order 1999
items.description: This Order revokes the Consular Relations (Merchant Shipping) (Union of Soviet Socialist Republics) Order 1970, which gave effect to the relevant provisions of the Treaty on Merchant Navigation between the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland and the Union of Soviet Socialist Republics signed at London on 3rd April 1968 (Cmnd. 5008). The Order comes into force on 6th May 1999.

items.title: The International Mobile Satellite Organisation (Immunities and Privileges) Order 1999
items.description: This Order confers privileges and immunities on the International Mobile Satellite Organisation, on representatives of its members and on its officers and experts. These privileges and immunities are conferred in accordance with an Agreement which has been negotiated between the Government of the United Kingdom and the International Mobile Satellite Organisation (Cm). The Order revokes the INMARSAT (Immunities and Privileges) Order 1980. The Order will enable Her Majesty’s Government to give effect to the Agreement, which will enter into force on signature. The Order will come into force on the date on which the Agreement enters into force.

items.title: The Scottish Adjacent Waters Boundaries Order 1999
items.description: This Order describes for the purposes of the Scotland Act 1998–

items.title: The Scottish Administration (Offices) Order 1999
items.description: This Order specifies the offices in the Scottish Administration which are not ministerial offices for the purposes of the Scotland Act 1998 (“the Act”).

items.title: The National Minimum Wage (Offshore Employment) Order 1999
items.description: This Order in Council has the effect of extending the provisions of the National Minimum Wage Act 1998 to workers who work, or ordinarily work, in the territorial waters of the United Kingdom.

items.title: The Education (Inspectors of Schools in Wales (No. 2) Order 1999
items.description: This Order appoints the persons named in the Schedule Her Majesty’s Inspectors of Schools in Wales

items.title: The Students' Allowances (Scotland) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations consolidate, with minor amendments, the Students' Allowances (Scotland) Regulations 1996 (“the 1996 Regulations”) and the subsequent amending instrument. They enable the Secretary of State to pay allowances to persons attending courses of education who fulfil certain criteria as to eligibility and they prescribe the conditions and requirements subject to which allowances may be paid.

items.title: The Health Boards (Membership and Procedure) (No.2) Amendment Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Health Boards (Membership and Procedure) (No.2) Regulations 1991 (“the principal Regulations”). Regulation 6(1)(a) of the principal Regulations, which disqualifies a person from being a member of a Health Board if he is chairman or a director of an NHS trust, is omitted.

items.title: The National Health Service Trusts (Membership and Procedure) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations further amend the National Health Service Trusts (Membership and Procedure) (Scotland) Regulations 1991 (“the principal Regulations”) which make provision concerning the membership and procedure of NHS trusts. The Regulations increase the maximum number of directors of an NHS trust from eleven to twelve where the trust is regarded as having a significant teaching commitment. In addition, the time limit of 1 year during which a person can be both a chairman or non-executive director of an NHS trust and a chairman, member, director or employee of another health service body is removed.

items.title: The National Health Service Trusts (Appointment of Trustees) (Scotland) Order 1999
items.description: This Order appoints the chairman, non-executive directors and executive directors and their successors in office of each of the National Health Service Trusts specified in Schedule 1 to the Order as trustees for the purposes of holding any property which is to be held in trust on behalf of that Trust.

items.title: The Housing (Right to Acquire) (Discount) Order 1999
items.description: This Order specifies the amount of discount for the purposes of section 17 of the Housing Act 1996 (the right to acquire). Tenants of registered social landlords who have a right to acquire their homes under sections 16 and 17 of the 1996 Act are able to do so with a discount from the purchase price. The amount of the discount varies according to the area in which the dwelling is situated.

items.title: The Miscellaneous Food Additives (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations, which apply to Great Britain, amend the Miscellaneous Food Additives Regulations 1995, as amended (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Public Offers of Securities (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Public Offers of Securities Regulations 1995, which implement Council Directive 89/298/EEC, coordinating the requirements for the drawing-up, scrutiny and distribution of the prospectus to be published when transferable securities are offered to the public (OJ L124, 5.5.89, p. 8). They provide for a wider definition of the circumstances in which offers of Euro-securities may be advertised whilst benefiting from an exemption from the requirement to publish a prospectus.

items.title: A4 Trunk Road (Hammersmith & Fulham and Kensington & Chelsea) Red Route Traffic Order 1999
items.description: This version of the A4 Trunk Road (Hammersmith & Fulham and Kensington & Chelsea) Red Route Traffic Order 1999 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Water Supply (Water Fittings) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations make provision for preventing contamination and waste of water supplied by a water undertaker. They do not apply to certain water fittings in connection with water supplied for non-domestic purposes, or to water fittings lawfully installed before 1st July 1999: regulation 2.

items.title: The Magistrates' Courts (Forms) (Amendment) Rules 1999
items.description: These Rules prescribe a new Form 27A, for use in cases where statements are served under the procedure in section 12 of the Magistrates' Courts Act 1980, as amended by the Magistrates' Courts (Procedure) Act 1998, and a new Form 28A, for a defendant to record his intended plea and statement of mitigation.

items.title: The Offshore Installations (Safety Zones) (No. 2) Order 1999
items.description: This order establishes, under section 22 of the Petroleum Act 1987, safety zones having a radius of 500 metres from the specified point, around each installation specified in the Schedule to this order and stationed in waters to which section 21(7) of that Act applies (these include territorial waters and waters in areas designated under section 1(7) of the Continental Shelf Act 1964 (c. 29)) (Article 2).

items.title: The Northern Ireland (Emergency Provisions) Act 1996 (Code of Practice) Order 1999
items.description: This Order appoints 24th May 1999 as the date on which the code of practice made under section 53A(1)(a) of the Northern Ireland (Emergency Provisions) Act 1996 (“the 1996 Act”) will come into operation.

items.title: The Northern Ireland (Sentences) Act 1998 (Specified Organisations) Order 1999
items.description: This Order specifies those organisations which the Secretary of State believes are concerned in terrorism connected with the affairs of Northern Ireland, or in promoting or encouraging it, and have not established or are not maintaining a complete and unequivocal ceasefire. The organisations mentioned in the Schedule are specified organisations for the purposes of the Northern Ireland (Sentences) Act 1998 (c. 35), which makes provision about the release on licence of certain prisoners serving sentences of imprisonment in Northern Ireland. The effect of specifying an organisation is that a prisoner who is, or who would be likely to become, a supporter of such an organisation is ineligible for release under the Act.

items.title: The Income Support (General) (Standard Interest Rate Amendment) (No. 4) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations amend Schedule 3 to the Income Support (General) Regulations 1987 (S.I. 1987/1967), as substituted and amended, with respect to the standard rate of interest applicable to a loan which qualifies for income support under that Schedule to those Regulations. The new rate is 7.08 per cent. (regulation 2). The Regulations also revoke, with a saving provision, regulations which made a previous amendment to that standard rate of interest (regulation 3).

items.title: The City of Edinburgh (Guided Busways) Order (Section 51) Order 1999
The Southend Community Care Services National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Amendment Order 1999
items.description: This Order amends the Order (“the Establishment Order”) which established the Southend Community Care Services National Health Service Trust.

items.title: The St George’s Healthcare National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Amendment Order 1999
items.description: This Order amends the Order (“the Establishment Order”) which established the St George’s Healthcare National Health Service Trust.

items.title: The South Birmingham Mental Health National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Amendment Order 1999
items.description: This Order amends the Order (“the Establishment Order”) which established the South Birmingham Mental Health National Health Service Trust.

items.title: The Lerwick Harbour Revision Order 1999
items.description: This Order:–

items.title: The Northern Ireland (Emergency Provisions) Act 1996 (Audio Recording of Interviews) Order 1999
items.description: This Order requires the audio recording, in accordance with the code of practice made under section 53A(1)(a) of the Northern Ireland (Emergency Provisions) Act 1996 as it has effect for the time being, of

items.title: The Countryside Access (Amendment) Regulations 1999 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Countryside Access Regulations 1994 (“the principal Regulations”) which implement in part a zonal programme approved by the European Commission under Article 7 of Council Regulation (EEC) No. 2078/92 (OJ No. L215, 30.7.92, p. 85) on agricultural production methods compatible with the requirements of the protection of the environment and the maintenance of the countryside.

items.title: The Environmentally Sensitive Areas (Wales) Designation Orders (Amendment) Order 1999
items.description: This Order amends the environmentally sensitive areas designation orders made under section 18 of the Agriculture Act 1986 (“the 1986 Act”) designating areas in Wales as environmentally sensitive areas, which implement in part a zonal programme approved by the European Commission under article 7 of Council Regulation (EEC) No. 2078/92 (OJ No. L215, 30.7.92, p. 85) on agricultural production methods compatible with the requirements of the protection of the environment and the maintenance of the countryside.

items.title: The Land in Care Scheme (Tir Gofal) (Wales) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations, which apply to Wales, contain the provisions of a new agri-environment scheme implementing in part a zonal programme approved by the European Commission under Article 7 of Council Regulation (EEC) No. 2078/92 (OJ No. L215, 30.7.92, p. 85) on agricultural production methods compatible with the requirements for the protection of the environment and the maintenance of the countryside. The new agri-environment scheme, which replaces existing agri-environment schemes applying to Wales, is entitled the Land in Care Scheme or Tir Gofal (which Welsh term means land which is in the care of any person who has an interest in it).

items.title: The Countryside Stewardship (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations, which apply to England (including the Isles of Scilly), amend the Countryside Stewardship Regulations 1998 (S.I. 1998/1327) (“the principal Regulations”), which make provision supplementing Council Regulation (EEC) No. 2078/92 (OJ No. L215, 30.7.92, p.85), as amended by Commission Regulation (EC) No. 2772/95 (OJ No. L 288, 1.12.95, p.35) which was in turn rectified by Commission Regulation (EC) No. 1962/96 (OJ No. L259, 12.10.96 p.7) on agricultural methods compatible with the requirements of the protection of the environment and the maintenance of the countryside.

items.title: The Education (School Performance Information) (England) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations, made under sections 408, 537 and 537A of the Education Act 1996, apply in relation to schools in England. They consolidate with amendments the Education (School Performance Information) (England) Regulations 1998 (“the 1998 Regulations”) as amended, which are revoked.

items.title: The Beef Special Premium (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations amend, with effect from 22nd May 1999, the Beef Special Premium Regulations 1996 (S.I. 1996/3241) (“the principal Regulations”) which implemented Council Regulation (EEC) 805/68 (OJ No. L148, 28.6.68, p. 24) on the common organisation of the market in beef and veal, as last amended by Council Regulation (EC) 1633/98 (OJ No. L210, 28.7.98, p. 17), and as applied in detail by Commission Regulation (EEC) 3886/92 (OJ No. L391, 31.12.92, p. 20), as last amended by Commission Regulation (EC) 2604/98 (OJ No. L328, 4.12.98, p. 6).

items.title: The Government of Wales Act 1998 (Commencement No. 3) Order 1999
items.description: This version of the The Government of Wales Act 1998 (Commencement No. 3) Order 1999 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Scottish Criminal Cases Review Commission (Application to Summary Proceedings) Order 1999
items.description: The Scottish Criminal Cases Review Commission is a new statutory body corporate that is established under Part XA of the Criminal Procedure (Scotland) Act 1995. The Commission will consider convictions, sentences and findings made in solemn proceedings with a view to referring a case to the High Court if they believe that a miscarriage of justice may have occurred and that it is in the interests of justice that a reference should be made. The Commission will begin to exercise their statutory powers to review cases on 1st April 1999 by virtue of the Crime and Punishment (Scotland) Act 1997 (Commencement No. 5 and Transitional Provision and Savings) Order 1999 (S.I. 1999/652).

items.title: The Local Government Act 1988 (Defined Activities) (Exemptions) (Aylesbury Vale District Council and Thanet District Council) Order 1999
items.description: Under Part I (competition) of the Local Government Act 1988 work falling within certain defined activities may be carried out by local authorities only if particular conditions are fulfilled.

items.title: The M606/M62 Motorways (Bradford Link) (Speed Limit) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations impose a 50 miles per hour speed limit on the length of motorway linking the M606 Motorway (southbound carriageway) with the M62 Motorway (westbound carriageway).

items.title: The Education (Baseline Assessment) (Wales) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations make provision in support of baseline assessment, introduced by Chapter I of Part IV of the Education Act 1997.

items.title: The Criminal Justice Act 1993 (Commencement No. 10) Order 1999
items.description: This Order brings into force on 1st June 1999 those provisions of the Criminal Justice Act 1993 which are not already in force, extending the jurisdiction of courts in England and Wales in relation to certain offences of dishonesty and blackmail. By virtue of section 78(5) of the Act, nothing in any of those provisions applies to any act, omission or other event occurring before that date.

items.title: The Disability Discrimination Act 1995 (Commencement Order No. 6) Order 1999
items.description: This Order provides for the coming into force on 26th April 1999 of provisions in the Disability Discrimination Act 1995 enabling the making of Regulations.

items.title: The Disability Discrimination (Services and Premises) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations are made under the Disability Discrimination Act 1995 (“the Act”). S21(2) of the Act concerns the duty of service providers, when a physical feature makes it impossible or unreasonably difficult for disabled persons to access its service, to remove the feature, alter it so it no longer has that effect, provide a reasonable means of avoiding it or provide a reasonable alternative method of making it available to disabled persons. These Regulations make provision for various things to be treated as physical features.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Amnesic Shellfish Poisoning) Order 1999 Revocation Order 1999
The Swansea-Manchester Trunk Road (A483) (South of Welshpool Bypass Detrunking) Order 1999
The Magistrates' Courts (Procedure) Act 1998 (Commencement No. 2) Order 1999
items.description: This Order brings into force section 1 of the Magistrates' Courts (Procedure) Act 1998 on 4th May 1999. Section 5 of that Act came into force on Royal Assent; sections 2, 3 and 4 were brought into force on 1st September 1998 by the Magistrates' Courts (Procedure) Act 1998 (Commencement No. 1) Order 1998.

items.title: The A40 Trunk Road (Ealing) Red Route (Clearway) Traffic Order 1995 Variation Order 1999
items.description: This version of the The A40 Trunk Road (Ealing) Red Route (Clearway) Traffic Order 1995 Variation Order 1999 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The School Standards and Framework Act 1998 (Commencement No. 5 and Saving and Transitional Provisions) Order 1999
items.description: This Order brings certain provisions of the School Standards and Framework Act 1998 into force on 1st February 1999 and 10th March 1999 respectively.

items.title: The A1 Trunk Road (Barnet) Red Route (Speed Limit) Experimental Traffic Order 1999
items.description: This version of the The A1 Trunk Road (Barnet) Red Route (Speed Limit) Experimental Traffic Order 1999 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Traffic Areas (Reorganisation) (Wales) Order 1999
items.description: This Order creates a new traffic area which will cover Wales and be known as the Welsh Traffic Area. The area previously within the South-Wales Traffic Area (which is abolished) and the former counties of Clwyd and Gwynedd within the North-Western Traffic Area are transferred by this Order to the Welsh Traffic Area.

items.title: The Road Traffic (Parking Adjudicators) (London) (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: The Road Traffic (Parking Adjudicators) (London) Regulations 1993 (S.I. 1993/1202) (“the 1993 Regulations”) prescribed the procedure to be followed by parking adjudicators acting under the provisions of Part II of the Road Traffic Act 1991 in relation to parking in London. These Regulations amend the 1993 Regulations so as to apply them to parking adjudicators when acting as traffic adjudicators pursuant to Part II (bus lanes) of the London Local Authorities Act 1996 and to make consequential modifications.

items.title: The Social Landlords (Additional Purposes or Objects) (No. 2) Order 1999
items.description: Section 2 of the Housing Act 1996 specifies the bodies that are eligible for registration as social landlords. For two categories of body (a society registered under the Industrial and Provident Societies Act 1965 and a company registered under the Companies Act 1985) the section sets out conditions that must be satisfied for registration. In addition to the core purposes or objects, there are permissible additional purposes or objects.

items.title: The Exchange of Securities (General) (Amendment) Rules 1999
items.description: These Rules further amend the Exchange of Securities (General) Rules 1979. Rule 2 amends the definition of “CGO Service member” to reflect the transfer of the responsibility for operating the CGO Service from the Bank of England to CRESTCo Limited. This is intended to occur on a contractual basis on 24th May 1999 but, if notice is given in the London, Edinburgh and Belfast Gazettes that the transfer of responsibility will not occur until a later date, the commencement of Rule 2 is postponed until the day on which the responsibility is transferred (which will be specified in either the original notice or a subsequent notice published in the same Gazettes prior to the specified day).

items.title: The Stock Transfer (Gilt-edged Securities) (CGO Service) (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations amend the Stock Transfer (Gilt-edged Securities) (CGO Service) Regulations 1985 to reflect the transfer of the responsibility for operating the CGO Service from the Bank of England to CRESTCo Limited. This is intended to occur on a contractual basis on 24th May 1999 but, if notice is given in the London, Edinburgh and Belfast Gazettes that the transfer of responsibility will not occur until a later date, the commencement of these Regulations is postponed until the day on which the responsibility is transferred (which will be specified in either the original notice or a subsequent notice published in the same Gazettes prior to the specified day).

items.title: The Financial Markets and Insolvency (CGO Service) Regulations 1999
items.description: The Regulations make amendments to the Financial Markets and Insolvency Regulations 1991 (the principal Regulations) which are necessary to reflect the fact that operation of the Central Gilts Office Service (CGO Service) is to be transferred by the Bank of England to CRESTCo Limited. This is intended to occur on a contractual basis on 24th May 1999 but, if a notice is given in the London, Edinburgh and Belfast Gazettes that the transfer of responsibility will not occur until a later date, nothing in the Regulations will take effect until the day on which the responsibility is transferred. This will be specified either in the original notice or in a subsequent notice published in the same Gazettes prior to the specified day. The effect of the transfer will be that persons who originally played a role in the operation of the CGO Service under contract with the Bank of England will in future do so under contract with CRESTCo Limited.

items.title: The Stock Transfer (Gilt-edged Securities) (Exempt Transfer) (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations amend the seven sets of principal Stock Transfer (Gilt-edged Securities) (Exempt Transfer) Regulations which enable securities of the descriptions specified in them to be transferred by a CGO Service member through the medium of the CGO Service. The amendments, which relate to definitions in the principal Regulations, enable those Regulations to continue to apply after the responsibility for operating the CGO Service is transferred from the Bank of England to CRESTCo Limited. This is intended to occur on a contractual basis on 24th May 1999 but, if notice is given in the London, Edinburgh and Belfast Gazettes that the transfer of responsibility will not occur until a later date, the commencement of these Regulations is postponed until the day on which the responsibility is transferred (which will be specified in either the original notice or a subsequent notice published in the same Gazettes prior to the specified day).

items.title: The General Optical Council (Education Committee Rules) Order of Council 1999
items.description: The Rules approved by this Order make provision for the new composition of the Education Committee of the General Optical Council to reflect changes in the composition of the General Council made by the General Optical Council (Membership) Order of Council 1998. They replace rules approved by the General Optical Council (Education Committee Rules) Order of Council 1974 as amended by the General Optical Council (Education Committee Rules) (Amendment) Order of Council 1984.

items.title: The Local Government Pension Scheme (Miscellaneous Provisions) Regulations 1999
items.description: These regulations make various amendments to the Local Government Pension Scheme Regulations 1997 (“the principal Regulations”), the Local Government Pension Scheme (Transitional Provisions) Regulations 1997 and the Local Government Pension Scheme (Provision of Information, Administrative Expenses and Restitution) Regulations 1997.

items.title: The Housing (Preservation of Right to Buy) (Amendment) Regulations 1999
The European Parliamentary Elections Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations, which replace the Regulations revoked by Schedule 5, apply in Great Britain. They provide for the conduct of European Parliamentary elections under the regional list system of election. That system was introduced by amendments made to the European Parliamentary Elections Act 1978 by the European Parliamentary Elections Act 1999.

items.title: The Building Societies (Merger Notification Statement) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations prescribe the information to be sent to members of a building society where the Building Societies Commission has directed the society, under section 42B of the Building Societies Act 1986, to resolve to transfer the engagements of the society to one or more other societies by resolution of the board of directors of the society rather than by resolution of its members.

items.title: The Civil Procedure (Modification of Enactments) Order 1999
items.description: This version of the The Civil Procedure (Modification of Enactments) Order 1999 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Luton and Dunstable Hospital National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Amendment Order 1999
items.description: This Order amends the Order (“the Establishment Order”) which established the Luton and Dunstable Hospital National Health Service Trust.

items.title: The Broadcasting (Restrictions on the Holding of Licences) (Amendment) Order 1999
items.description: This order amends the Broadcasting (Restrictions on the Holding of Licences) Order 1991 by substituting a new sub-paragraph (a) of article 16(1) and revoking article 16(3), (4) and (5) so as to omit the words “domestic satellite service” following the amendments to the Broadcasting Act 1990 which were made by the Satellite Television Regulations 1997 (S.I. 1997/1682) and to permit any of the persons mentioned in Part V of Schedule 2 to the Broadcasting Act 1990 to hold a licence to provide a local delivery service.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Rules of the Court of Session Amendment No. 3) (External Orders Affecting Proceeds of Crime) 1999
items.description: This Act of Sederunt makes amendments to the Rules of the Court of Session 1994. The amendments are mainly consequential upon Orders in Council under sections 40 and 43 of the Proceeds of Crime (Scotland) Act 1995, but paragraph 2(7)(b) and (c) corrects an omission and an error in rule 76.4; paragraph 2(7)(d) makes provision for intimation of an interlocutor making a restraint order to certain persons not affected by that order; and paragraph 2(7)(e) makes new provision both as part of the consequential amendments and in relation to rule 76.4 as it already applied.

items.title: The Lay Representatives (Rights of Audience) Order 1999
items.description: This Order provides for lay representatives to continue to exercise rights of audience in small claims cases in the county courts. Section 11 of the Courts and Legal Services Act 1990 has been amended to facilitate the making of new Civil Procedure Rules, under which small claims cases are no longer referred to arbitration, but are allocated to a small claims track. This Order does not alter current practice, but is made as a consequence of those changes.

items.title: The Recreation Grounds (Revocation of Parish Council Byelaws) Order 1999
items.description: This Order revokes the byelaws specified in the Schedule to the Order, which were made in exercise of the powers conferred on parish councils by section 8(1)(d) of the Local Government Act 1894 (56 and 57 Vict. c. 73). The relevant part of that section was repealed by section 272(1) of, and Schedule 30 to, the Local Government Act 1972. The byelaws continued in force notwithstanding the repeal by virtue of section 262(1) of the 1972 Act. The byelaws are revoked as being substantially superseded by byelaws made under other enactments.

items.title: The Plant Protection Products (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Plant Protection Products Regulations 1995 (S.I. 1995/887) as amended (“the principal Regulations”) which implement in Great Britain Council Directive 91/414/EEC concerning the placing of plant protection products on the market (OJ No. L230, 19.8.91, p. 1 as read with OJ No. L170, 25.6.92, p. 40) (“the Directive”), as amended.

items.title: The Income Support (General) (Standard Interest Rate Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations amend Schedule 3 to the Income Support (General) Regulations 1987 (S.I. 1987/1967), as substituted and amended, with respect to the standard rate of interest applicable to a loan which qualifies for income support under that Schedule to those Regulations. The new rate is 8.24 per cent. (regulation 2). The Regulations also revoke, with a saving provision, regulations which made a previous amendment to that standard rate of interest (regulation 3).

items.title: The Social Security Revaluation of Earnings Factors Order 1999
items.description: This Order is made consequent upon a review under section 148 of the Social Security Administration Act 1992.

items.title: The Education (National Curriculum) (Key Stage 1 Assessment Arrangements) (England) Order 1999
items.description: This Order revokes and replaces the Education (National Curriculum) (Assessment Arrangements for the Core Subjects) (Key Stage 1) (England) Order 1995. It specifies the assessment arrangements for the core subjects in the final year of the first key stage, as they apply to maintained schools in England. “Maintained schools” are now defined to mean, in relation to the period before 1st September 1999, any county, voluntary or grant-maintained school or any maintained special or grant-maintained special school which is not established in a hospital and, in relation to the period on and after that date, to mean any community, foundation or voluntary school or any community or foundation special school not established in a hospital (article 3(1)).

items.title: The Education (Relevant Areas for Consultation on Admission Arrangements) Regulations 1999
items.description: Section 89(2) of the School Standards and Framework Act 1998 requires the admission authority for every maintained school to consult other admission authorities before determining the school’s admission arrangements for a school year. Under section 89(2)(b) this includes a duty to consult all admission authorities for schools in “the relevant area”, which is defined by section 89(3) as the local authority’s area or such other area as is determined by or in accordance with regulations. These Regulations make provision for the relevant areas to be determined by local education authorities and, in certain cases, redetermined by the adjudicator or (in Wales) the Secretary of State. They come into force on 1st April 1999.

items.title: The Education (Objections to Admission Arrangements) Regulations 1999
items.description: Section 90 of the School Standards and Framework Act 1998 enables objections to be referred to the adjudicator or (in Wales) the Secretary of State about admission arrangements for maintained schools which have been determined under section 89 of the Act. Objections may be made by the admission authorities for other maintained schools and, in certain circumstances, by parents.

items.title: The Rail Vehicle Accessibility (Midland Metro T69 Vehicles) (Exemption No. 2) Order 1999
items.description: This Order authorises the use of T69 Vehicles numbers 1 to 16 on Line 1 of the Midland Metro System although they do not conform with all the requirements of the Rail Vehicle Accessibility Regulations 1998 concerning public address systems. It sets a time limit on the authorisation.

items.title: The Gaming Clubs (Hours and Charges) (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations increase the maximum charges which may be made for admission to gaming on bingo club premises from £8.50 to £10.00.

items.title: The Amusements with Prizes (Variation of Monetary Limits) Order 1999
items.description: This Order (which extends to England and Wales and Scotland) increases the monetary limits specified in section 16(3) of the Lotteries and Amusements Act 1976 (c. 32) and revokes previous Orders which are superseded or spent.

items.title: The Education (Determination of Admission Arrangements) Regulations 1999
items.description: Section 89 of the School Standards and Framework Act 1998 provides that the admission authority for every maintained school must determine the school’s admission arrangements for each school year in accordance with the requirements set out in that section. These Regulations make provision for a number of purposes relating to the requirements of section 89. The Regulations come into force on 1st April 1999, except that regulations 3 to 7 do not apply for arrangements to admit pupils any earlier than the school year 2001/2002 (S.I. 1998/3165 deals with admission arrangements for the school year 2000/2001).

items.title: The Gaming Act (Variation of Monetary Limits) Order 1999
items.description: This Order (which extends to England and Wales and Scotland) increases the monetary limits specified in sections 20(3) and (8) and 21(2) of the Gaming Act 1968 (c. 65) and described in articles 2 to 4 of the Order, and revokes previous Orders which are superseded or spent.

items.title: The Federal Republic of Yugoslavia (Supply, Sale and Export of Petroleum and Petroleum Products) (Penalties and Licences) Regulations 1999 (revoked)
items.description: Council Regulation (EC) No. 900/99 of 29 April 1999 prohibits the supply, sale and export to the Federal Republic of Yugoslavia of specified petroleum and petroleum products. These Regulations–

items.title: The A3220 Trunk Road (Kensington & Chelsea and Hammersmith & Fulham) Red Route Traffic Order 1999
items.description: This version of the The A3220 Trunk Road (Kensington & Chelsea and Hammersmith & Fulham) Red Route Traffic Order 1999 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Secure Remands and Committals (Prescribed Description of Children and Young Persons) Order 1999
items.description: This Order prescribes girls and boys aged 12, 13 or 14 and girls aged 15 or 16 as children and young persons who, upon being remanded to local authority accommodation, may be required to be placed and kept in secure accommodation by a court under section 23(4) of the Children and Young Persons Act 1969 (as that section is amended by section 97 of the Crime and Disorder Act 1998).

items.title: The National Crime Squad (Complaints) (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Crime Squad (Complaints) Regulations 1998 (the 1998 Regulations) in relation to complaints against seconded police members.

items.title: The Local Government (Publication of Staffing Information) (England) (Revocation) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations revoke the Local Government (Publication of Staffing Information) (England) Regulations 1995 which require local and certain other authorities in England to publish information specified in the Local Government (Publication of Staffing Information) (England) Code 1995 in the manner and form, and on the occasions, specified in the Code.

items.title: The European Parliamentary Elections (Northern Ireland) (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations amend the European Parliamentary Elections (Northern Ireland) Regulations 1986 (“the 1986 Regulations”).

items.title: The Education (School Information) (Wales) (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: Section 92(1) and (6) of the School Standards and Framework Act 1998 place local education authorities under a duty to publish such information about the admissions arrangements of maintained schools in their area in such manner as may be prescribed by regulations. Section 537 of the Education Act 1996 enables the Secretary of State by regulations to impose additional requirements relating to the publication of information.

items.title: The NCIS (Complaints) (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations amend the NCIS (Complaints) Regulations 1998 (the 1998 Regulations) in relation to complaints against seconded police members.

items.title: The National Health Service (Clinical Negligence Scheme) (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations further amend the National Health Service (Clinical Negligence Scheme) Regulations 1996 which established a Scheme whereby NHS trusts and certain other bodies providing NHS services may make provision for meeting liabilities to third parties in connection with personal injury arising out of negligence in the carrying out of their functions.

items.title: The National Health Service (Existing Liabilities Scheme) (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations further amend the National Health Service (Existing Liabilities Scheme) Regulations 1996 which established a Scheme whereby bodies providing NHS services may make provision for meeting liabilities incurred before 1st April 1995 to third parties in connection with personal injury arising out of negligence in the carrying out of their functions.

items.title: The Warehousekeepers and Owners of Warehoused Goods Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations come into force on 1st October 1999.

items.title: The Crime and Disorder Act 1998 (Commencement No. 4) Order 1999
items.description: This Order brings sections 43 to 45, 97 and 98 of the Crime and Disorder Act 1998 into force on 1st June 1999. These provisions relate to time limits and to the remand and committal of children and young persons. The Order also brings into force on that date a number of minor related provisions.

items.title: The Land Registration Rules 1999
items.description: These rules amend the Land Registration Rules 1925 so as to:

items.title: The Criminal Justice Act 1991 (Commencement No. 3) (Amendment) Order 1999
items.description: This Order amends the Criminal Justice Act 1991 (Commencement No. 3) Order 1992 (S.I. 1992/333) to bring into force on 1st June 1999 minor supplementary provisions relating to the committal for sentence of children and young persons to local authority secure accommodation.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Rules of the Court of Session Amendment No. 4) (References to the Court of Justice of the European Communities) 1999
items.description: This Act of Sederunt amends rules 65.2 and 65.3 of the Rules of the Court of Session (which relate to the form and preparation of references to the Court of Justice of the European Communities).

items.title: Act of Adjournal (Criminal Procedure Rules Amendment No. 2) 1999
items.description: This Act of Adjournal adds a new paragraph (3) to rule 31.5 of the Criminal Procedure Rules 1996 (which relates to references to the Court of Justice of the European Communities) requiring parties to have regard, in drafting references in Form 31.5, to guidance notes set out in the Notes Appendix (which is also added to the Rules by this Act). In addition, a new, more detailed version of Form 31.5 is substituted for the existing form.

items.title: The Education (Funding for Teacher Training) Designation Order 1999
items.description: This Order designates Bexley London Borough Council, The London Diocesan Board for Schools (a charitable company limited by guarantee with the registered charity number 313000), and United Synagogue (a charitable unincorporated association with the registered charity number 242552) as institutions eligible for funding under Part I of the Education Act 1994.

items.title: The Durham College of Agriculture and Horticulture (Dissolution) Order 1999
items.description: This Order dissolves with effect from 1st June 1999 the further education corporation established to conduct Durham College of Agriculture and Horticulture. It provides for the transfer of its property, rights and liabilities to East Durham and Houghall Community College, and secures the rights of its employees by applying section 26(2) to (4) of the Further and Higher Education Act 1992.

items.title: The East Durham Community College (Dissolution) Order 1999
items.description: This Order dissolves with effect from 1st June 1999 the further education corporation established to conduct East Durham Community College. It provides for the transfer of its property, rights and liabilities to East Durham and Houghall Community College, and secures the rights of its employees by applying section 26(2) to (4) of the Further and Higher Education Act 1992.

items.title: The Education (Adjudicators Inquiry Procedure etc.) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations make provision for the holding of local inquiries by adjudicators. They establish a timetable for each procedural step, and provide for exchange of information between interested parties in addition to regulating matters relating to the conduct of the inquiry itself and to the notification of the adjudicator’s decision.

items.title: The Education (Head Teachers) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations make transitional provision in relation to the prospective repeal of Part II of the Education Act 1996. In relation to county, voluntary and maintained special schools organised in two or more departments, each with a head teacher, section 132 of that Act provides that provisions of the Act conferring functions on the head teacher have effect as if each department was a separate school. It is proposed to commence the repeal on 1st September 1999 (the appointed day for the purposes of section 20(7) of the School Standards and Framework Act 1998) when the schools to which section 132 relates will enter new categories.

items.title: The Road Traffic (Permitted Parking Area and Special Parking Area) (County Borough of Neath Port Talbot) Order 1999
items.description: This Order designates the County Borough of Neath Port Talbot as both a permitted parking area and a special parking area in accordance with Schedule 3 to the Road Traffic Act 1991 and applies with modifications specified provisions of Part II of that Act to the areas so designated (referred to in the Order as “the parking area”). The Order also modifies the Road Traffic Regulation Act 1984 in relation to that area (Schedule 2).

items.title: The Pembrokeshire (Llangwm and Hook Community) Order 1999
items.description: This Order constitutes a new community, to be known as Hook, in the County of Pembrokeshire as from 1st April 1999.

items.title: The School Standards and Framework Act 1998 (School Attendance Targets) (Modification) Regulations 1999
items.description: Section 63 of the School Standards and Framework Act 1998 gives power to the Secretary of State to make regulations relating to the setting of school attendance targets by governing bodies of maintained schools.

items.title: The Government of Wales Act 1998 (Commencement No. 5) Order 1999
items.description: This Order brings into force on 1st May and 1st July 1999 respectively, the following provisions of the Government of Wales Act 1998–

items.title: The A40 Trunk Road (Ealing) Red Route Traffic Order 1995 Variation Order 1999
items.description: This version of the The A40 Trunk Road (Ealing) Red Route Traffic Order 1995 Variation Order 1999 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Road Traffic (Permitted Parking Area and Special Parking Area) (County of Kent) (Borough of Maidstone) (Amendment) Order 1999
items.description: This Order amends the Road Traffic (Permitted Parking Area and Special Parking Area) (County of Kent) (Borough of Maidstone) Order 1997 (“the principal Order”) which designated a permitted parking area and a special parking area in the Borough of Maidstone.

items.title: The Environment Act 1995 (Commencement No. 15) Order 1999
items.description: Article 2 brings into force on 29th April 1999 paragraphs 161 to 163 of Schedule 22 to the Environment Act 1995, which insert sections into the Water Resources Act 1991 concerning notices requiring persons to carry out anti-pollution works and operations.

items.title: The Social Security (Adjudication) (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security (Adjudication) Regulations 1995. They make provision for the effective date of review decisions where entitlement to an attendance allowance or a disability living allowance is reduced or removed because the person does not satisfy the conditions of entitlement. They also provide for the effective date of a decision that a person is not, or is not to be treated as, incapable of work. The effective date provisions apply to revised entitlement to benefits where receipt of other benefits is a condition (new regulation 59A(4)).

items.title: The Registration of Births, Deaths and Marriages (Fees) (Amendment) Order 1999
items.description: This Order amends the Registration of Births, Deaths and Marriages (Fees) Order 1998 by inserting an additional fee which is to be payable where a certificate of birth, death or marriage is issued by a custodian of the register other than a registrar or superintendent registrar under the Social Security Administration Act 1992 from 1st June 1999.

items.title: The Child Support Commissioners (Procedure) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations make provision for the procedure to be followed in proceedings before a Child Support Commissioner under the Child Support Act 1991. These Regulations revoke the Child Support Commissioners (Procedure) Regulations 1992 and amending instruments and are necessary as a result of the introduction of the unified appeals tribunals established under the Social Security Act 1998.

items.title: The Wirral Tramway Order 1999
items.description: This Order authorises Wirral Metropolitan Borough Council to construct works for the purpose of extending the Wirral Tramway from the present terminus at Egerton Bridge to Taylor Street in the Borough of Wirral and temporarily to use land for the construction or maintenance of those works.

items.title: The Housing (Right to Acquire or Enfranchise) (Designated Rural Areas) Order 1999
items.description: This Order designates the areas specified in the Schedule as rural areas for the purposes of section 1AA(3)(a) of the Leasehold Reform Act 1967 (additional right to enfranchise) and section 17 of the Housing Act 1996 (the right to acquire). The right of tenants with long leases to enfranchise under section 1AA of the 1967 Act and the right of tenants of registered social landlords to acquire their homes under section 16 and 17 of the 1996 Act do not apply in respect of properties in areas which have been designated as rural areas. In the case of tenants with long leases, the additional conditions in section 1AA(3)(b) and (c) of the 1967 Act must be fulfilled before the right to enfranchise is excluded.

items.title: The Chiropractors Act 1994 (Commencement No. 2) Order 1999
items.description: This Order brings into force on 13th May 1999 the following provisions of the Chiropractors Act 1994 which relate to the General Council’s education and training functions and to membership of the statutory committees–

items.title: The Road Traffic (Permitted Parking Area and Special Parking Area) (City of Manchester) Order 1999
items.description: This Order designates the City of Manchester, excluding trunk roads, the lengths of road described in Part I of Schedule 1 and the area around Manchester Airport described in Part II of that Schedule, as both a permitted parking area and a special parking area in accordance with Schedule 3 to the Road Traffic Act 1991 and applies with modifications various provisions of Part II of that Act to the designated area. In particular section 73 of the Road Traffic Act 1991 is modified so as to provide for the appointment of parking adjudicators by a joint committee of local authorities of which Manchester City Council is a constituent authority. The Order also modifies the Road Traffic Regulation Act 1984 in relation to the designated area.

items.title: The Carriage by Air Acts (Implementation of Protocol No. 4 of Montreal, 1975) Order 1999
items.description: This Order implements in the UK Protocol No. 4 of Montreal, 1975 which amends the Warsaw Convention (the Convention for the Unification of Certain Rules relating to International Carriage by Air signed at Warsaw on 12th October 1929), as amended by The Hague Protocol on 28th September 1955.

items.title: The Carriage by Air (Parties to Convention) Order 1999
items.description: The Carriage by Air Act 1961 gives effect in the United Kingdom to the Warsaw Convention as amended at The Hague, 1955, relating to international carriage by air, and the Carriage by Air Acts (Application of Provisions) Order 1967 makes transitional provision for the application of that Act to carriage still governed by the unamended Warsaw Convention, to which the United Kingdom remains a party, and to carriage governed by the Warsaw Convention as amended by Additional Protocol No. 1 of Montreal 1975.

items.title: The Broadcasting (Guernsey) Order 1999
items.description: This Order extends to the Bailiwick of Guernsey section 94 of the Broadcasting Act 1996, which relates to the renewal of licences to provide local radio services, and amends the Broadcasting Act 1990 (Guernsey) (No. 2) Order 1991 (S.I. 1991/1709), which extends the Broadcasting Act 1990, with modifications, to Guernsey.

items.title: The Broadcasting (Jersey) Order 1999
items.description: This Order extends to the Bailiwick of Jersey section 94 of the Broadcasting Act 1996, which relates to the renewal of licences to provide local radio services, and amends the Broadcasting Act 1990 (Jersey) (No. 2) Order 1991 (S.I. 1991/1710), which extends the Broadcasting Act 1990, with modifications, to Jersey.

items.title: The Geneva Conventions Act (Guernsey) Order 1999
The Women Priests (Channel Islands) Order 1999
items.description: This Order applies the Priests (Ordination of Women) Measure 1993 and the Ordination of Women (Financial Provisions) Measure 1993 to the Channel Islands in accordance with a Scheme prepared by the Bishop of Winchester.

items.title: The Reciprocal Enforcement of Maintenance Orders (Hague Convention Countries) (Variation) Order 1999
items.description: The Reciprocal Enforcement of Maintenance Orders (Hague Convention Countries) Order 1993 provides for the implementation in the United Kingdom of the Convention on the Recognition and Enforcement of Decisions Relating to Maintenance Obligations concluded in The Hague on 2nd October 1973. The Republic of Estonia and the Republic of Poland, in accordance with Article 31 of the Convention, have deposited their instruments of accession to the Convention.

items.title: The Scotland Act 1998 (Cross-Border Public Authorities)(Specification) Order 1999
The Road Traffic (Permitted Parking Area and Special Parking Area) (County of Hampshire) (City of Winchester) (Amendment) Order 1999
items.description: This Order amends the Road Traffic (Permitted Parking Area and Special Parking Area) (County of Hampshire) (City of Winchester) Order 1996 (“the principal Order”) which designated a permitted parking area and a special parking area in the City of Winchester.

items.title: The Judicial Committee (Powers in Devolution Cases) Order 1999
items.description: This Order confers powers on the Judicial Committee of the Privy Council for the purposes of proceedings under the Scotland Act 1998, the Government of Wales Act 1998 and the Northern Ireland Act 1998.

items.title: The Swansea (1999) National Health Service Trust (Change of Name) Order 1999
items.description: This Order changes the name of an NHS Trust established by Order under section 5 of the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990 from the Swansea (1999) National Health Service Trust to the Swansea National Health Service Trust. It also changes the trust’s Welsh name, makes consequential amendments to the trust’s establishment Order and makes transitional provisions.

items.title: The Public Service Vehicles (Community Licences) Regulations 1999 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations give effect in Great Britain to Article a of Council Regulation (EEC) No. 684/92 of 16 March 1992 (“the Council Regulation") on common rules for the international carriage of passengers by coach and bus, as amended by Council Regulation (EC) No. 11/98 of 11 December 1997. The Council Regulation establishes a Community-wide licence allowing carriers operating public service vehicles access to the market for the international carriage of passengers by road.

items.title: The Rules of the Air (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations amend the Rules of the Air Regulations 1996.1Following changes to article 61 of the Air Navigation (No. 2) Order 1995, references in Rule 5(2) are now made to a flying display, a flying display director and a pilot display authorisation (regulation 2(1) and (2)).2Permission required by an aircraft to taxi on the apron or the manoeuvring area of an aerodrome or by a vehicle or person to go or move on the manoeuvring area of an aerodrome may be obtained from the aerodrome flight information service unit (regulation 2(4) and (5)).

items.title: The Air Navigation (General) (Second Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Air Navigation (General) Regulations 1993 by prescribing requirements which must be met by an airborne collision avoidance system carried in accordance with the Air Navigation (No. 2) Order 1995.

items.title: The Cambridgeshire (Coroners' Districts) (Amendment) Order 1999
items.description: The County of Cambridgeshire was divided into five coroners' districts by the Cambridgeshire (Coroners' Districts) Order 1974. Two of these, Cambridge City and Southern were amalgamated to form the Cambridge City and Southern Coroners' District by the Cambridgeshire (Coroners' Districts) (Amendment) Order 1991. A third, Peterborough, ceased to be part of Cambridgeshire by the Cambridgeshire (City of Peterborough) (Structural, Boundary and Electoral Changes) Order 1996 and the Peterborough (Coroners) Order 1998, when the unitary authority of Peterborough was established.

items.title: The Social Security (Hospital In-Patients, Attendance Allowance and Disability Living Allowance) (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Social Security (Hospital In-Patients) Regulations 1975 (“the Hospital In-Patients Regulations”), the Social Security (Attendance Allowance) Regulations 1991 (“the Attendance Allowance Regulations”) and the Social Security (Disability Living Allowance) Regulations 1991 (“the Disability Living Allowance Regulations”).

items.title: The Harrow College (Incorporation) Order 1999
items.description: This Order establishes a further education corporation under section 16(1) of the Further and Higher Education Act 1992 for the purpose of establishing and conducting Harrow College, and appoints 1st August 1999 as the “operative date” for the purposes of Part I of that Act.

items.title: The Harrow College (Government) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations prescribe the form of instrument of government and articles of government for Harrow College.

items.title: The Education (Nursery Education and Early Years Development) (England) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations, which apply only in relation to nursery education in England, prescribe for the purposes of the new duty imposed by section 118 of the School Standards and Framework Act 1998 (“the Act”) (under which a local education authority must secure sufficient provision of nursery education for their area), the minimum age in relation to which that new duty applies (regulation 2).

items.title: The Scotland Act 1998 (General Transitory, Transitional and Savings Provisions) Amendment Order 1999
items.description: This Order amends the Scotland Act 1998 (General Transitory, Transitional and Savings Provisions) Order 1999 (S.I. 1999/901) (“the principal Order”).

items.title: The Cattle Identification (Amendment) Regulations 1999 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Cattle Identification Regulations 1998 (S.I. 1998/871) so as to provide that the competent authority for the purposes of Article 7.3 and (for the purposes of making the register available) Article 7.4 of Council Regulation (EC) 820/97 is the local authority or, in the case of England, the Ministry of Agriculture, Fisheries and Food; in the case of Scotland, the Secretary of State for Scotland; and in the case of Wales, the Secretary of State for Wales until 1st July 1999 and the National Assembly for Wales on and after that date.

items.title: The European Parliamentary Elections (Returning Officer’s Charges) (Northern Ireland) Order 1999
items.description: This Order, which extends only to Northern Ireland, makes provision for the payment of the Chief Electoral Officer as returning officer at European Parliamentary elections in respect of expenses incurred in connection therewith.

items.title: The Magistrates' Courts (Children and Young Persons) (Amendment) Rules 1999
items.description: These Rules amend the Magistrates' Courts (Children and Young Persons) Rules 1992 and certain forms in Schedule 2 to those Rules to make provision for the remand or committal of 15 and 16 year old boys into local authority accommodation with a requirement that they be placed and kept in secure accommodation. The amendments come into force on 1st June 1999.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Devolution Issues Rules) 1999
items.description: This Act of Sederunt amends the Rules of the Court of Session 1994 by adding a new Chapter 25A providing rules to regulate the procedure where a devolution issue (within the meaning of Schedule 6 to the Scotland Act 1998, Schedule 10 to the Northern Ireland Act 1998 and Schedule 8 to the Government of Wales Act 1998) arises in any proceedings.

items.title: Act of Adjournal (Devolution Issues Rules) 1999
items.description: This Act of Adjournal amends the Act of Adjournal (Criminal Procedure Rules) 1996 by adding a new Chapter 40 providing rules to regulate the procedure where a devolution issue (within the meaning of Schedule 6 to the Scotland Act 1998, Schedule 10 to the Northern Ireland Act 1998 and Schedule 8 to the Government of Wales Act 1998) arises in any proceedings.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Proceedings for Determination of Devolution Issues Rules) 1999
items.description: These Rules make provision for the procedures to be followed in civil proceedings in the Sheriff Court where a devolution issue within the meaning of the Scotland Act 1998, the Northern Ireland Act 1998 and the Government of Wales Act 1998, is raised by the parties to those proceedings.

items.title: The Scotland Act 1998 (Transitory and Transitional Provisions) (Members' Interests) Order 1999
items.description: This Order makes transitory and transitional provisions by virtue of section 129(1) of the Scotland Act 1998 (c. 46) (“the Act”) regarding the registration of interests of members of the Scottish Parliament and the Lord Advocate and Solicitor General for Scotland if they are not members of the Parliament.

items.title: The Scotland Act 1998 (Transitory and Transitional Provisions) (Complaints of Maladministration) Order 1999
items.description: This Order makes transitory and transitional provisions for the investigation by a Scottish Parliamentary Commissioner for Administration (“Scottish Commissioner”) of complaints of maladministration in connection with action taken in exercise of administrative functions by any member of the Scottish Executive and certain other persons, bodies and authorities.
