items.title: The Election Petition (Amendment) Rules 1999
items.description: These Rules make amendments to the Election Petition Rules 1960 consequent upon the implementation of the Civil Procedure Rules 1998.

items.title: The Port of London Authority Harbour Revision Order 1999
items.description: The Order extends the limits of the Port of London to include an area which had previously been excluded from those limits because prior to 1987 it had been outside the territorial waters of the United Kingdom. It also allows the Port of London Authority to charge river duties of tonnage on shipping passing through the limits of the Port of London on a voyage between the river Medway and a place outside those limits.

items.title: The A406 Trunk Road (Barnet) Red Route (Clearway) Traffic Order 1996 Variation Order 1999
items.description: This version of the The A406 Trunk Road (Barnet) Red Route (Clearway) Traffic Order 1996 Variation Order 1999 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Food Safety (General Food Hygiene) (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Food Safety (General Food Hygiene) Regulations 1995 (“the general Regulations”) to implement Commission Directive 98/28/EC (OJ No. L 140, 12.5.98, p.10) which grants a derogation from certain provisions of Council Directive 93/43/EEC on the hygiene of foodstuffs (OJ No. L 175, 19.7.93, p.1). They permit, subject to certain conditions, the bulk transport of raw sugar by sea in receptacles, containers or tankers that are not used exclusively for the transport of foodstuffs.

items.title: The Producer Responsibility Obligations (Packaging Waste) (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations amend the Producer Responsibility Obligations (Packaging Waste) Regulations 1997, which impose on producers obligations to recover and recycle packaging waste, and related obligations, in order to attain the targets in Article 6(1) of Directive 94/62/EC.

items.title: The Social Security (Overlapping Benefits) Amendment (No. 2) Regulations 1999
The Environmentally Sensitive Areas (Lake District) Designation (Amendment) Order 1999
items.description: This Order further amends the Environmentally Sensitive Areas (Lake District) Designation Order 1993 (S.I. 1993/85) (“the principal Order”), which designated an area in the Lake District as an environmentally sensitive area. The principal Order designated Council Regulation (EEC) No. 2078/92 (OJ No. L215, 30.7.92, p. 85) on agricultural production methods compatible with the requirements of protection of the environment and the maintenance of the countryside, as amended by Commission Regulation (EC) No. 2772/95 (OJ No. L288, 1.12.95, p. 35), as in turn rectified by Commission Regulation (EC) No. 1962/96 (OJ No. L259, 12.10.96, p. 7).

items.title: The Environmentally Sensitive Areas (Breckland) Designation (Amendment) Order 1999
items.description: This Order amends the Environmentally Sensitive Areas (Breckland) Designation Order 1993 (S.I. 1993/455) (“the principal Order”), which designated an area in Breckland as an environmentally sensitive area. The principal Order implemented Council Regulation (EEC) No. 2078/92 (OJ No. L215, 30.7.92, p. 85) on agricultural production methods compatible with the requirements of protection of the environment and the maintenance of the countryside, as amended by Commission Regulation (EC) No. 2772/95 (OJ No. L288, 1.12.95, p. 35), as in turn rectified by Commission Regulation (EC) No. 1962/96 (OJ No. L259, 12.10.96, p. 7).

items.title: The Environmentally Sensitive Areas (Test Valley) Designation (Amendment) Order 1999
items.description: This Order amends the Environmentally Sensitive Areas (Test Valley) Designation Order 1993 (S.I. 1993/459) (“the principal Order”), which designated an area in the Test Valley as an environmentally sensitive area. The principal Order designated Council Regulation (EEC) No. 2078/92 (OJ No. L215, 30.7.92, p. 85) on agricultural production methods compatible with the requirements of protection of the environment and the maintenance of the countryside, as amended by Commission Regulation (EC) No. 2772/95 (OJ No. L288, 1.12.95, p. 35), as in turn rectified by Commission Regulation (EC) No. 1962/96 (OJ No. L259, 12.10.96, p. 7).

items.title: The Environmentally Sensitive Areas (North Kent Marshes) Designation (Amendment) Order 1999
items.description: This Order amends the Environmentally Sensitive Areas (North Kent Marshes) Designation Order 1993 (S.I. 1993/82) (“the principal Order”), which designated an area in the North Kent Marshes as an environmentally sensitive area. The principal Order implemented Council Regulation (EEC) No. 2078/92 (OJ No. L215, 30.7.92, p. 85) on agricultural production methods compatible with the requirements of protection of the environment and the maintenance of the countryside, as amended by Commission Regulation (EC) No. 2772/95 (OJ No. L288, 1.12.95, p. 35), as in turn rectified by Commission Regulation (EC) No. 1962/96 (OJ No. L259, 12.10.96, p. 7).

items.title: The Environmentally Sensitive Areas (North Peak) Designation (Amendment) Order 1999
items.description: This Order amends the Environmentally Sensitive Areas (North Peak) Designation Order 1993 (S.I. 1993/457) (“the principal Order”), which designated an area in the North Peak as an environmentally sensitive area. The principal Order implemented Council Regulation (EEC) No. 2078/92 (OJ No. L215, 30.7.92, p. 85) on agricultural production methods compatible with the requirements of protection of the environment and the maintenance of the countryside, as amended by Commission Regulation (EC) No. 2772/95 (OJ No. L288, 1.12.95, p. 35), as in turn rectified by Commission Regulation (EC) No. 1962/96 (OJ No. L259, 12.10.96, p. 7).

items.title: The Environmentally Sensitive Areas (Avon Valley) Designation (Amendment) Order 1999
items.description: This Order further amends the Environmentally Sensitive Areas (Avon Valley) Designation Order 1993 (S.I. 1993/84) (“the principal Order”), which designated an area in the Avon Valley as an environmentally sensitive area. The principal Order implemented Council Regulation (EEC) No. 2078/92 (OJ No. L215, 30.7.92, p.85) on agricultural production methods compatible with the requirements of protection of the environment and the maintenance of the countryside, as amended by Commission Regulation (EC) No. 2772/95 (OJ No. L288, 1.12.95, p.35) as in turn rectified by Commission Regulation (EC) No. 1962/96 (OJ No. L259, 12.10.96, p.7).

items.title: The Environmentally Sensitive Areas (Clun) Designation (Amendment) Order 1999
items.description: This Order further amends the Environmentally Sensitive Areas (Clun) Designation Order 1993 (S.I. 1993/456) (“the principal Order”) which designated an area in the vicinity of Clun as an environmentally sensitive area. The principal Order implemented Council Regulation (EEC) No. 2078/92 (OJ No. L215, 30.7.92, p.85) on agricultural production methods compatible with the requirements of protection of the environment and the maintenance of the countryside, as amended by Council Regulation (EC) No. 2772/95 (OJ No. L288, 1.12.95, p.35) as in turn rectified by Council Regulation (EC) No. 1962/96 (OJ No. L259, 12.10.96, p.7).

items.title: The National Lottery (Imposition of Penalties and Revocation of Licences) Procedure Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations make provision for the procedure to be followed where a body corporate holding a licence granted under section 5 or 6 of the National Lottery etc. Act 1993 (the 1993 Act) wishes to make oral representations about a proposal by the Director General of the National Lottery (the Director General) either to impose a penalty in respect of the contravention of a condition of such a licence or to revoke such a licence.

items.title: The Environmentally Sensitive Areas (South Wessex Downs) Designation (Amendment) Order 1999
items.description: This Order amends the Environmentally Sensitive Areas (South Wessex Downs) Designation Order 1993 (S.I. 1993/86) (“the principal Order”), which designated an area in South Wessex Downs as an environmentally sensitive area. The principal Order implemented Council Regulation (EEC) No. 2078/92 (OJ No. L215, 30.7.92, p. 85) on agricultural production methods compatible with the requirements of protection of the environment and the maintenance of the countryside, as amended by Commission Regulation (EC) No. 2772/95 (OJ No. L288, 1.12.95, p. 35), as in turn rectified by Commission Regulation (EC) No. 1962/96 (OJ No. L259, 12.10.96, p. 7).

items.title: The Environmentally Sensitive Areas (Exmoor) Designation (Amendment) Order 1999
items.description: This Order further amends the Environmentally Sensitive Areas (Exmoor) Designation Order 1993 (S.I. 1993/83) (“the principal Order”), which designated an area in Exmoor as an environmentally sensitive area. The principal Order implemented Council Regulation (EEC) No. 2078/92 (OJ No. L215, 30.7.92, p. 85) on agricultural production methods compatible with the requirements of protection of the environment and the maintenance of the countryside, as amended by Commission Regulation (EC) No. 2772/95 (OJ No. L288, 1.12.95, p. 35), as in turn rectified by Commission Regulation (EC) No. 1962/96 (OJ No. L259, 12.10.96, p. 7).

items.title: The Environmentally Sensitive Areas (Suffolk River Valleys) Designation (Amendment) Order 1999
items.description: This Order further amends the Environmentally Sensitive Areas (Suffolk River Valleys) Designation Order 1993 (S.I. 1993/458) (“the principal Order ”), which designated an area in the Suffolk River Valleys as an environmentally sensitive area. The principal Order implemented Council Regulation (EEC) No. 2078/92 (OJ No. L215, 30.7.92, p.85) on agricultural production methods compatible with the requirements of protection of the environment and the maintenance of the countryside, as amended by Commission Regulation (EC) No. 2772/95 (OJ No. L288, 1.12.95, p.35) as in turn rectified by Commission Regulation (EC) No. 1962/96 (OJ No. L259, 12.10.96, p.7).

items.title: The European Specialist Medical Qualifications Amendment Regulations 1999 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the European Specialist Medical Qualifications Order 1995 (“the 1995 Order"), which implemented European obligations contained principally in Council Directive 93/16/EEC (OJ No. L165, 7.7.93, p. 1) relating to the training of specialist doctors and mutual recognition of their qualifications (“the Directive"). The need for changes to the 1995 Order arises from amendments to the Directive made by Commission Directives 98/21/EC (OJ No. L119, 22.4.98, p. 15) and 98/63/EC (OJ No. L253, 15.9.98, p. 24).

items.title: The Value Added Tax (Amendment) (No.3) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 9th June 1999, amend the rules governing the time when a VAT charge arises on supplies of construction services. The amendments alleviate the impact of the current provisions on situations which do not involve tax avoidance. The amendments also continue to prevent avoidance of VAT involving the deferral of a VAT charge under contrived contracts where payment for construction services is not due for many years.

items.title: The Legal Aid in Criminal and Care Proceedings (Costs) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1999
items.description: This version of the The Legal Aid in Criminal and Care Proceedings (Costs) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1999 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Council of the City of York (Millennium Bridge) Scheme 1998 Confirmation Instrument 1999
The European Parliamentary Elections (Local Returning Officers' Charges) Order 1999
items.description: This Order, which applies in Great Britain, provides for the expenses of local returning officers at European Parliamentary elections. Local returning officers are defined by regulation 2 of the European Parliamentary Elections Regulations 1999 and discharge functions in each parliamentary constituency within a European Parliamentary electoral region in accordance with regulation 4 of those Regulations.

items.title: The European Parliamentary Elections (Returning Officers' Charges) Order 1999
items.description: This Order, together with the European Parliamentary Elections (Local Returning Officer’s Charges) Order 1999 provide for payments for services and expenses of returning officers in connection with the conduct of European Parliamentary elections in Great Britain.

items.title: The Scotland Act 1998 (Transitory and Transitional Provisions) (Publication and Interpretation etc. of Acts of the Scottish Parliament) Order 1999
items.description: This Order makes transitory and transitional provisions in relation to the numbering, citation and interpretation of Acts of the Scottish Parliament and subordinate legislation made under such Acts (“Scottish subordinate legislation”) and other documents.

items.title: The Education Development Plans (England) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations, which are made under sections 6 and 7 of the School Standards and Framework Act 1998 (“the 1998 Act”), apply in relation to local education authorities in England.

items.title: The North and West Salmon Fishery District Designation Order 1999
The Conon Salmon Fishery District Designation Order 1999
The Lochaber Salmon Fishery District Designation Order 1999
The Pathfinder National Health Service Trust (Change of Name) Order 1999
items.description: This Order changes the name of an NHS trust established by Order under Part I of the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990 from the Pathfinder National Health Service Trust to the South West London and St George’s Mental Health National Health Service Trust, and makes consequential amendments to that Order and transitional provisions.

items.title: The Education (Al-Furqan Primary School, Tyseley) (Exemption from Pay and Conditions) Order 1999
items.description: This Order, made under section 3(4) of the School Teachers' Pay and Conditions Act 1991 (“the Act”), comes into force on 1st June 1999. With effect from that date, section 2(6) and (7) of the Act, which provides that the statutory conditions of employment of school teachers (their remuneration and such of their other conditions of employment as relate to their professional duties and working time) are governed by pay and conditions orders made under section 2(1) of that Act, shall no longer apply to the statutory conditions of employment of school teachers in Al-Furqan Primary School, Tyseley, Birmingham.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Rules of the Court of Session Amendment No. 5) (Miscellaneous) 1999
Act of Adjournal (Criminal Procedure Rules Amendment No. 3) 1999
The Walsgrave Hospitals National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Amendment Order 1999
items.description: This Order amends the constitution of the Walsgrave Hospitals National Health Service Trust, an NHS trust established by Order under Part I of the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990, by decreasing the number of executive directors from 6 to 5. The amendment is required because the Walsgrave Hospitals National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Amendment Order 1998 (S.I. 1998/3082), in error, increased from 5 to 6 the number of the Trust’s executive directors. Under the National Health Service Trusts (Membership and Procedure) Amendment Regulations 1998 (S.I. 1998/1975), the maximum permitted number of executive directors of an NHS trust is 5.

items.title: The European Parliamentary Election Petition (Amendment) Rules 1999 (revoked)
items.description: These Rules make amendments to the European Parliamentary Election Petition Rules 1979 consequent upon the implementation of the Civil Procedure Rules 1998.

items.title: The Education (Infant Class Sizes) (Grant) Regulations 1999
items.description: Regulations under section 1 of the School Standards and Framework Act 1998 provide that an infant class may not contain more than 30 pupils at any time while an ordinary teaching session is conducted by a single qualified teacher (or, where the session is conducted by more than one qualified teacher, more than 30 pupils for every teacher). These Regulations, which are made under section 3 of that Act, authorise the Secretary of State to pay grants to a local education authority in connection with expenditure incurred by them (and approved by the Secretary of State) for the purpose of ensuring that that limit on the size of infant classes is complied with at schools maintained by that authority (regulation 3).

items.title: The European Parliamentary Elections (Welsh Forms) (Amendment) Order 1999
items.description: Articles 4 to 7 of this Order amend the European Parliamentary Elections (Welsh Forms) Order 1989 (“the 1989 Order”) to take account of the replacement of the European Parliamentary Elections Regulations 1986 (S.I. 1986/2209) by the European Parliamentary Elections Regulations 1999 and the changes made by the latter Regulations. Those Regulations give effect to the regional list system of election for European Parliamentary elections introduced by the European Parliamentary Elections Act 1999 (c. 1).

items.title: The Misuse of Drugs (Safe Custody) (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations amend the Misuse of Drugs (Safe Custody) Regulations 1973 in respect of certain products which are used for scientific and diagnostic purposes and which contain an extremely small amount and proportion of controlled drugs. The products in question are exempted from regulations 14 and 18 to 24 of the 1985 Regulations (which relate to documentation, labelling and record-keeping) and the security requirements of the 1973 Regulations.

items.title: The Misuse of Drugs (Amendment) Regulations 1999 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Misuse of Drugs Regulations 1985 (“the principal Regulations”) by exempting certain products, described in regulation 2(1) of the principal regulations, from sections 3(1), 4(1) and 5(1) of the Misuse of Drugs Act 1971 (which relate to the importation, exportation, production, supply and possession of controlled drugs). These Regulations also exempt such products from regulations 14 and 18 to 24 of the principal Regulations (which relate to documentation, labelling and record-keeping). These Regulations exempt products used for scientific or diagnostic purposes which contain an extremely small amount and proportion of controlled drugs.

items.title: The Local Government Act 1988 (Defined Activities) (Exemption) (Tower Hamlets London Borough Council) Order 1999
items.description: Under Part I (competition) of the Local Government Act 1988 work falling within certain defined activities may be carried out by local authorities only if particular conditions are fulfilled.

items.title: The Education (Governors' Annual Reports) (Wales) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations, which apply only in relation to schools in Wales, set out–

items.title: The Education (Annual Parents' Meetings) (Wales) Regulations 1999
items.description: These regulations, which are made under subsection (4) of section 43 of the School Standards and Framework Act 1998 (“the 1998 Act”) and apply only to schools in Wales, make provision with respect to proceedings of annual parents' meetings which governing bodies of community, foundation, voluntary, community special and foundation special schools are required to hold under that section. The regulations, which replicate provisions contained in Schedule 18 to the Education Act 1996 (which is repealed prospectively by the 1998 Act), shall come into force on 1st September 1999 when the existing categories of maintained schools move to their new categories under the 1998 Act.

items.title: The Non-resident Companies (General Insurance Business) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations modify the effect of Chapter IV of Part XVII of the Income and Corporation Taxes Act 1988 (controlled foreign companies) (“Chapter IV”). The modifications relate to companies resident outside the United Kingdom which carry on general insurance business and draw up accounts relating to that business on a non-annual basis (“relevant foreign companies”), being companies in which companies resident in the United Kingdom (“United Kingdom companies”) have a relevant interest within the meaning of section 752A of the 1988 Act (inserted by paragraph 7 of Schedule 17 to the Finance Act 1998 (c. 36)).

items.title: The Local Authority (Stocks and Bonds) (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations amend the Local Authority (Stocks and Bonds) Regulations 1974 so as to enable title to certain stock to continue to be evidenced and transferred without a written instrument after the responsibility for operating the CGO Service is transferred from the Bank of England to CRESTCo Limited (which will occur on a contractual basis on the day these Regulations come into force).

items.title: The Government Stock (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Government Stock Regulations 1965 so as to ensure that provisions relating to the CGO service continue to apply after responsibility for operating that Service is transferred from the Bank of England to CRESTCo Limited (which will occur on a contractual basis on the day on which these Regulations come into force).

items.title: The Income Support (General) (Standard Interest Rate Amendment) (No. 5) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations amend Schedule 3 to the Income Support (General) Regulations 1987 (S.I. 1987/1967), as substituted and amended, with respect to the standard rate of interest applicable to a loan which qualifies for income support under that Schedule to those Regulations. The new rate is 6.66 per cent. (regulation 2). The Regulations also revoke, with a saving provision, regulations which made a previous amendment to that standard rate of interest (regulation 3).

items.title: The Civil Aviation (Navigation Services Charges) (Third Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations amend the Civil Aviation (Navigation Services Charges) Regulations 1998 (“the principal Regulations”) as follows:1The charge payable to the CAA by the operator of a helicopter which flies from any place in the United Kingdom to a vessel or an off-shore installation within the area of the Northern North Sea described in regulation 7(2) of the principal Regulations is increased from £123 to £147 (regulation 2(1)).2The charge payable to the CAA by an operator of a helicopter which flies from any place in the United Kingdom to a vessel or an off-shore installation within the area of the Southern North Sea described in regulation 7(4) of the principal Regulations is increased from £39 to £55 (regulation 2(2)).

items.title: The Potato Industry Development Council (Amendment) Order 1999
items.description: This Order, which comes into force on 1st July 1999, amends the Potato Industry Development Council Order 1997 (S.I. 1997/266) (“the principal Order”). The principal Order established a development council (“the Council”) for the potato industry in Great Britain. The Council’s functions include the keeping of a register of producers and first purchasers, the receipt of annual returns from producers and first purchasers, and the levying of certain charges upon producers and first purchasers.

items.title: The Radcliffe Infirmary National Health Service Trust (Dissolution) Order 1999
items.description: This Order dissolves the Radcliffe Infirmary National Health Service Trust on 1st June 1999.

items.title: The Police (Secretary of State’s Objectives) (No. 2) Order 1999
items.description: This Order sets out the Secretary of State’s objectives for the policing of the areas of all police authorities established under section 3 of the Police Act 1996. The objectives add an additional objective (d) to those set out in the Police (Secretary of State’s Objectives) Order 1999 which is revoked by this Order. Section 6(2)(a) of the 1996 Act requires police authorities to have regard to these objectives in the discharge of their functions. Local policing objectives determined under section 7 of the 1996 Act must be consistent with these objectives. Section 8 of the 1996 Act requires police authorities to issue local policing plans which must give particulars of these objectives.

items.title: The M32 Motorway (Hambrook Interchange to Lower Ashley Road Interchange) and Connecting Roads Scheme 1989 (Variation) Scheme 1999
items.description: This version of the The M32 Motorway (Hambrook Interchange to Lower Ashley Road Interchange) and Connecting Roads Scheme 1989 (Variation) Scheme 1999 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A205 Trunk Road (Southwark) Red Route (Prohibited Turns) Traffic Order 1999
items.description: This version of the The A205 Trunk Road (Southwark) Red Route (Prohibited Turns) Traffic Order 1999 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A1 Trunk Road (Haringey) Red Route Traffic Order 1993 Variation Order 1999
items.description: This version of the The A1 Trunk Road (Haringey) Red Route Traffic Order 1993 Variation Order 1999 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Unfair Dismissal and Statement of Reasons for Dismissal (Variation of Qualifying Period) Order 1999
items.description: This Order, which comes into force on 1st June 1999, lowers the qualifying period of continuous employment needed to acquire the right not to be unfairly dismissed from two years to one year. It also lowers the qualifying period of continuous employment needed for the entitlement, on request, to a written statement of reasons for dismissal from two years to one year.

items.title: The Northern Ireland (Location of Victims' Remains) Act 1999 (Immunities and Privileges) Order 1999
items.description: This Order confers privileges and immunities on the Commission for the Location of Victims' Remains and on its members, staff and agents. The Commission was established under the Agreement between the Government of the United Kingdom and the Government of Ireland signed on 27th April 1999.

items.title: The Protection of Wrecks (Designation) Order 1999
items.description: This Order designates as a restricted area for the purposes of the Protection of Wrecks Act 1973 the area within a distance of 250 metres from the site of the wreck of a seventeenth century vessel, which vessel the Secretary of State is satisfied ought to be protected from unauthorised interference on account of its historical and archaeological importance.

items.title: The Education Development Plans (Wales) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations, which are made under sections 6 and 7 of the School Standards and Framework Act 1998 (“the 1998 Act”), apply in relation to local education authorities in Wales.

items.title: The Restriction of Liberty Order (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations made under sections 245A(8) and 245C(3) of the Criminal Procedure (Scotland) Act 1995 as inserted by section 5 of the Crime and Punishment (Scotland) Act 1997 amend the Restriction of Liberty Order (Scotland) Regulations 1998 which regulate aspects of the monitoring by electronic and radio devices the compliance of offenders with requirements of restriction of liberty orders.

items.title: The Education (School Inspection) (Wales) (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: The School Standards and Framework Act 1998 (“the 1998 Act”) provides for new categories of maintained schools which will replace, from 1st September 1999, the existing categories of such schools. The 1998 Act amends the Education Act 1996 and the School Inspections Act 1996 by substituting references to those new categories and a new definition of the term “maintained school”. These regulations amend the Education (School Inspection) (Wales) Regulations 1998 to reflect those and other changes made by the 1998 Act.

items.title: The Education (Inspection of Nursery Education) (Wales) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations, which apply only in relation to nursery education provision in Wales, prescribe the period within which a report of an inspection of nursery education under Schedule 26 to the School Standards and Framework Act 1998 must be made; the authorities and persons to whom a copy of such a report must be sent; and the intervals at which nursery education is to be inspected under that Schedule (regulations 3 and 4).

items.title: The Education (Special Educational Needs) (Information) (Wales) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 1st September 1999 and apply only to Wales, make provision for the publication of information about matters relating to the provision of education for pupils with special educational needs in their schools by the governing bodies of maintained schools, and for the inclusion of information in annual reports to parents about their policies for such pupils.

items.title: The Railways Act 1993 (Consequential Modifications) Order 1999
items.description: This Order modifies the Environmental Protection Act 1990 (“the 1990 Act”) and the Litter (Statutory Undertakers) (Designation and Relevant Land) Order 1991 in consequence of the provisions of the Railways Act 1993.

items.title: The Rail Vehicle Accessibility (LTS Rail Class 357 Vehicles) Exemption Order 1999
items.description: This Order authorises the use of specified rail vehicles forming Class 357 multiple-units although they do not conform with provisions of the Rail Vehicle Accessibility Regulations 1998. It sets time limits on the authorisations and imposes conditions.

items.title: The A30 Trunk Road (Woodleigh Junction Slip Roads) Order 1999
The Aeroplane Noise Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations revoke and replace the Aeroplane Noise (Limitation on Operation of Aeroplanes) Regulations 1993 S.I. 1993/1409, as amended by the Aeroplane Noise (Limitation on Operation of Aeroplanes) (Amendment) Regulations 1994, S.I. 1994/1734 (regulation 2). They also amend the Air Navigation (Noise Certification) Order 1990 (which, inter alia, implemented provisions contained in Council Directive 89/629/EEC of 4 December 1989 on the limitation of noise emissions from civil subsonic jet aeroplanes). The Regulations make provision to implement all of the Community obligations of the United Kingdom contained in three directives relating to noise emissions from aeroplanes, two of which are the subject of amending directives.

items.title: The Judicial Pensions and Retirement Act 1993 (Addition of Qualifying Judicial Offices) Order 1999
items.description: This Order adds two offices to the list of offices–

items.title: The Social Security and Child Support (Decisions and Appeals) (Amendment) Regulations 1999
The Stamp Duty and Stamp Duty Reserve Tax (Open-ended Investment Companies) (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations amend the Stamp Duty and Stamp Duty Reserve Tax (Open-ended Investment Companies) Regulations 1997 (S.I. 1997/1156) so as to provide that the exemption from stamp duty and stamp duty reserve tax for mergers of authorised unit trusts with open-ended investment companies shall extend beyond 1st July 1999.

items.title: The Horserace Betting Levy (Bookmakers' Committee) Regulations 1999
The Education (Schedule 32 to the School Standards and Framework Act 1998) (Wales) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations, which apply only in relation to former grant-maintained schools in Wales, provide for the payment of grants by the Secretary of State to the governing bodies of former grant-maintained schools in cases where a decision has been made, or obligation incurred, to pay the grant before 1st September 1999 (regulation 3).

items.title: The Education (School Performance Information) (Wales) (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: This version of the The Education (School Performance Information) (Wales) (Amendment) Regulations 1999 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A1 Trunk Road (Islington) Red Route Traffic Order 1993 Variation Order (No. 2) 1999
items.description: This version of the The A1 Trunk Road (Islington) Red Route Traffic Order 1993 Variation Order (No. 2) 1999 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A1 Trunk Road (Islington) Red Route (Bus Priority) Traffic Order 1999
items.description: This version of the The A1 Trunk Road (Islington) Red Route (Bus Priority) Traffic Order 1999 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Adoption (Amendment) Rules 1999
items.description: The Adoption Rules 1984 apply the Rules of the Supreme Court 1965 and the County Court Rules 1981. On 26th April 1999, the Civil Procedure Rules 1998 came into force in respect of civil proceedings; they supersede the Rules of the Supreme Court and the County Court Rules, which will cease to have effect. These Rules provide for the Rules of the Supreme Court and County Court Rules to continue to apply to the Adoption Rules. The exception is the assessment of costs, where the relevant provisions of the Civil Procedure Rules will apply instead.

items.title: The Food Labelling (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1999 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations, which apply to Great Britain, amend the Food Labelling Regulations 1996, as amended (“the principal Regulations"). The Regulations implement Commission Directive 1999/10/EC providing for derogations from the provisions of Article 7 of Council Directive 79/112/EEC as regards the labelling of foodstuffs.

items.title: The A1 Trunk Road (Islington) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 1999
items.description: This version of the The A1 Trunk Road (Islington) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 1999 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A50 Trunk Road (Uttoxeter Service Area, Staffordshire) Order 1999
The M32 Motorway (Junction Two, Northbound Exit Slip Road, Special Road) (Detrunking) Order 1999
The Education (Mandatory Awards) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 1st September 1999, supersede the Education (Mandatory Awards) Regulations 1998 as amended.

items.title: The Social Security Commissioners (Procedure) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations regulate the procedure of the Social Security Commissioners in determining claims and questions arising from decisions of the unified appeals tribunals established under section 7 of the Social Security Act 1998. These Regulations also prescribe the procedure to be followed in the case of a reference made under the Forfeiture Act 1982. These Regulations revoke the Social Security Commissioners (Procedure) Regulations 1987 and amending instruments and are necessary as a result of the introduction of the unified appeals tribunals established under the 1998 Act.

items.title: The Chiropractors Act 1994 (Commencement No. 3) Order 1999
items.description: This Order brings into force on 15th June 1999 the following provisions of the Chiropractors Act 1994 which relate to the opening of the General Council’s register and registration–

items.title: The Education (Individual Pupils' Achievements) (Information) (Wales) (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 1st September 1999 amend the Education (Individual Pupils' Achievements) (Information) (Wales) Regulations 1997 by substituting a new definition of “maintained school” in those Regulations reflecting the new categories of maintained schools provided for by the School Standards and Framework Act 1998, the relevant provisions of which will come into force on 1st September 1999.

items.title: The School Standards and Framework Act 1998 (Appointed Day) (Wales) Order 1999
items.description: This Order appoints 1st September 1999 as the appointed day for certain provisions of Part I of Schedule 32 to the School Standards and Framework Act 1998. It applies only to Wales.

items.title: The Criminal Justice Act 1993 (Commencement No. 11) Order 1999
items.description: This Order rectifies an omission in the Criminal Justice Act 1993 (Commencement No. 10) Order 1999 (S.I. 1999/1189), by bringing into force the remaining provisions of the Criminal Justice Act 1993 extending the jurisdiction of courts in England and Wales in relation to certain offences of dishonesty and blackmail on 1st June 1999. By virtue of section 78(5) of the Act, nothing in any of those provisions applies to any act, omission or other event occurring before that date.

items.title: The A205 Trunk Road (Southwark) Red Route (Bus Lanes) Traffic Order 1999
items.description: This version of the The A205 Trunk Road (Southwark) Red Route (Bus Lanes) Traffic Order 1999 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Foundation Body Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations make provision for and in connection with the establishment, membership and functions of foundation bodies and the steps to be taken in connection with schools joining a group of schools for which a foundation body acts.

items.title: The Education (Grants) (Music, Ballet and Choir Schools) (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Education (Grants) (Music, Ballet and Choir Schools) Regulations 1995. They come into force on 1st September 1999 and have effect in relation to a school year beginning on or after that date.

items.title: The Education (Assisted Places) (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Education (Assisted Places) Regulations 1997 in respect of a school year beginning on or after 1st September 1999.

items.title: The Education (Assisted Places) (Incidental Expenses) (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Education (Assisted Places) (Incidental Expenses) Regulations 1997 (“the 1997 Regulations”) in respect of a school year beginning on or after 1st September 1999. The 1997 Regulations provide for the payment of grants as regards incidental expenses, and for the remission of incidental expenses, in respect of pupils eligible to continue to hold assisted places by virtue of section 2 of the Education (Schools) Act 1997, notwithstanding the abolition of the assisted places scheme by section 1 of that Act.

items.title: The Water Supply (Water Fittings) (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations make minor amendments to the Water Supply (Water Fittings) Regulations 1999 to correct defects.

items.title: The Combined Probation Areas (Humberside) Order 1999
items.description: This Order amends the Combined Probation Areas Order 1986 (S.I. 1986/1713) to take account of the creation of the new petty sessional divisions of North Lincolnshire, Goole and Howdenshire and South Hunsley Beacon. These petty sessional divisions are created by the Petty Sessional Divisions (Humberside) Order 1999 (S.I. 1999/493) which comes into force, subject to certain exceptions, on 1st June 1999.

items.title: The Social Security Amendment (Non-Cash Vouchers) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations amend the Council Tax Benefit (General) Regulations 1992 (S.I. 1992/1814), the Disability Working Allowance (General) Regulations 1991 (S.I. 1991/2887), the Family Credit (General) Regulations 1987 (S.I. 1987/1973), the Housing Benefit (General) Regulations 1987 (S.I. 1987/1971), the Income Support (General) Regulations 1987 (S.I. 1987/1967) (collectively referred to below as “the income-related benefits”) and the Jobseeker’s Allowance Regulations 1996 (S.I. 1996/207).

items.title: The Police Act 1997 (Commencement No. 6) Order 1999
items.description: This Order brings into force those provisions not already in force of Part III of the Police Act 1997, which makes provision for the authorisation, in specified circumstances, of the entry on or interference with property or wireless telegraphy on the application of a member of a police force, or of the National Criminal Intelligence Service or the National Crime Squad, or of a customs officer. Part III, and most of the provisions of Part I, II, IV and VI, have now been brought into force: see the Police Act 1997 (Commencement No. 5 and Transitional Provisions) Order 1998 (S.I. 1998/354).

items.title: The Social Security Act 1998 (Commencement No. 7 and Consequential and Transitional Provisions) Order 1999
items.description: This Order provides for the coming into force on 1st June 1999 of the provisions of the Social Security Act 1998 which—

items.title: The Northern Ireland (Location of Victims' Remains) Act 1999 (Commencement of Section 2) Order 1999
items.description: This Order provides for the coming into force of section 2 of the Northern Ireland (Location of Victims' Remains) Act 1999 (c. 7). Section 2 makes provision in relation to the Independent Commission for the Location of Victims' Remains, which was established by an agreement made on 27th April 1999 between the United Kingdom and Irish Governments. The other provisions of the Act came into force on Royal Assent (26th May 1999).

items.title: The Scotland Act 1998 (Agency Arrangements) (Specification) Order 1999
items.description: This Order specifies certain functions of a Minister of the Crown and of the Scottish Ministers for the purposes of section 93(1) of the Scotland Act 1998. Section 93(1) allows a Minister of the Crown to make arrangements for any of his specified functions to be exercised on his behalf by the Scottish Ministers and allows the Scottish Ministers to make arrangements for any of their specified functions to be exercised on their behalf by a Minister of the Crown.

items.title: The Olive Oil (Designations of Origin) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations, which apply to Great Britain, provide for the enforcement and execution of Commission Regulation (EC) No. 2815/98 (OJ No. L349, 24.12.98, p.56) as amended by Commission Regulation (EC) No. 640/1999 (OJ No. L82, 26.3.1999, p.8) (together hereinafter referred to as “Regulation 2815/98”).

items.title: The Smoke Control Areas (Exempted Fireplaces) Order 1999
items.description: Section 20 of the Clean Air Act 1993 generally prohibits the emission of smoke in smoke control areas. The Secretary of State may by order made under section 21 of the Act exempt specified classes of fireplace from the provisions of section 20 if he is satisfied that they can be used for burning fuel other than authorised fuels without producing any smoke or a substantial quantity of smoke.

items.title: The Federal Republic of Yugoslavia (Supply, Sale and Export of Petroleum and Petroleum Products) (No. 2) Regulations 1999
items.description: Council Regulation (EC) No. 900/99 of 29th April 1999 prohibits the supply, sale and export to the Federal Republic of Yugoslavia of specified petroleum and petroleum products. These Regulations–

items.title: The Energy Information (Lamps) Regulations 1999 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations implement Council Directive 92/75/EEC (on the indication by labelling and standard product information of the consumption of energy and other resources by household appliances) and Commission Directive 98/11/EC (implementing Council Directive 92/75/EEC with regard to energy labelling of household lamps).

items.title: The Magistrates' Courts Committees (West Yorkshire) Amalgamation (Amendment) Order 1999
items.description: This Order amends the Magistrates' Courts Committees (West Yorkshire) Amalgamation Order 1998, which provides for the replacement of the magistrates' courts committees for the West Riding, Calderdale and Leeds City by a single committee to be known as the West Yorkshire magistrates' courts committee.

items.title: The Road Vehicles (Construction and Use) (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations amend the definition of “the emissions publication” in Schedule 7B to the Road Vehicles (Construction and Use) Regulations 1986 so as to refer to the most recent (i.e. the fifth) edition of this publication. The publication specifies, with respect to vehicles with spark ignition engines, the maximum permitted carbon monoxide content of exhaust emissions at idling speed and the limits for the ratio of air to petrol vapour entering the combustion chamber, in relation to each of the vehicle models named in the publication.

items.title: The Council Tax (Exempt Dwellings) (Amendment) (No. 2) Order 1999
items.description: Section 4 of the Local Government Finance Act 1992 provides that the Secretary of State may prescribe by order classes of dwellings for which no council tax is payable. The Council Tax (Exempt Dwellings) Order 1992 prescribes such classes of dwellings.

items.title: The Housing Renewal Grants (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations amend Part II of the Housing Renewal Grants Regulations 1996 (“the principal Regulations”), which sets out a means test for determining the amount of renovation grant and disabled facilities grant which may be paid by local authorities to owner-occupier and tenant applicants under Chapter I of Part I of the Housing Grants, Construction and Regeneration Act 1996.

items.title: The Water Supply (Water Quality) (Amendment) Regulations 1999 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Water Supply (Water Quality) Regulations 1989, which, in part, implement Council Directive 80/778/EEC (OJ No. L229, 30.8.80, p. 11) on the quality of water intended for human consumption. The amendments make specific provision intended to ensure that water supplied for human consumption is not contaminated with cryptosporidium, a parasite which can be detrimental to human health.

items.title: The Feeding Stuffs (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations amend the Feeding Stuffs Regulations 1995 as amended (“the principal Regulations") and implement–

items.title: The General Chiropractic Council (Constitution and Procedure) Rules Order 1999
items.description: This Order, which is made under the Chiropractors Act 1994, approves Rules made by the General Chiropractic Council (“the Council”) setting out the grounds of and procedure for removal of members of the Council and the arrangements for the appointment and co-option of members to the statutory committees of the Council. The Rules also prescribe the circumstances in which an unexpired term of office (where a member fails to complete his full term) need not be filled before the end of that term, and provide for the appointment of an acting Chairman of the Council in circumstances where the Chairman is unable to act because the post is vacant or for another reason specified in Rule 6.

items.title: The Local Government Act 1988 (Defined Activities) (Exemptions) (Bournemouth, Harrogate, Poole and Worthing Borough Councils) Order 1999
items.description: Under Part I (competition) of the Local Government Act 1988 work falling within certain defined activities may be carried out by local authorities only if particular conditions are fulfilled (“Part I requirements”).

items.title: The Housing Benefit and Council Tax Benefit (General) Amendment (No.2) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Housing Benefit (General) Regulations 1987 (S.I. 1987/1971) (the “Housing Benefit Regulations”) and the Council Tax Benefit (General) Regulations 1992 (S.I. 1992/1814) (the “Council Tax Benefit Regulations”).

items.title: The Natural Mineral Water, Spring Water and Bottled Drinking Water Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 30th June 1999, implement Council Directive 80/777/EEC on the approximation of the laws of the Member States relating to the exploitation and marketing of natural mineral waters (OJ No. L229, 30.8.80, p. 1) as amended by Directive 96/70/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council (OJ No. L229, 23.11.96, p. 26).

items.title: The Relocation Grants (Form of Application) (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations amend the Form set out in the Schedule to the Relocation Grants (Form of Application) Regulations 1997 to be used for an application for relocation grant payable under sections 131–140 of the Housing Grants, Construction and Regeneration Act 1996.

items.title: The Food (Animals and Animal Products from Belgium) (Emergency Control) Order 1999
items.description: This Order is made under section 13 of the Food Safety Act 1990 in consequence of it appearing to the Minister of Agriculture, Fisheries and Food that the carrying out of commercial operations with respect to animals and animal products originating from Belgium may involve imminent risk of injury to health.

items.title: The Animal Feedingstuffs from Belgium (Control) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations provide for the implementation in Great Britain, in relation to animal consumption, of the Commission Decision of 3rd June 1999 on protective measures with regards to contamination by dioxins of certain animal products intended for human or animal consumption.

items.title: The Civil Aviation (Route Charges for Navigation Services) (Third Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Civil Aviation (Route Charges for Navigation Services) Regulations 1997 (the “principal Regulations”) as follows:

items.title: The National Assembly for Wales (Local Government (Contracts) Act 1997) (Modifications) Order 1999
items.description: The Local Government (Contracts) Act 1997 (“the Act”) makes provision about the power of local authorities to enter into contracts.

items.title: The Water and Sewerage Undertakers (Pipelaying and Other Works) (Code of Practice) Order 1999
items.description: This version of the The Water and Sewerage Undertakers (Pipelaying and Other Works) (Code of Practice) Order 1999 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Public Interest Disclosure Act 1998 (Commencement) Order 1999
items.description: This Order brings into force on 2nd July 1999 all the provisions of the Public Interest Disclosure Act 1998 (“the Act”) which did not come into force on the passing of the Act. The provisions of the Act which came into force on its passing are sections 17 and 18 and, for certain purposes, sections 1 and 8.

items.title: The Public Interest Disclosure (Compensation) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations modify the application of provisions of the Employment Rights Act 1996 (“the 1996 Act”) to compensation awarded, or a compensatory award made, in cases where an employee has been dismissed, or selected for dismissal on the grounds of redundancy, because he made a protected disclosure.

items.title: The Public Interest Disclosure (Prescribed Persons) Order 1999
items.description: This Order prescribes persons for the purposes of section 43F of the Employment Rights Act 1996 (“the 1996 Act”).

items.title: The Cosmetic Products (Safety) (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations amend the Cosmetic Products (Safety) Regulations 1996 (S.I. 1996/2925) (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Seeds (Fees) (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 1st July 1999, amend the Seeds (Fees) Regulations 1985 (as amended). They prescribe revised fees in respect of matters arising under the Cereal Seeds Regulations 1993 (as amended), the Fodder Plant Seeds Regulations 1993 (as amended), the Oil and Fibre Plant Seeds Regulations 1993 (as amended), the Beet Seeds Regulations 1993 (as amended), the Vegetable Seeds Regulations 1993 (as amended) and the Seeds (Registration, Licensing and Enforcement) Regulations 1985 (as amended) (regulation 2).

items.title: The Bovines and Bovine Products (Trade) (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations amend the Bovines and Bovine Products (Trade) Regulations 1999, S.I. 1999/1103, to correct an omission in regulation 13(2) of those Regulations. The amendment inserts a reference to the operator of an establishment approved under regulation 12 of those Regulations. Copies of these amending Regulations are being issued free of charge to all known purchasers of S.I. 1999/1103 by the International Trade Unit of the Animal Health (BSE) Division of the Ministry of Agriculture, Fisheries and Food, Government Buildings, Hook Rise South, Tolworth, Surbiton, Surrey KT6 7NF.

items.title: The Railtrack (Luton Parkway Station: Land Acquisition) Order 1999
items.description: The Order confers powers on Railtrack PLC for the compulsory acquisition of land and rights over land at or in the vicinity of the Luton Retail Park in connection with the provision of the proposed new Luton Parkway railway station. The Order does not authorise the construction of the station or any other development.

items.title: The A638 Trunk Road (North Of Doncaster, Redhouse To County Boundary) (Detrunking) Order 1999
items.description: This version of the The A638 Trunk Road (North Of Doncaster, Redhouse To County Boundary) (Detrunking) Order 1999 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The National Lottery etc. Act 1993 (Amendment of Section 23) Order 1999
items.description: This Order amends section 23(2) of the National Lottery etc. Act 1993, which sets out the distributing bodies for expenditure on or connected with sport, by adding the United Kingdom Sports Council to the bodies specified in it. The United Kingdom Sports Council is an incorporated body established by Royal Charter.

items.title: The Excise Goods (Sales on Board Ships and Aircraft) Regulations 1999 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations come into force on 1st July 1999.

items.title: The Miscellaneous Products of Animal Origin (Import Conditions) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations implement in Great Britain, Council Directive 96/90/EC amending Directive 92/118/EEC laying down animal health and public health requirements governing trade in and imports into the Community of miscellaneous products of animal origin.

items.title: The Value Added Tax (Chiropractors) Order 1999
items.description: This Order amends item 1 of Group 7 of Schedule 9 to the Value Added Tax Act 1994 (c. 23) so as to provide exemption from VAT for services supplied by a chiropractor who is registered on the register of chiropractors and for services supplied by persons who are not so registered where the services are wholly performed or directly supervised by a person who is so registered. The Order implements Article 13A(1)(c) of the Sixth Council Directive on the harmonisation of the laws of the member States relating to turnover taxes—common system of value added tax: uniform basis of assessment (77/388/EEC) (O.J. No. L145, 17.5.1977, p.1).

items.title: The Financial Services Act 1986 (Overseas Investment Exchanges and Overseas Clearing Houses) (Periodical Fees) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations prescribe that overseas investment exchanges and overseas clearing houses recognised under the Financial Services Act 1986 must pay periodical fees of £9,000. The first such fee is payable on the date the exchange or clearing house is recognised. Subsequent fees are payable on the anniversary of that date.

items.title: The Home-Grown Cereals Authority (Rate of Levy) Order 1999
items.description: For the purposes of financing the Home-Grown Cereals Authority’s non-trading functions under Part I of the Cereals Marketing Act 1965 for the year beginning 1st July 1999, this Order specifies the rates of dealer levy, grower levy and processor levies which appear to the Ministers to be sufficient to meet the amount apportioned to certain cereals grown in the United Kingdom namely, wheat (including durum wheat), barley, oats, rye, maize, triticale, or any two or more of such cereals grown as one crop, and the rate of levy which appears to them to be sufficient to meet the amount apportioned to certain oilseeds grown in the United Kingdom, namely rapeseed, linseed, soyabean, sunflowerseed or any two or more of such oilseeds grown as one crop.

items.title: The Industrial Training Levy (Engineering Construction Board) Order 1999
items.description: This Order gives effect to proposals of the Engineering Construction Industry Training Board which were submitted to the Secretary of State. The proposals are for the imposition of a levy on employers in the engineering construction industry (including employers who are not mainly engaged in engineering construction activities but are engaged in any related or administrative activities of a kind to which paragraph 1(c) of Schedule 1 to the Industrial Training (Engineering Board) Order 1964 (as amended by the Industrial Training (Engineering Construction Board) Order 1991) applies; and for those employers the levy will be imposed only in respect of the said related or administrative activities) for the purpose of raising money towards meeting the expenses of the Board.

items.title: The Food Safety (Fishery Products and Live Shellfish) (Hygiene) Amendment (No. 2) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Food Safety (Fishery Products and Live Shellfish) (Hygiene) Regulations 1998 (the principal Regulations) to give effect to the provisions of Council Directive 96/43/EC which relate to the financing of hygiene inspections of fishery products. Regulation 9 makes a technical amendment to the Food Safety (Fishery Products and Live Shellfish) (Hygiene) Amendment Regulations 1999 which relates to the introduction of standard registration documents for the movement of live shellfish.

items.title: The Industrial Training Levy (Construction Board) Order 1999
items.description: This Order gives effect to proposals of the Construction Industry Training Board which were submitted to the Secretary of State. The proposals are for the imposition of a levy on employers in the construction industry for the purpose of raising money towards meeting the expenses of the Board.

items.title: The Scotland Act 1998 (Concurrent Functions) Order 1999
items.description: This Order provides that certain functions transferred to the Scottish Ministers by virtue of section 53 of the Scotland Act 1998 (c. 46) shall be exercisable by a Minister of the Crown as well as by the Scottish Ministers.

items.title: The Scotland Act 1998 (Transitory and Transitional Provisions) (Orders subject to Special Parliamentary Procedure) Order 1999
items.description: This Order makes provision for transitory and transitional purposes in connection with the coming into force, on 1st July 1999, of section 94 (private legislation) of the Scotland Act 1998 (c. 46) (“the 1998 Act”), in so far as that section relates to orders subject to special parliamentary procedure (“special procedure orders”).

items.title: The Scotland Act 1998 (Transitory and Transitional Provisions) (Laying of Reports) Order 1999
items.description: This Order makes provision for transitional and transitory purposes under the Scotland Act 1998 (c. 46).

items.title: The Scotland Act 1998 (Transitory and Transitional Provisions) (Complaints of Maladministration) Amendment Order 1999
items.description: This Order amends the Scotland Act 1998 (Transitory and Transitional Provisions) (Complaints of Maladministration) Order 1999 (S.I. 1999/1351) (“the principal Order”). The principal Order made provision for the investigation by a Scottish Parliamentary Commissioner for Administration (“Scottish Commissioner”) of complaints of maladministration in connection with action taken in the exercise of the administrative functions by any member of the Scottish Executive and certain other persons, bodies and authorities.

items.title: The Scotland Act 1998 (Transfer of Borrowing of the Registers of Scotland Executive Agency Trading Fund) Order 1999
items.description: This Order provides for any amount outstanding immediately before the commencement of section 71(5) of the Scotland Act 1998 (c. 46) in respect of the principal of the sum treated by virtue of section 2(3) of the Government Trading Funds Act 1973 (c. 43) as issued to the Registers of Scotland Executive Agency Trading Fund on the day on which the Order establishing that Fund came into force (“the issue date”) to be treated as an advance made by the Secretary of State to the Scottish Ministers on the issue date.

items.title: The Cambuslang College (Change of Name) (Scotland) Order 1999
The Petroleum (Current Model Clauses) Order 1999
items.description: This Order is made for the purposes of section 5 of the Petroleum Act 1998 (c. 17) and comes into force on the commencement of that Act, that it to say on its commencement in accordance with section 52 (see section 48(2)), referred to below as “the appointed day” (15th February 1999).

items.title: The Goods Infringing Intellectual Property Rights (Customs) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations revoke and replace the Counterfeit and Pirated Goods (Customs) Regulations 1995. Those earlier Regulations had made provision consequential upon Council Regulation (EC) No. 3295/94, laying down measures to prohibit the release for free circulation, export, re-export or entry for a suspensive procedure of counterfeit and pirated goods. That Council Regulation has since been amended by Council Regulation (EC) No. 241/1999, which from 1st July 1999 extends the scope of the prohibitions to goods infringing patents and supplementary protection certificates and to goods under all forms of customs supervision. It also provides a procedure enabling holders of Community trademarks to make a single application for customs intervention in any number of member States.

items.title: The Contaminants in Food (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations, which apply to Great Britain, make provision for the enforcement and execution of Commission Regulation (EC) No. 1525/98 (OJ No. L201, 17.7.98, p. 43) and Commission Regulation (EC) No. 864/1999 (OJ No. L108, 27.4.1999, p. 16) both of which amend Commission Regulation (EC) No. 194/97 setting maximum levels for certain contaminants in foodstuffs (together “the Commission Regulation”). These Regulations also implement Commission Directive 98/53/EC (OJ No. L201, 17.7.98, p. 93) laying down the sampling methods and the methods of analysis for the official control of the levels for certain contaminants in foodstuffs.

items.title: The National Health Service (Indicative Amounts) (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (Indicative Amounts) Regulations 1997 so as to provide for the Bro Taf Health Authority to perform the function previously performed under those Regulations by the Welsh Health Common Services Authority. The Welsh Health Common Services Authority was abolished, by the Welsh Health Common Services Authority (Abolition) Order, on 1st April 1999.

items.title: The Housing Renewal Grants (Prescribed Form and Particulars) (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations amend the Form set out in the Schedule to the Housing Renewal Grants (Prescribed Form and Particulars) Regulations 1996, to be used by owner-occupiers and tenants when applying for housing renewal grants under Chapter I of Part I of the Housing Grants, Construction and Regeneration Act 1996.

items.title: The Road Traffic Regulation Act 1984 (Amendment) Order 1999
items.description: This Order amends Schedule 9 to the Road Traffic Regulation Act 1984.

items.title: The Magistrates' Courts Committees (West Glamorgan and South Wales) Amalgamation Order 1999
items.description: This Order provides for the replacement of the magistrates' courts committees for West Glamorgan and South Wales by a single committee to be known as the South Wales magistrates' courts committee.

items.title: The Petroleum Act 1998 (Commencement No. 1) Order 1999
items.description: This Order provides that the Petroleum Act 1998 shall come into force in accordance with section 52(4) thereof on 15th February 1999. Subsection (4) of section 52 is subject to subsections (1) and (2) of that section, which apply in relation to the coming into force of the “excepted provisions” mentioned in article 2(2). The Order accordingly brings into force on that date all the provisions of the Act other than those excepted provisions.

items.title: The Army Terms of Service (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations amend the Army Terms of Service Regulations 1992 (“the 1992 Regulations”).

items.title: The National Savings Bank (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1999
items.description: These regulations amend the National Savings Bank Regulations 1972 so as to introduce a new category of individual savings account (“ISA”) known as a TESSA only account. Provision for the introduction of ISAs was made by the Individual Savings Account Regulations 1998 and the National Savings Bank introduced its own ISA on 6th April 1999 pursuant to the National Savings Bank (Amendment) Regulations 1999 which amended the National Savings Bank Regulations 1972. References to particular regulations in the rest of this note are to the 1972 Regulations as amended by these Regulations.

items.title: The Public Record Office (Fees) (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations delete from the Public Record Office (Fees) Regulations 1999 fee 6.1(ix), which related to the supply of copies of records with additional finishes.

items.title: The Excise Duties (Personal Reliefs) (Amendment) Order 1999
items.description: This Order, which comes into force on 1st July 1999, amends the Excise Duties (Personal Reliefs) Order 1992 (S.I. 1992/3155) (the principal Order).

items.title: The Goods Infringing Intellectual Property Rights (Consequential Provisions) Regulations 1999
items.description: The Counterfeit and Pirated Goods (Consequential Provisions) Regulations 1995 made provision consequential on Council Regulation (EC) No. 3295/94, laying down measures to prohibit the release for free circulation, export, re-export or entry for a suspensive procedure of counterfeit and pirated goods (O.J. L341, 30.12.94).

items.title: The General Teaching Council for Wales (Constitution) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations provide for the constitution of the General Teaching Council for Wales. The Council will consist of twelve elected teacher members, nine members appointed by the Secretary of State from nominees of organisations set out in the Schedule and four members appointed by the Secretary of State.

items.title: The Road Traffic Offenders (Prescribed Devices) Order 1999
items.description: This Order prescribes a speed detection device for the purposes of section 20 of the Road Traffic Offenders Act 1988, as substituted by section 23 of the Road Traffic Act 1991 (speeding offences etc.: admissibility of certain evidence). The device prescribed captures and records images of motor vehicles at two positions on the road and calculates the average speed of the vehicles between those positions.

items.title: The National Health Service (General Medical Services) (Scotland) Amendment (No.3) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations further amend the National Health Service (General Medical) Services (Scotland) Regulations 1995 (S.I. 1995/416) (“the 1995 Regulations”) which regulate the terms on which doctors provide general medical services under the National Health Service (Scotland) Act 1978.

items.title: The Registration of Marriages (Welsh Language) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations consolidate with mostly minor amendments the Registration of Marriages (Welsh Language) Regulations 1986, and subsequent amending regulations which are revoked. The Welsh language translation of the forms set out in Schedule one to the regulations has been updated and modernised.

items.title: The Welfare of Animals (Transport) (Amendment) Order 1999
items.description: This Order amends the Welfare of Animals (Transport) Order 1997 (S.I. 1997/1480 as amended by S.I. 1998/2537). It makes provision for the enforcement of Council Regulation (EC) No. 411/98 (“the Regulation”) on additional animal protection standards applicable to road vehicles used for the carriage of livestock on journeys exceeding eight hours (OJ No. L52, 21.2.98, p. 8).

items.title: The Social Security and Child Support (Decisions and Appeals) Amendment (No. 2) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security and Child Support (Decisions and Appeals) Regulations 1999 (“the principal Regulations”). Regulation 2 amends regulation 3(5) as regards decisions on disability or incapacity related benefits. Revision can take place on grounds connected with the claimant’s condition for error of law or ignorance of or mistake as to a material fact if the claimant or recipient of the benefit knew or could reasonably be expected to know of the fact and that it was relevant to the original decision.

items.title: The National Health Service (General Medical Services) Amendment (No. 2) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations further amend the National Health Service (General Medical Services) Regulations 1992 (“the principal Regulations”) which regulate the terms on which doctors provide general medical services under the National Health Service Act 1977 (“the 1977 Act”).

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Axxon Telecom Limited) Order 1999
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Axxon Telecom Limited a licence on 9th December 1998 under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in the licence. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 14th June 1999. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Axxon Telecom Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force on 13th July 1999.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Cyberlight Europe Plc) Order 1999
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Cyberlight Europe Plc a licence on 13th October 1998 under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in the licence. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 14th June 1999. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Cyberlight Europe Plc will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force on 13th July 1999.

items.title: The Midland Metro (Penalty Fares) (Activating) Order 1999
items.description: This Order gives effect, as from 18th January 1999, to the penalty fare provisions in sections 4 to 7 of the Midland Metro (Penalty Fares) Act 1991 with respect to travel on any vehicle of the Midland Metro system.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (NTT Europe Limited) Order 1999
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to NTT Europe Limited a licence on 13th October 1998 under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in the licence. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 14th June 1999. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, NTT Europe Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force on 13th July 1999.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Japan Telecom UK Limited) Order 1999
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Japan Telecom UK Limited a licence on 9th December 1998 under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in the licence. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 14th June 1999. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Japan Telecom UK Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force on 13th July 1999.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Jersey Telecoms) Order 1999
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Jersey Telecoms a licence on 9th December 1998 under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in the licence. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 14th June 1999. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Jersey Telecoms will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force on 13th July 1999.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (City Telecom (HK) Limited) Order 1999
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to City Telecom (HK) Limited a licence on 9th December 1998 under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in the licence. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 14th June 1999. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 14th June 1999. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, International Optical Network Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force on 13th July 1999.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Belgacom UK Limited) Order 1999
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Belgacom UK Limited a licence on 13th October 1998 under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in the licence. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 14th June 1999. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Belgacom UK Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force on 13th July 1999.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Call-Net (UK) Limited) Order 1999
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Call-Net (UK) Limited a licence on 13th October 1998 under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in the licence. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 14th June 1999. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Call-Net (UK) Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force on 13th July 1999.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Flute Limited) Order 1999
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Flute Limited a licence on 9th December 1998 under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in the licence. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 14th June 1999. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Flute Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force on 13th July 1999.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Zereau Limited) Order 1999
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Zereau Limited a licence on 13th October 1998 under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in the licence. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 14th June 1999. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Zereau Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force on 13th July 1999.

items.title: The Housing Revenue Account General Fund Contribution Limits (Scotland) Order 1999
The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Eurotunnel Telecommunications Limited) Order 1999
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Eurotunnel Telecommunications Limited a licence on 5th August 1998 under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in the licence. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 14th June 1999. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Eurotunnel Telecommunications Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force on 13th July 1999.

items.title: The Driving Licences (Exchangeable Licences) Order 1999
items.description: This Order designates South Africa and the provinces and territories of Canada under section 108(2)(b) of the Road Traffic Act 1988 as making satisfactory provision for the issue of certain classes of driving licence. This enables a person holding one of those licences to exchange it for a corresponding British licence.
