items.title: The Prevention of Terrorism (Temporary Provisions) Act 1989 (Designated Ports) Order 1999
items.description: This Order adds Campbeltown, Southampton, Torquay and Troon Seaports, and Sheffield City Airport, to the list of ports designated, for the purposes of paragraph 8 of Schedule 5 to the Prevention of Terrorism (Temporary Provisions) Act 1989, in Part I of Schedule 6 to that Act.

items.title: The Education (Islamia Primary School, Brent) (Exemption from Pay and Conditions) Order 1999
items.description: This Order, made under section 3(4) of the School Teachers' Pay and Conditions Act 1991 (“the Act”), comes into force on 15th November 1999. With effect from that date, section 2(6) and (7) of the Act, which provides that the statutory conditions of employment of school teachers (their remuneration and such of their other conditions of employment as relate to their professional duties and working time) are governed by pay and conditions orders made under section 2(1) of that Act, shall no longer apply to the statutory conditions of employment of school teachers in Islamia Primary School, Brent.

items.title: The Carriage by Air (Sterling Equivalents) Order 1999
items.description: This Order specifies the sterling equivalents of amounts expressed in gold francs as the limit of the air carrier’s liability under the Warsaw Convention. It supersedes the Carriage by Air (Sterling Equivalents) Order 1996.

items.title: The Medicines (Aristolochia) (Temporary Prohibition) Order 1999
items.description: This Order prohibits the sale, supply and importation of any medicinal product consisting of or containing a plant belonging to a species of the genus Aristolochia, or any extract from such a plant.

items.title: The Local Government Residuary Body (England) (Winding Up) Order 1999
items.description: This Order winds up the Local Government Residuary Body (“the Residuary Body”) which was established to deal with the residual matters of abolished authorities following the review of the structure of local government in England. All remaining property, rights, liabilities and functions (including functions in relation to the Residuary Body’s accounts) are transferred to the Secretary of State. Part II (constitution and membership) of the Order which established the Residuary Body is revoked.

items.title: Nuclear Reactors (Environmental Impact Assessment for Decommissioning) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations implement as respects Great Britain Council Directive 85/337/EEC on the assessment of the effects of certain public and private projects on the environment (O.J. No. L175, 5.7.1985, p.40), as amended by Council Directive 97/11/EC (O.J. No. L73, 14.3.1997, p.5), to the extent that the Directive relates to the dismantling or decommissioning of nuclear power stations and other nuclear reactors.

items.title: The General Optical Council (Testing of Sight by Persons Training as Ophthalmic Opticians Rules) (Amendment) Order of Council 1999
items.description: The Rules approved by this Order amend the General Optical Council’s Rules on the Testing of Sight by Persons Training as Ophthalmic Opticians 1993. They add an additional category of persons to those exempt from section 24(1) of the Opticians Act 1989 (which makes it an offence for anyone other than a registered medical practitioner or registered ophthalmic optician to test the sight of another person). The additional category is that of persons training as ophthalmic opticians outside the United Kingdom but currently receiving instruction at a training institution approved by the General Optical Council.

items.title: The Confirmation to Small Estates (Scotland) Order 1999
The Legal Services Ombudsman (Jurisdiction) (Amendment) Order 1999
items.description: This Order extends the jurisdiction of the Legal Services Ombudsman under section 22 of the Courts and Legal Services Act 1990 to include the Chartered Institute of Patent Agents.

items.title: The Community Health Councils (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations amend the Community Health Councils Regulations 1996 to provide that the duty on Health Authorities to consult a Community Health Council on proposals for substantial developments or variations in the health service in the Council’s district (regulation 18(1) of the 1996 Regulations) does not apply to proposals to establish or dissolve a Primary Care Trust in England.

items.title: The Treasury Bills (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations amend the Treasury Bills Regulations 1877 to–

items.title: The Finance Act 1998, Section 159 (Appointed Day) and Schedule 26 (Commencement Etc.) Order 1999
items.description: Section 159(1) of the Finance Act 1998 amends section 8 of the Treasury Bills Act 1877 to enable Treasury bills to be issued by the Treasury (either directly or through such agent as the Treasury think fit). Section 159(2) of the 1998 Act provides for that amendment to apply in relation to issues made on or after such day as the Treasury may appoint by order. This Order appoints 15th November 1999 as the day in question.

items.title: The Cornwall Healthcare National Health Service Trust (Transfer of Trust Property) Order 1999
items.description: This Order transfers to the Cornwall Healthcare National Health Service Trust on 16th November 1999 trust property held by the Plymouth Community Services National Health Service Trust. A copy of the schedule of trust property to which the Order refers can be obtained from the Department of Health, Accounts and Trust Allocations Branch, Quarry House, Quarry Hill, Leeds LS2 7UE.

items.title: The Police and Criminal Evidence Act 1984 (Codes of Practice No. 5) Order 1999
items.description: This Order appoints 1st March 1999 as the date on which a revised code of practice under section 66(a) of the Police and Criminal Evidence Act 1984 will come into operation. It supersedes the revised code which has been in operation since 15th May 1997.

items.title: The Bath and West Community National Health Service Trust (Transfer of Trust Property) Order 1999
items.description: This Order transfers to the Bath and West Community National Health Service Trust on 16th November 1999 trust property formerly held by the Taunton and Somerset National Health Service Trust. A copy of the schedule of trust property to which the Order refers can be obtained from the Department of Health, Accounts and Trust Allocations Branch, Room 3W58, Quarry House, Quarry Hill, Leeds LS2 7UE.

items.title: The Housing (Right to Buy) (Priority of Charges) (England) Order 1999
items.description: This Order specifies three bodies as approved lending institutions for the purposes of section 156 of the Housing Act 1985 (priority of charges on disposals under the right to buy). Other bodies have been specified by previous orders. Such bodies hereby also become approved lending institutions for the purposes of section 36 of the 1985 Act (priority of charges on voluntary disposals by local authorities) and section 12 of the Housing Act 1996 (priority of charges on voluntary disposals by registered social landlords).

items.title: The Housing (Right to Buy) (Limits on Discount) (Wales) Order 1999
items.description: A person exercising the right to buy a dwelling-house in Wales under Part V of the Housing Act 1985 may be entitled, under sections 129 to 131 of and Schedule 4 to the Act, to a discount equal to a percentage of the price before discount.

items.title: The Motor Cycles Etc. (EC Type Approval) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations consolidate with amendments the Motor Cycles (EC Type Approval) Regulations 1995.

items.title: The University Hospitals of Leicester National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Order 1999
items.description: This Order establishes the University Hospitals of Leicester National Health Service Trust, an NHS trust provided for in section 5 of the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990. It also provides for the functions of the trust both before (article 7) and after (article 3) its operational date (the date on which it assumes all its functions) and the number of executive and non-executive directors (article 4), and that one of the latter shall be appointed from the University of Leicester (article 5). It specifies the operational date and the accounting date of the trust (article 6) and makes provision for assistance to the trust by Leicestershire Health Authority before its operational date (article 8). Finally, it specifies the value of assets in excess of which the Secretary of State is to consider the disposal of the assets (article 9).

items.title: The District of Kennet (Electoral Changes) Order 1999
items.description: This Order gives effect to recommendations by the Local Government Commission for England for electoral changes in the district of Kennet. The changes have effect in relation to local government elections to be held on and after 1st May 2003.

items.title: The District of North Wiltshire (Electoral Changes) Order 1999
items.description: This Order gives effect to recommendations by the Local Government Commission for England for electoral changes in the district of North Wiltshire. The changes have effect in relation to local government elections to be held on and after 1st May 2003.

items.title: The District of Salisbury (Electoral Changes) Order 1999
items.description: This Order gives effect, with modifications, to recommendations by the Local Government Commission for England for electoral changes in the district of Salisbury. The changes have effect in relation to local government elections to be held on and after 1st May 2003. The modifications relate to the names of the wards now designated Laverstock, St Martin and Milford, and Lower Wylye and Woodford Valley.

items.title: The Excise Duty (Amendment of the Isle of Man Act 1979) Order 1999
items.description: This Order, which comes into force on 1st December 1999, amends section 1(1) of the Isle of Man Act 1979(c. 58). It provides for lottery duty chargeable under the law of the United Kingdom or the Isle of Man to be included in the common duty arrangements.

items.title: The District of West Wiltshire (Electoral Changes) Order 1999
items.description: This Order gives effect to recommendations by the Local Government Commission for England for electoral changes in the district of West Wiltshire. The changes have effect in relation to local government elections to be held on and after 1st May 2003.

items.title: The Borough of Swindon (Electoral Changes) Order 1999
items.description: This Order gives effect to recommendations by the Local Government Commission for England for electoral changes in the borough of Swindon.

items.title: The Town and Country Planning (Environmental Impact Assessment) (England and Wales) Regulations 1999 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations implement, in relation to town and country planning in England and Wales, Council Directive 85/337/EEC on the assessment of the effects of certain public and private projects on the environment (O.J. No. L 175, 5.7.1985, p. 40), as amended by Council Directive 97/11/EC (O.J. No. L 73, 14.3.1997, p.5).

items.title: The Value Added Tax (Input Tax) (Amendment) Order 1999
items.description: This Order amends the Value Added Tax (Input Tax) Order 1992 (S.I. 1992/3222) (the Input Tax Order). Part of the changes take effect on 1st December 1999 and part on 1st March 2000.

items.title: The Rail Vehicle Accessibility (Serco Metrolink T68A Vehicles) Exemption Order 1999
items.description: This version of the The Rail Vehicle Accessibility (Serco Metrolink T68A Vehicles) Exemption Order 1999 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Organic Farming (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations amend the Organic Farming Regulations 1999 (as amended) (“the principal Regulations”) which provide for the payment of aid to farmers who undertake to introduce organic farming methods and comply with certain environmental management conditions, pursuant to Article 2(1)(a) and (d) of Council Regulation (EEC) No. 2078/92 (OJ No. L215, 30.7.92, p. 85) (“the Agri-environment Regulation”). The Agri-environment Regulation was repealed by Article 55(1) of Council Regulation (EC) No. 1257/99 (OJ No. L160, 26.6.99, p.80), but, by virtue of Article 55(3) of the latter Regulation, the Agri-environment Regulation continues to apply to actions approved by the Commission before 1st January 2000.

items.title: The Wireless Telegraphy (Cordless Telephone Apparatus) (Restriction and Marking) Order 1999
items.description: This Order revokes and replaces the Wireless Telegraphy (Cordless Telephone Apparatus) (Restriction and Marking) Order 1992 (S.I. 1992/2009).

items.title: The Northern Ireland Act 1998 (Commencement No. 4) Order 1999
The Education (Amendment of School and Pupil Information Regulations) (England) Regulations 1999
items.description: The Regulations amend the Education (School Performance Information) (England) Regulations 1999 (“the 1999 Regulations”), which relate to the collection and publication of information about the performance of schools. They also amend the Education (Individual Pupils' Achievements) (Information) Regulations 1997 (“the 1997 Regulations”), which relate to the head teacher’s report to parents and adult pupils on individual pupils' achievements, and apply in relation to pupils and former pupils at maintained schools in England only.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Core Telecommunications Limited) Order 1999
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Core Telecommunications Limited a licence on 25th June 1999 under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in the licence. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 8th November 1999. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Core Telecommunications Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force on 7th December 1999.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (WinStar Communications Limited) Order 1999
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to WinStar Communications Limited a licence on 25th June 1999 under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in the licence. items.title: The Naval, Military and Air Forces Etc. (Disablement and Death) Service Pensions Amendment Order 1999
items.description: This Order amends the Naval, Military and Air Forces Etc. (Disablement and Death) Service Pensions Order 1983 (“the principal Order”) which makes provision for pensions and other awards in respect of disablement or death due to service in the naval, military and air forces.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (TNI (Telecom) Limited) Order 1999

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (WXNET Limited) Order 1999

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (G.C. Global Communications Limited) Order 1999
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to G.C. Global Communications Limited a licence on 28th June 1999 under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in the licence. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 8th November 1999. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, G.C. Global Communications Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force on 7th December 1999.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Level 3 Communications Limited) Order 1999
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Level 3 Communications Limited a licence on 28th October 1998 under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in the licence. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 8th November 1999. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Level 3 Communications Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force on 7th December 1999.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Pantheon Telecom (UK) Limited) Order 1999
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Pantheon Telecom (UK) Limited a licence on 11th June 1999 under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in the licence. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 8th November 1999. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Pantheon Telecom (UK) Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force on 7th December 1999.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (BSI ACCESS UK Limited) Order 1999
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to BSI ACCESS UK Limited a licence on 15th June 1999 under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in the licence. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 8th November 1999. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, BSI ACCESS UK Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force on 7th December 1999.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (StarGlobal Ltd) Order 1999
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to StarGlobal Ltd a licence on 1st June 1999 under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in the licence. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 8th November 1999. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, StarGlobal Ltd will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force on 7th December 1999.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Telecom New Zealand (UK) Licences Limited) Order 1999
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Telecom New Zealand (UK) Licences Limited a licence on 1st June 1999 under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in the licence. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 8th November 1999. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Telecom New Zealand (UK) Licences Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force on 7th December 1999.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (CompleTel UK Limited) Order 1999
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to CompleTel UK Limited a licence on 11th January 1999 under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in the licence. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 8th November 1999. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, CompleTel UK Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force on 7th December 1999.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Teleport UK Limited (trading as Satellite Media Services)) Order 1999
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Teleport UK Limited (trading as Satellite Media Services) a licence on 15th January 1999 under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in the licence. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 8th November 1999. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Teleport UK Limited (trading as Satellite Media Services) will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force on 7th December 1999.

items.title: The Northern Birmingham Mental Health National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Amendment Order 1999
items.description: This Order amends the Northern Birmingham Mental Health National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Order 1994 (“the Establishment Order”) which established the Northern Birmingham Mental Health National Health Service Trust, by substituting a new article 3 (functions of the trust).

items.title: The Local Authorities (Alteration of Requisite Calculations) (Wales) Regulations 1999
items.description: Sections 32 and 43 of the Local Government Finance Act 1992 (“the 1992 Act”) set out respectively how a billing authority and a major precepting authority are to calculate their budget requirements for a financial year and sections 33 and 44 of the 1992 Act set out respectively how a billing authority and a major precepting authority are to calculate the basic amount of their council tax.

items.title: The Local Authorities (Requisite Calculations) (Wales) (Prescribed Amounts) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations prescribe the proportion of council tax benefit and the guideline budget requirement for each local authority in Wales for the financial year beginning on 1st April 1999 for the purposes of sections 33(3C) and 33(3D) of the Local Government Finance Act 1992.

items.title: The Corporation Tax (Simplified Arrangements for Group Relief) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations make provision for arrangements (“the arrangements”) under which corporation tax group relief may be claimed or surrendered by an authorised company acting on behalf of companies in the group in relation to accounting periods of claimant companies ending on or after 1st July 1999.

items.title: The Crime and Disorder Act 1998 (Commencement No. 5) Order 1999
items.description: This Order brings sections 46 and 49 of the Crime and Disorder Act 1998 into force, to the extent that they are not already in force, on 1st November 1999. These provisions relate to a bailed person’s first appearance in a magistrates' court and the powers of single justices respectively. Both sections are already in force in the areas specified in Schedule 2 to the Crime and Disorder Act 1998 (Commencement No. 2 and Transitional Provisions) Order 1998.

items.title: The Asbestos (Prohibitions) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations amend the Asbestos (Prohibitions) Regulations 1992 (S.I. 1992/3067) (“the Asbestos Regulations 1992") and come into force on 24th November 1999. The Asbestos (Prohibitions) (Amendment) Regulations 1999 (S.I. 1999/2373) (“the Asbestos Amendment Regulations 1999") also amend the Asbestos Regulations 1992 and come into force on 24th November 1999.

items.title: The Road Vehicles (Brake Linings Safety) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations restrict the supply of brake linings on safety grounds and give effect to Commission Directive 98/12/EC of 27th January 1998 adapting to technical progress Council Directive 71/320/EEC on the approximation of the laws of the Member States relating to the braking devices of certain categories of motor vehicles and their trailers (“the Braking Devices Directive”). The Directive lays down standards for asbestos-free brake linings. These Regulations are made under section 11(5) of the Consumer Protection Act 1987 and adopt the definition of “vehicle” used in the Road Traffic Act 1988 and in the Road Traffic (Northern Ireland) Order 1995 (S.I. 1995/2994) (N.I.18). The definition includes motor cycles.

items.title: The Financial Markets and Insolvency (Settlement Finality) Regulations 1999
items.description: The Regulations implement Directive 98/26/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council on settlement finality in payment and securities settlement systems (OJ L166, 19.5.98, p. 45) (“the Directive”). The Directive seeks to reduce the risks associated with participation in payment and securities settlement systems by minimising the disruption caused by insolvency proceedings brought against a participant in such a system.

items.title: The Devon (Coroners' Districts) Order 1999
items.description: The County of Devon was divided into four coroners' districts by the Devon (Coroners' Districts) Order 1979 (S.I. 1979/590). Following the establishment of unitary authorities for Plymouth and Torbay in 1998, two of those coroners' districts, Torbay and South Devon and Plymouth and South-West Devon became separated from the County of Devon for the purposes of the jurisdiction of coroners by virtue of the Devon (Coroners) Order 1998 (S.I. 1998/355). This Order amalgamates the other two coroners' districts established by the Devon (Coroners' Districts) Order 1979, Exeter and East Devon and North and West Devon, into one coroner’s district to be known as the Exeter and Greater Devon coroner’s district.

items.title: The River Thames (Hungerford Footbridges) Order 1999
items.description: This Order authorises the interference with navigation that may be caused by the construction of the proposed Hungerford Footbridges across the River Thames.

items.title: The Fishing Vessels (EC Directive on Harmonised Safety Regime) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations give effect in part to Council Directive 97/70/EC setting up a harmonised safety regime for fishing vessels of 24 metres in length and over (OJ L34, 9.2.98, p.1) as amended by Commisson Directive 1999/19/EC (OJ L83, 27.3.99, p.48) which is not relevant to these Regulations (“the Council Directive”). The Council Directive essentially provides for compliance with the Torremolinos Protocol (Cmnd. 3339) relating to the Torremolinos International Convention for the Safety of Fishing Vessels 1977 (Cmnd. 7252) (“the Torremolinos Protocol”).

items.title: The Financial Services Act 1986 (Restriction of Exemption) Order 1999
items.description: The Order restricts the exemption for which section 45(1)(j) of the Financial Services Act 1986 makes provision for the managers of common investment funds established under section 22 of the Charities Act 1960 or section 24 of the Charities Act 1993. Subject to the transitional provision for which article 3 of the Order makes provision, its effect is that the exemption will only apply to the manager of a fund the trusts of which prevent property being transferred to the fund except by or on behalf of a charity the charity trustees of which are also the trustees appointed to manage the fund. This type of fund is commonly known as a pooling scheme fund.

items.title: The Social Security (Categorisation of Earners) Amendment Regulations 1999
The Insurance Brokers (Registration) Act 1977 (Amendment) Order 1999
items.description: This Order reduces the number of members to represent registered insurance brokers on the Insurance Brokers Registration Council and provides for the nomination of four members of the Council by the Treasury.

items.title: The A406 Trunk Road (Hanger Lane, Ealing) (Prohibition of U-Turn) Order 1999
items.description: This version of the The A406 Trunk Road (Hanger Lane, Ealing) (Prohibition of U-Turn) Order 1999 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Magistrates' Courts Committee Areas Order 1999
items.description: This is the first order under section 27A(2) of the Justices of the Peace Act 1997, inserted by section 81 of the Access to Justice Act 1999. It specifies the magistrates' courts committee areas for England outside Greater London and Wales. Each of the areas was a magistrates' courts committee area immediately before section 81 came into force.

items.title: The Petty Sessions Areas Order 1999
items.description: This is the first Order made under section 4 of the Justices of the Peace Act 1997 as substituted by section 75 of the Access to Justice Act 1999. It specifies the petty sessions areas for England and Wales. Each of the areas was a petty sessions area immediately before section 75 came into force.

items.title: The Justices of the Peace (Commission Areas) Order 1999
items.description: This is the first order under section 1 of the Justices of the Peace Act 1997 as substituted by section 74 of the Access to Justice Act 1999. It specifies the commission areas for England and Wales. Each of the areas was a commission area immediately before section 74 came into force.

items.title: The Road Traffic (NHS Charges) (Appeals: Ending of Transitional Period) Order 1999
items.description: This Order appoints 28th November 1999 as the day on which the transitional period for England and Wales ends for the purposes of the appeals provisions of the Road Traffic (NHS Charges) Act 1999.

items.title: The Wednesbury Education Action Zone Order 1999
items.description: This Order establishes the Wednesbury Education Action Zone, which comprises the schools listed in Schedule 2. The Order also provides (in articles 4 and 5) for the membership of the Education Action Forum for the zone.

items.title: The Wythenshawe Education Action Zone Order 1999
items.description: This Order establishes the Wythenshawe Education Action Zone, which comprises the schools listed in Schedule 2. The Order also provides (in articles 4 and 5) for the membership of the Education Action Forum for the zone.

items.title: The East Manchester Education Action Zone Order 1999
items.description: This Order establishes the East Manchester Education Action Zone, which comprises the schools listed in Schedule 2. The Order also provides (in articles 4 and 5) for the membership of the Education Action Forum for the zone.

items.title: The Dudley Partnership for Achievement Education Action Zone Order 1999
items.description: This Order establishes the Dudley Partnership for Achievement Education Action Zone, which comprises the schools listed in Schedule 2. The Order also provides (in articles 4 and 5) for the membership of the Education Action Forum for the zone.

items.title: The Telford and Wrekin Education Action Zone Order 1999
items.description: This Order establishes the Telford and Wrekin Education Action Zone, which comprises the schools listed in Schedule 2. The Order also provides (in articles 4 and 5) for the membership of the Education Action Forum for the zone.

items.title: The Heart of Slough Education Action Zone Order 1999
items.description: This Order establishes the Heart of Slough Education Action Zone, which comprises the schools listed in Schedule 2. The Order also provides (in articles 4 and 5) for the membership of the Education Action Forum for the zone.

items.title: The Southend Education Action Zone Order 1999
items.description: This Order establishes the Southend Education Action Zone, which comprises the schools listed in Schedule 2. The Order also provides (in articles 4 and 5) for the membership of the Education Action Forum for the zone.

items.title: The Hastings and St Leonards Education Action Zone Order 1999
items.description: This Order establishes the Hastings and St Leonards Education Action Zone, which comprises the schools listed in Schedule 2. The Order also provides (in articles 4 and 5) for the membership of the Education Action Forum for the zone.

items.title: The Preston Education Action Zone Order 1999
items.description: This Order establishes the Preston Education Action Zone, which comprises the schools listed in Schedule 2. The Order also provides (in articles 4 and 5) for the membership of the Education Action Forum for the zone.

items.title: The North East Derbyshire Coalfields Education Action Zone Order 1999
items.description: This Order establishes the North East Derbyshire Coalfields Education Action Zone, which comprises the schools listed in Schedule 2. The Order also provides (in articles 4 and 5) for the membership of the Education Action Forum for the zone.

items.title: The New Horizons Kent–Somerset Virtual Education Action Zone Order 1999
items.description: This Order establishes the New Horizons Kent–Somerset Virtual Education Action Zone, which comprises the schools listed in Schedule 2. The Order also provides (in articles 4 and 5) for the membership of the Education Action Forum for the zone.

items.title: The Widening Horizons—North Islington Education Action Zone Order 1999
items.description: This Order establishes the Widening Horizons—North Islington Education Action Zone, which comprises the schools listed in Schedule 2. The Order also provides (in articles 4 and 5) for the membership of the Education Action Forum for the zone.

items.title: The Hackney Education Action Zone Order 1999
items.description: This Order establishes the Hackney Education Action Zone, which comprises the schools listed in Schedule 2. The Order also provides (in articles 4 and 5) for the membership of the Education Action Forum for the zone.

items.title: The Gas Act 1986 (Exemptions) (No. 4) (Amendment) Order 1999
items.description: This Order amends the Gas Act 1986 (Exemptions) (No. 4) Order 1996 (S.I. 1996/2795), so as to extend its duration to 31st December 2000 (instead of 30th November 1999).

items.title: The Competition Act 1998 (Commission Investigation and Director’s Investigation) Order 1999
items.description: This Order prescribes the provisions of Community law for the purposes of the meanings of “Commission investigation” and “Director’s investigation” given in section 61(1) of the Competition Act 1998.

items.title: The Housing (Right to Acquire) (Discount) (Amendment) Order 1999
items.description: This Order amends article 2 of the Housing (Right to Acquire) (Discount) Order 1999 which sets the amount of discount given to tenants purchasing their homes under sections 16 and 17 of the Housing Act 1996 (the right to acquire). The Order provides that the maximum discount a tenant may receive is 50% of the market value of the dwelling house.

items.title: The Value Added Tax Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 1st December 1999, further amend the Value Added Tax Regulations 1995 (S.I. 1995/2518) (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Carriage of Dangerous Goods (Amendment) Regulations 1999 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations implement—Commission Directive 96/86/EC (OJ No. L335, 24.12.96, p.43) adapting to technical progress Council Directive 94/55/EC (OJ No. L319, 12.12.94, p.7) on the approximation of the laws of the Member States with regard to the transport of dangerous goods by road;Commission Directive 96/87/EC (OJ No. L335, 24.12.96, p.45) adapting to technical progress Council Directive 96/49/EC (OJ No. L235, 17.9.96, p.25) on the approximation of the laws of the Member States with regard to the transport of dangerous goods by rail;Commission Directive 1999/47/EC (OJ No. L169, 5.7.99, p.1) of 5th October 1998 adapting to technical progress Council Directive 94/55/EC and Commission Directive 96/86/EC on the approximation of the laws of the Member States with regard to the transport of dangerous goods by road; andCommission Directive 1999/48/EC (OJ No. L169, 5.7.99, p.58) of 5th October 1998 adapting to technical progress Council Directive 96/49/EC and Commission Directive 96/87/EC on the approximation of the laws of the Member States with regard to the transport of dangerous goods by rail.

items.title: The Insurance Brokers Registration Council Election Scheme Approval Order 1999
items.description: This Order approves an election scheme made by the Insurance Brokers Registration Council in exercise of their powers under paragraph 2(2)(a) of the Schedule to the Insurance Brokers (Registration) Act 1977. The Scheme provides for the election of six members to represent registered insurance brokers on the Council. The Scheme also provides for the Insurance Brokers Registration Council, in consultation with the Treasury, to waive the need for elections on it being expedient for there not to be an election, in view of the likelihood, within 24 months of any elections being held, of the dissolution of the Council, or the transfer or repeal of any of its functions.

items.title: The Building Societies (Members' Requisitions) Order 1999
items.description: This Order amends the definition of a members' requisition in paragraph 20A(2) of Schedule 2 to the Building Societies Act 1986 by increasing to 500 the minimum number of a building society’s members needed to requisition a special meeting of the society.

items.title: The Building Societies (Nominations for Directors' Election) Order 1999
items.description: This Order amends section 61(1) of the Building Societies Act 1986 by making a fivefold increase in the minimum number of members whom a building society may require under its rules to nominate a person for election as a director. The minimum number is on a scale between 50 and 250 depending on the size of the society.

items.title: The Building Societies (Members' Resolutions) Order 1999
items.description: This Order increases the requisite number of members of a building society as defined in paragraph 31(2)(a) of Schedule 2 to the Building Societies Act 1986 who may propose a resolution at an annual general meeting. Where a society’s commercial assets exceed £100 million, the requisite number of members is five hundred and in any other case it is one hundred.

items.title: The Glenfield Hospital, the Leicester General Hospital and the Leicester Royal Infirmary National Health Service Trusts (Dissolution) Order 1999
items.description: This Order dissolves on 1st April 2000:

items.title: The Income Tax (Indexation) (No. 2) Order 1999
items.description: Section 257C(3) of the Income and Corporation Taxes Act 1988 (“the Act”) operates to provide that the Treasury shall by order made by statutory instrument before 6th April 2000 specify the amount which by virtue of that section shall, unless Parliament otherwise determines, be treated as specified for the purpose of section 257(1) of the Act (personal allowance for persons under 65) for the year of assessment 2000–01. This Order specifies that amount as £4,385.

items.title: The Magistrates' Courts (Forms) (Amendment No. 2) Rules 1999
items.description: These Rules prescribe new Forms 92ZA and 92ZB, for use in cases where persons are sentenced by way of sections 12 and 13 of the Criminal Justice Act 1991 to a curfew order with an electronic monitoring requirement. Forms 92ZA and 92ZB are to be served on the person subject to the order and the officer responsible for electronically monitoring compliance with it respectively.

items.title: The Crown Court (Forms Amendment) Rules 1999
items.description: These Rules prescribe new forms for use in cases where persons are sentenced by way of sections 12 and 13 of the Criminal Justice Act 1991 to a curfew order with an electronic monitoring requirement. The forms are to be served on the person subject to the order and those responsible for electronically monitoring compliance with it respectively.

items.title: The Price Marking Order 1999
items.description: This Order is made under section 4 of the Prices Act 1974 and implements Directive 98/6/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council (OJ No. L180, 18.3.98 p. 27) on consumer protection in the indication of the prices of products offered to consumers.

items.title: The Portland Harbour Fishery (Variation) Order 1999
items.description: This Order varies the Portland Harbour Fishery Order 1989 by extending for ten years the period for which the right of several fishery in Portland Harbour is granted to Portland Oystermen Limited (“the Company”) (article 2).

items.title: The Avalon, Somerset, National Health Service Trust (Change of Name) Order 1999
items.description: This Order changes the name of an NHS trust established by Order under Part I of the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990 from the Avalon, Somerset, National Health Service Trust to the Somerset Partnership National Health Service and Social Care Trust, and makes consequential amendments to that Order and transitional provisions.

items.title: The A13 Trunk Road (Tower Hamlets) Red Route (Bus Priority) Traffic Order 1999
items.description: This version of the The A13 Trunk Road (Tower Hamlets) Red Route (Bus Priority) Traffic Order 1999 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Conservation of Seals (England) Order 1999
items.description: This Order prohibits with effect from 19th December 1999 the killing, injuring or taking of grey seals and common seals in the counties of England bordering the North Sea set out in article 3(a) of the Order and the adjacent territorial waters. The Conservation of Seals (England) Order 1996 (S.I. 1996/2905) ceases to have effect on that date. Any person who acts in contravention of this Order is guilty of an offence under section 3(2) of the Conservation of Seals Act 1970.

items.title: The Asylum Support (Interim Provisions) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations make provision requiring certain local authorities to provide support to asylum-seekers and their dependants who appear to be, or to be likely to become, destitute. This requirement will begin on 6th December 1999 and come to an end on 1st April 2002.

items.title: The Housing Accommodation (Persons subject to Immigration Control) (Amendment) (England) Order 1999
items.description: Section 9(1) of the Asylum and Immigration Act 1996 requires a local housing authority, so far as practicable, to secure that no tenancy of, or licence to occupy, housing accommodation provided under Part II of the Housing Act 1985 (provision of housing accommodation), other than accommodation allocated under Part VI of the Housing Act 1996 (allocation of housing accommodation), is granted to a person subject to immigration control unless he is of a class specified in an order made by the Secretary of State.

items.title: The Occupational Pension Schemes (Contracting-out) (Amount Required for Restoring State Scheme Rights) Amendment Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations amend the Occupational Pension Schemes (Contracting-out) (Amount Required for Restoring State Scheme Rights and Miscellaneous Amendment) Regulations 1998 and the Occupational Pension Schemes (Contracting-out) (Amount Required for Restoring State Scheme Rights and Miscellaneous Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1998 (“the principal Regulations”) which provide for the determination by the Commissioners of Inland Revenue of the amount required for restoring, to members of occupational pension schemes which wind up insolvent, certain rights under the State retirement pension scheme.

items.title: The Water and Sewerage Undertakers (Pipelaying and Other Works) (Code of Practice) Order 1999
items.description: This version of the The Water and Sewerage Undertakers (Pipelaying and Other Works) (Code of Practice) Order 1999 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The General Chiropractic Council (Professional Indemnity Insurance) Rules Order 1999
items.description: This Order, which is made under the Chiropractors Act 1994, approves Rules made by the General Chiropractic Council requiring registered chiropractors to take out professional indemnity insurance.

items.title: The Jobseeker’s Allowance Amendment (New Deal) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations amend the Jobseeker’s Allowance Regulations 1996 (S.I. 1996/207) (“the Jobseeker’s Allowance Regulations”).

items.title: The Financial Services Act 1986 (Exemption) Order 1999
items.description: This Order replaces the current exemption of the Commonwealth Development Corporation (to be renamed CDC Group plc) relieving that body of the need to be authorised for the purposes of the Financial Services Act 1986 (c. 60). This reflects the fact that the Commonwealth Development Corporation Act 1978 (c. 2) referred to in the current exemption is to be substantially repealed by the Commonwealth Development Corporation Act 1999 (c. 20). This Order comes into force at the time when CDC Group plc’s memorandum and articles of association are registered by the Registrar of Companies pursuant to section 4 of the 1999 Act.

items.title: The Immigration (Transit Visa) (Amendment) Order 1999
items.description: This Order amends the Immigration (Transit Visa) Order 1993 (as amended by the Immigration (Transit Visa) (Amendment) Order 1998, the Immigration (Transit Visa) (Amendment No. 2) Order 1998 and the Immigration (Transit Visa) (Amendment No. 3) Order 1998) by adding the Republic of Croatia to the list of countries or territories whose nationals or citizens need a visa for passing through the United Kingdom. It also revokes spent provisions.

items.title: The Jobseeker’s Allowance (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations amend the Jobseeker’s Allowance Regulations 1996.

items.title: The Water Appointment (Competition Commission) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations provide for the appointment of members to the Competition Commission, and for the procedure to be followed, in respect of references to the Commission made by the Director General of Water Services, under the Water Industry Act 1991, for the determination of matters arising under an instrument of appointment of a water or sewerage undertaker.

items.title: The Gas Act 1986 (Exemptions) (No. 2) Order 1999
items.description: Section 6A of the Gas Act 1986, as substituted by section 4 of the Gas Act 1995, provides for the granting by order of exemptions from the prohibition contained in section 5 of the 1986 Act from carrying on the conveyance, supply or shipping of gas without a licence. This Order contains an exemption in respect of the conveyance of gas through pipes which do not exceed 16.093 kilometres from the gas processing facility at the Lindholm Compressor Site, Vulcan Way, Bawtry Road, Hatfield Woodhouse, Doncaster DN7 6TE, to a pipe-line system operated by a public gas transporter (article 3). The exemption is subject to conditions relating to the provision of information to a public gas transporter to whose pipe-line system gas is conveyed in pursuance of the exemption, if directed by the Secretary of State, and to conditions relating to the provision of information to the Director General of Gas Supply (article 4).

items.title: The Crime (Sentences) Act 1997 (Commencement No. 3) Order 1999
items.description: This Order brings into force section 4 of the Crime (Sentences) Act 1997 (minimum of three years for third domestic burglary) on 1st December 1999. It also brings fully into force a number of related provisions in the 1997 Act.

items.title: The A13 Trunk Road (A112 Prince Regent Lane Junction Improvement, Trunk Road and Slip Roads) Order 1999
items.description: This version of the The A13 Trunk Road (A112 Prince Regent Lane Junction Improvement, Trunk Road and Slip Roads) Order 1999 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Legal Aid in Civil Proceedings (Remuneration) (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations make changes to the references, in the Legal Aid in Civil Proceedings (Remuneration) Regulations 1994, to the Rules of the Supreme Court and the County Court Rules, resulting from the revocation of those Rules, and their replacement, by the Civil Procedure Rules 1998. They also limit the amount which, under the Legal Aid in Civil Proceedings (Remuneration) Regulations 1994, can be allowed in a fast track claim in respect of advocate’s cost and the costs of a legal representative’s attendance at trial, to the fixed amounts prescribed by the Civil Procedure Rules.

items.title: The Greater London Magistrates' Courts Authority (Constitution) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations make provision for the membership, constitution and procedure of the Greater London Magistrates' Courts Authority. The Authority is to be established under section 30A(1) of the Justices of the Peace Act 1997, which is inserted into the 1997 Act by section 83(1) of the Access to Justice Act 1999. The Authority will be the magistrates' courts committee for Greater London when section 30A(2), which is also inserted into the Justices of the Peace Act 1997 by section 83(1) of the Access to Justice Act 1999, comes into force.

items.title: The A3 Trunk Road (Wandsworth) Red Route Experimental Traffic Order 1999
items.description: This version of the The A3 Trunk Road (Wandsworth) Red Route Experimental Traffic Order 1999 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Good Laboratory Practice Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations contain the legislative measures necessary for the implementation within the United Kingdom of Council Directive 87/18/EEC on the harmonisation of laws, regulations and administrative provisions relating to the application of the principles of good laboratory practice and the verification of their applications for tests on chemical substances, as amended by Commission Directive 1999/11/EC adapting to technical progress the principles of good laboratory practice as specified in Council Directive 87/18/EEC, and for the implementation of Council Directive 88/320/EEC on the inspection and verification of Good Laboratory Practice (GLP), as amended by Commission Directive 1999/12/EC adapting to technical progress for a second time the Annex to Council Directive 88/320/EEC.

items.title: The Motor Fuel (Composition and Content) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations implement Directive 98/70/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council of October 13 1998 relating to the quality of petrol and diesel fuels and amending Council Directive 93/12/EEC (“the Directive”). They revoke and replace the Motor Fuel (Composition and Content) Regulations 1994 (“the 1994 Regulations”).

items.title: The Social Security (Claims and Information) Regulations 1999
items.description: The Regulations contained in this Instrument are made either by virtue of, or in consequence of, provisions in the Welfare Reform and Pensions Act 1999 (c. 30) (“the 1999 Act”). This Instrument is made before the end of the period of 6 months beginning with the coming into force of those provisions; the regulations in it are therefore exempt from the requirement in section 172(1) of the Social Security Administration Act 1992 (c. 5) to refer proposals to make these Regulations to the Social Security Advisory Committee and are made without reference to that Committee.

items.title: The Social Security (Incapacity for Work) Miscellaneous Amendments Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Social Security (Incapacity for Work) (General) Regulations 1995 (S.I. 1995/311), in consequence of the coming into force of the Welfare Reform and Pensions Act 1999—

items.title: The Tax Credit (New Category of Child Care Provider) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations make a scheme for establishing a new category of persons whose charges for providing child care are to be taken into account for the purposes of determining the appropriate amount of working families' tax credit or disabled person’s tax credit. The persons whose charges will be taken into account for these purposes will be approved by an organisation that has been accredited by the Secretary of State.

items.title: The Docklands Light Railway (Miscellaneous Closure Exemptions) Order 1999
items.description: This Order exempts certain stretches of railway line and Pudding Mill Lane Station, which form part of the Docklands Light Railway, from the closure provisions contained in sections 39 and 41 of the Railways Act 1993 (closure of operational passenger networks and facilities used in connection with passenger services).

items.title: The Docklands Light Railway (Lewisham Extension) (Exemptions) Order 1999
items.description: This Order provides for the grant of exemptions from the franchising and closure provisions of the Railways Act 1993 (“the 1993 Act”) to Docklands Light Railway Limited (“DLR”) for the stretch of railway constructed from Lewisham Station to a point just north of Mudchute Station.

items.title: The Railways (Alternative Closure Procedure) (Amendment) Order 1999
items.description: This Order amends the Railway (Alternative Closure Procedure) Order 1994 (“the 1994 Order”) by adding the railway passenger services operated on the network constructed pursuant to powers conferred by the London Docklands Railway (Lewisham) Act 1993 to the services designated in 1994 Order as railway passenger services in relation to which Schedule 5 of the Railways Act 1993 is to have effect. Schedule 5 contains provisions relating to the discontinuance of railway passenger services.

items.title: The Value Added Tax (Amendment) (No. 4) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 1st January 2000, further amend the Value Added Tax Regulations 1995 (S.I. 1995/2518) (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Value Added Tax (Importation of Investment Gold) Relief Order 1999
items.description: This Order, which comes into force on 1st January 2000, implements the exemption for importations of investment gold as required by Council Directive 98/80/EC, 12th October 1998—Special scheme for investment gold—amending Council Directive 77/388/EEC.

items.title: The Value Added Tax (Investment Gold) Order 1999
items.description: This Order, which comes into force on 1st January 2000, implements the exemption for investment gold, the option to tax and the “reverse charge” provisions of Council Directive 98/80/EC, 12th October 1998—Special scheme for investment gold—amending Council Directive 77/388/EEC.

items.title: The Value Added Tax (Terminal Markets) Order 1999
items.description: This Order, which comes into force on 1st January 2000, further amends the Value Added Tax (Terminal Markets) Order 1973 (S.I. 1973/173) (“the principal Order”) and extends its provisions in respect of certain supplies involving “investment gold” as defined in a new article 2(5) of the principal Order. This Order affects only certain supplies involving members of the London Bullion Market Association (LBMA).

items.title: The Value Added Tax (Input Tax) (Amendment) (No. 2) Order 1999
items.description: This Order, which comes into force on 1st January 2000, amends the Value Added Tax (Input Tax) Order 1992 (S.I. 1992/3222). The Order aligns the definition of “collectors' items” and “works of art” with section 21 of the Value Added Tax Act 1994 (c. 23) which was amended by section 12 of the Finance Act 1999 (c. 16). In addition it excludes from the definition of “collectors' items” investment gold coins, so implementing the requirement of Council Directive 98/80/EC, 12th October 1998—Special scheme for investment gold—amending Council Directive EC 77/388/EEC.

items.title: The Value Added Tax (Treatment of Transactions) (Amendment) Order 1999
items.description: This Order, which comes into force on 1st January 2000, amends the Value Added Tax (Treatment of Transactions) Order 1995 (S.I. 1995/958). This Order aligns the definition of “work of art” with section 21 of the Value Added Tax Act 1994 (c. 23), which was amended by section 12 of the Finance Act 1999 (c. 16).

items.title: The Value Added Tax (Special Provisions) (Amendment) (No. 2) Order 1999
items.description: This Order, which comes into force on 1st January 2000, amends the Value Added Tax (Special Provisions) Order 1995 (S.I. 1995/1268) (“the principal Order”). The Order aligns the definition of “collectors' items” and “works of art” with section 21 of the Value Added Tax Act 1994 (c. 23), which was amended by section 12 of the Finance Act 1999 (c. 16). This Order excludes from the definition of “collectors' items” investment gold coins, so implementing the requirement of Council Directive 98/80/EC, 12th October 1998—Special scheme for investment gold—amending Council Directive EC 77/388/EEC.

items.title: The Value Added Tax (Input Tax) (Specified Supplies) Order 1999
items.description: This Order, which comes into force on 1st January 2000, replaces the Value Added Tax (Input Tax) (Specified Supplies) Order 1992 (S.I. 1992/3123). The Order is made under powers laid to the Treasury under Section 26(2)(c) of the Value Added Tax Act 1994. This 1999 Order updates the 1992 Order (which referred to the Value Added Tax Act 1983) and introduces new specified supplies in respect of investment gold.

items.title: The Free Zone (Liverpool) Designation (Variation) Order 1999
items.description: This Order varies the area designated a free zone by the Free Zone (Liverpool) Designation Order 1991 by increasing it from 281 hectares to 337.01 hectares, 77.87 hectares of which are located within the Metropolitan Borough of Wirrall and 259.14 hectares of which are located within the Metropolitan Borough of Sefton.

items.title: The Local Authorities (Calculation of Council Tax Base) (Amendment) (England) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations amend the Local Authorities (Calculation of Council Tax Base) Regulations 1992 by providing for the reduction in tax yield resulting from the Council Tax (Reductions for Disabilities) (Amendment) Regulations 1999. These latter Regulations provide for dwellings in valuation band A to qualify for a disability reduction with effect from 1st April 2000.

items.title: The Homelessness (Asylum-Seekers) (Interim Period) (England) Order 1999
items.description: This Order makes modifications to Part VII (homelessness) of the Housing Act 1996 (“the 1996 Act”) which apply only in relation to asylum-seekers who are eligible for housing assistance under that Part as a result of regulations made under section 185(2) of that Act and who are not made ineligible by section 186 (or any other provision) of that Act (article 2).

items.title: The Courses for Drink-Drive Offenders (Experimental Period) (Termination of Restrictions) Order 1999
items.description: Section 34A of the Road Traffic Offenders Act 1988 (which was inserted by section 30 of the Road Traffic Act 1991) empowers a court to make an order requiring a person who has been convicted of a drink-drive offence to attend a rehabilitation course. Section 31(1) of the 1991 Act, as subsequently modified by order, restricts the exercise of this power to an experimental period terminating at the end of 1999, and during that period to the trial of certain offences in designated courts, unless those restrictions are removed by an order made under subsection (2). This Order removes the restrictions.

items.title: The European Convention on Cinematographic Co-production (Amendment) Order 1999
items.description: The European Convention on Cinematographic Co-production Order 1994 (“the Principal Order”) provides that films made in accordance with the European Convention on Cinematographic Co-production (Cm 2495) are to be treated as British films for the purposes of Schedule 1 to the Films Act 1985. For the Convention to apply where there are only two co-producers one must be established in the United Kingdom and the other in one of the countries set out in the Schedule to the Principal Order. For the Convention to apply where there are three or more co-producers one must be established in the United Kingdom and at least two others in different countries set out in the Schedule to the Principal Order.

items.title: The Consular Fees (No. 2) Order 1999
items.description: This Order revokes and replaces the Consular Fees Order 1999.

items.title: The Afghanistan (United Nations Sanctions) Order 1999
items.description: This Order, made under the United Nations Act 1946, imposes restrictions pursuant to a decision of the Security Council of the United Nations in resolution 1267 of 15th October 1999 by prohibiting any aircraft from taking off from or landing in the United Kingdom, the Channel Islands or the Isle of Man, if it is owned, leased or operated by or on behalf of the Taliban. It also freezes assets of the Taliban or any undertaking owned or controlled by the Taliban.

items.title: The Afghanistan (United Nations Sanctions) (Channel Islands) Order 1999
items.description: This Order, made under the United Nations Act 1946, gives effect in the Channel Islands to the imposition of restrictions pursuant to a decision of the Security Council of the United Nations in Resolution No. 1267 of 15th October 1999 on certain actions making available or otherwise transferring funds and and financial resources to or for the benefit of the Taliban.

items.title: The Afghanistan (United Nations Sanctions) (Isle of Man) Order 1999
items.description: This Order, made under the United Nations Act 1946, gives effect in the Isle of Man to the imposition of restrictions pursuant to a decision of the Security Council of the United Nations in Resolution No. 1267 of 15th October 1999 on certain actions making available or otherwise transferring funds and financial resources to or for the benefit of the Taliban.

items.title: The Afghanistan (United Nations Sanctions) (Overseas Territories) Order 1999
items.description: This Order, made under the United Nations Act 1946, imposes restrictions pursuant to a decision of the Security Council of the United Nations in resolution 1267 of 15th October 1999 by prohibiting any aircraft from taking off from or landing in the territories specified in Schedule 2 if it is owned, leased or operated by or on behalf of the Taliban. It also freezes assets of the Taliban or any undertaking owned or controlled by the Taliban.

items.title: The Chartered Institute of Patent Agents Order 1999
items.description: This Order designates the Chartered Institute of Patent Agents as an authorised body for the purposes of sections 27 and 28 of the Courts and Legal Services Act 1990, under which authorised bodies may grant, respectively, rights of audience and rights to conduct litigation.

items.title: The Maximum Number of Judges Order 1999
items.description: This Order in Council amends section 4(1)(e) of the Supreme Court Act 1981 by increasing the maximum number of puisne judges of the High Court from 98 to 106.

items.title: The Exempt Charities Order 1999
items.description: This Order declares South Bank University to be an exempt charity within the meaning of the Charities Act 1993 with effect from 1st March 2000. An exempt charity is not required to be registered with the Charity Commissioners and the Commissioners cannot exercise any powers under the Act in relation to an exempt charity except at the charity’s request.

items.title: The Transfer of Functions (Agriculture and Food) Order 1999
items.description: This Order transfers the roles of the Secretary of State for Scotland, the Secretary of State for Wales and the Secretary of State in the exercise of certain functions relating to agriculture and food to the Minister of Agriculture, Fisheries and Food. Its purpose is to transfer functions which are in practice exercised by the Secretary of State for Scotland or the Secretary of State for Wales and which have become unnecessary following the devolution of functions to the Scottish Parliament and Executive and to the National Assembly for Wales.

items.title: The Transfer of Functions (Medicines and Poisons) Order 1999
items.description: This Order in Council, made under the Ministers of the Crown Act 1975, transfers the functions of the Secretaries of State concerned with health in Scotland and in Wales under the Medicines Act 1968 and the Medicines Act 1971 to the Secretary of State for Health (article 2(1)). It also transfers the functions under those Acts of the Secretary of State concerned with agriculture in Scotland and of the Secretary of State or the Secretary of State for Wales exercisable by virtue of the Transfer of Functions (Wales) (No. 1) Order 1978 to the Minister of Agriculture, Fisheries and Food (article 2(2)). The functions of the Secretary of State for Scotland and the Secretary of State for Wales under the Marketing Authorisations for Veterinary Medicinal Products Regulations 1994 and the Registration of Homoeopathic Veterinary Medicinal Products Regulations 1997 are transferred to the Minister of Agriculture, Fisheries and Food (article 2(3)). In addition the Order transfers the function of the Secretary of State for Scotland of appointing a member of the Poisons Board under Schedule 1 to the Poisons Act 1972 to the Secretary of State for the Home Department (article 2(4)).

items.title: The Transfer of Functions (Road Traffic) Order 1999
items.description: This Order provides for the transfer of certain functions under the Road Traffic Regulation Act 1984 (“the 1984 Act”) and the Road Traffic Act 1988 (“the 1988 Act”).

items.title: The Northern Ireland Assembly Commission (Crown Status) Order 1999
items.description: This Order provides for the Northern Ireland Assembly Commission, established by section 40 of the Northern Ireland Act 1998 (c. 47), to be treated as a Crown Body for the purposes of—

items.title: The Parliamentary Copyright (Northern Ireland Assembly) Order 1999
items.description: This Order modifies the provisions of section 165 of the Copyright, Designs and Patents Act 1988 (Parliamentary copyright), so that its provisions apply with modifications to works made by or under the direction or control of the Northern Ireland Assembly. By virtue of subsection (7) of section 165, the provisions of that section apply not only in respect of works made by or under the direction or control of the House of Commons or the House of Lords, but also subject to any exceptions or modifications specified by Order in Council, to works made by or under the direction or control of any other legislative body of a country to which Part I of the 1988 Act applies. The application of Part I extends to Northern Ireland.

items.title: The Local Government Act 1988 (Defined Activities) (Exemption) (Stoke on Trent City Council) Order 1999
items.description: Under Part I (competition) of the Local Government Act 1988 work falling within certain defined activities may be carried out by local authorities only if particular conditions are fulfilled.

items.title: The European Specialist Medical Qualifications Amendment (No. 2) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations amend the European Specialist Medical Qualifications Order 1995 (“the 1995 Order"), which implemented European obligations contained principally in Council Directive 93/16/EEC (OJ No. L165, 7.7.93, p. 1) relating to the training of specialist doctors and mutual recognition of their qualifications (“the Directive"). The need for changes to the 1995 Order arises from amendments to the Directive made by Commission Directive 99/46/EC (OJ No. L139, 2.6.99, p. 25).

items.title: The Curfew Order (Responsible Officer) Order 1999
items.description: This Order specifies the descriptions of persons who are to be made responsible, by a curfew order relating to a place of curfew in any part of England and Wales, for monitoring an offender’s whereabouts during the curfew periods.

items.title: The Social Security (New Deal Pilot) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations replace the pilot scheme established by the Social Security (New Deal Pilot) Regulations 1998 (S.I. 1998/2825), which were amended by the Social Security (New Deal Pilot) Amendment Regulations 1999 (S.I. 1999/976), with a further pilot scheme. The scheme relates to persons who claim a jobseeker’s allowance and who fulfil the criteria in regulation 3, 4 or 5 as to age, the period over which they have been receiving benefit and the location of appropriate offices at which they are claiming benefit, as identified in one of the Schedules.

items.title: The Non-Domestic Rating (Rural Settlements) (England) Order 1999
items.description: Schedule 1 to the Local Government and Rating Act 1997 amends the Local Government Finance Act 1988 to make provision for mandatory relief from non-domestic rates for certain general stores and post offices, and discretionary relief for hereditaments used for purpose beneficial to the local community, in certain rural settlements in areas designated as rural areas by order of the Secretary of State. The rural settlement are to be identified by reference to lists drawn up by billing authorities.

items.title: The Occupational Pensions (Revaluation) Order 1999
items.description: This Order is made, as required by paragraph 2(1) of Schedule 3 to the Pension Schemes Act 1993, in the revaluation year beginning on 1st January 1999. The revaluation percentages specified are relevant to the revaluation of benefits under occupational pension schemes, as required by section 84 of, and Schedule 3 to, that Act.

items.title: The Habitat (Water Fringe) (Amendment) Regulations 1999 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Habitat (Water Fringe) Regulations 1994 (S.I. 1994/1291) (as amended) (“the principal Regulations”) which implement in part a zonal programme approved by the European Commission under article 7 of Council Regulation (EEC) No. 2078/92 (OJ No. L215, 30.7.92, p.85) (“the agri-environment Regulation”) on agricultural methods compatible with the requirements of the protection of the environment and the maintenance of the countryside. The agri-environment Regulation was repealed by Article 55(1) of Council Regulation (EC) No. 1257/99 (OJ No. L160, 26.6.99, p.80) but, by virtue of Article 55(3) of the latter Regulation, the agri-environment Regulation continues to apply to actions approved by the Commission before 1st January 2000.

items.title: The Habitat (Salt-Marsh) (Amendment) Regulations 1999 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Habitat (Salt-Marsh) Regulations 1994 (S.I.1994/1293) (as amended) (“the principal Regulations”) which implement in part a zonal programme approved by the European Commission under article 7 of Council Regulation (EEC) No. 2078/92 (OJ No. L215, 30.7.92, p.85) (“the agri-environment Regulation”) on agricultural methods compatible with the requirements of the protection of the environment and the maintenance of the countryside. The agri-environment Regulation was repealed by Article 55(1) of Council Regulation (EC) No. 1257/99 (OJ No. L160, 26.6.99, p.80) but, by virtue of Article 55(3) of the latter Regulation, it continues to apply to actions approved by the Commission before 1st January 2000.

items.title: The Wireless Telegraphy (Third Generation Licences) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations make provision for a procedure for the grant of five wireless telegraphy licences authorising the use of apparatus at specified frequencies for the provision of Third Generation, a mobile communications system supporting services beyond the capability of the current Global Standard for Mobile Communications system.

items.title: The Equal Opportunities (Employment Legislation) (Territorial Limits) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations, which are made under section 2(2) of the European Communities Act 1972, implement (in Great Britain) Article 3(1)(g) of Directive 96/71/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council of 16th December 1996 concerning the posting of workers in the framework of the provision of services (OJ No. L018, 21. 1.97, p. 1-6).

items.title: The Croydon Community National Health Service Trust (Change of Name) Order 1999
items.description: This Order changes the name of an NHS trust established by Order under Part I of the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990 from the Croydon Community National Health Service Trust to the Croydon and Surrey Downs Community National Health Service Trust, and makes consequential amendments to that Order and transitional provisions.

items.title: The Chemicals (Hazard Information and Packaging for Supply) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations amend the Chemicals (Hazard Information and Packaging for Supply) Regulations 1994 by introducing new editions of the approved classification and labelling guide (regulation 3) and the approved supply list (regulation 4) thereby implementing Article 1 of Commission Directive 98/98/EC (OJ No. L355, 30.12.98, p. 1) adapting to technical progress for the 25th time Council Directive 67/548/EEC (OJ No. L196, 16.8.67, p. 1) on the approximation of the laws, regulations and administrative provisions relating to the classification, packaging and labelling of dangerous substances.

items.title: The Yugoslavia (Prohibition of Flights) (No. 2) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations specify the sanctions that may be imposed where the following provisions of Council Regulation (EC) No. 2151/1999 imposing a ban on flights between the European Community and the Federal Republic of Yugoslavia other than authorised flights serving the Republic of Montenegro or the Province of Kosovo. Those provisions are those set out—

items.title: The Consumer Credit (Total Charge for Credit, Agreements and Advertisements) (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations amend the Consumer Credit (Total Charge for Credit) Regulations 1980 (“the 1980 Regulations"), the Consumer Credit (Agreements) Regulations 1983 (“the 1983 Regulations") and the Consumer Credit (Advertisements) Regulations 1989 (“the 1989 Regulations").

items.title: The Social Security Act 1998 (Commencement No. 12 and Consequential and Transitional Provisions) Order 1999
items.description: This Order provides for the coming into force on 29th November 1999 of further provisions of the Social Security Act 1998 (“the Act") introducing new arrangements for decision-making in relation to income support, the social fund, child’s special allowance and the recovery of benefits from compensation.

items.title: The National Health Service (Choice of Medical Practitioner) Amendment Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (Choice of Medical Practitioner) Regulations 1998, which relate to the right of persons to choose the doctor from whom they wish to receive primary medical services.

items.title: The Telecommunications (Appeals) Regulations 1999 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations implement provisions in Directive 97/13/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council on a common framework for general authorisations and individual licences in the field of telecommunications services and Directive 97/51/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council amending Council Directives 90/387/EEC and 92/44/EEC for the purposes of adaptation to a competitive environment in telecommunications, which require Member States to provide an appeals procedure in relation to certain decisions of national regulatory authorities in the field of telecommunications.

items.title: The Education (School Day and School Year) (England) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations revoke and replace regulation 10 of the Education (Schools and Further Education) Regulations 1981 in relation to England only.

items.title: The Novel Foods and Novel Food Ingredients (Amendment) (England) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations amend the Novel Foods and Novel Food Ingredients Regulations 1997 in relation to England by applying (with modifications) provisions of section 25 of the Food Safety Act 1990 to information submitted as part of a request made by reference to Article 4.1 of Regulation (EC) No. 258/97 of the European Parliament and of the Council concerning novel foods and novel food ingredients (OJ No. L43, 14.2.97, p.1). Article 4.1 requires the person responsible for placing such foods or ingredients on the market to submit a request for assessment to the relevant EC member State, and the provisions of the 1990 Act so applied call for information covered by them to be protected from disclosure save as specified in the section.

items.title: The Distress for Rent (Amendment) (No. 3) Rules 1999
items.description: These Rules are consequential on the closure, by virtue of the Civil Courts (Amendment) (No. 3) Order 1999, of the county courts at Bridgwater, Grays Thurrock and Great Yarmouth, and removes references to them from the list of county courts in Appendix 3 to the Distress for Rent Rules 1988.

items.title: The Civil Courts (Amendment) (No. 3) Order 1999
items.description: This version of the The Civil Courts (Amendment) (No. 3) Order 1999 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Tax Credits (New Deal Pilot Consequential Amendments) (Northern Ireland) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations make amendments to the Family Credit (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987 (S.R. 1987 No. 463) and the Disability Working Allowance (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1992 (S.R. 1992 No. 78), which are consequential on the establishment of a pilot scheme known as “the intensive activity period of the New Deal pilot for 25 plus” (“the pilot scheme”) by the Social Security (New Deal Pilot) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1999 (S.R. 1999 No. 474). Family credit and disability working allowance became known as working families' tax credit and disabled person’s tax credit respectively (collectively “tax credit”) on 5th October 1999. The principal effects of the amendments are to enable participants in the pilot scheme to continue to claim tax credits.

items.title: The General Medical Council (Registration (Fees) (Amendment) Regulations) Order of Council 1999
items.description: This version of the The General Medical Council (Registration (Fees) (Amendment) Regulations) Order of Council 1999 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Immigration and Asylum Act 1999 (Commencement No. 1) Order 1999
items.description: This Order brings into force certain provisions of the Immigration and Asylum Act 1999 (the Act).

items.title: The Sale of Dogs (Identification Tag) Regulations 1999
