items.description: The Breeding and Sale of Dogs (Welfare) Act 1999 creates certain offences in relation to the sale of a dog which is not wearing a collar with an identifying tag or badge. Section 8(5) of that Act defines “identifying tag or badge” as a tag or badge which clearly displays information indicating the licensed breeding establishment at which it was born and any other information required by regulations.

items.title: The Breeding of Dogs (Licensing Records) Regulations 1999
items.description: The Breeding of Dogs Act 1973, as amended by the Breeding and Sale of Dogs (Welfare) Act 1999, requires the local authority to have regard to certain matters in determining whether to grant a licence for the keeping of a breeding establishment and about which conditions must be included in the licence. One such matter is that accurate records should be kept at the premises and made available for inspection (section 1(4)(i) of the 1973 Act).

items.title: The Dangerous Substances and Preparations (Safety) (Consolidation) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations amend the Dangerous Substances and Preparations (Safety) (Consolidation) Regulations 1994 (“the principal Regulations") as amended by the Dangerous Substances and Preparations (Safety) (Consolidation) (Amendment) Regulations 1996 and the Dangerous Substances and Preparations (Safety) (Consolidation) (Amendment) Regulations 1999. They partially implement Article 1(2) of Commission Directive 97/56/EC (O.J. No. L333, 4.12.1997, p.1), which amended for the sixteenth time Council Directive 76/769/EEC. These Regulations combined with the Chemicals (Hazard Information and Packaging for Supply) (Amendment) (No. 3) Regulations 1999 (S.I. 3194) fully implement Article 1(2) of Directive 97/56/EC.

items.title: The Chemicals (Hazard Information and Packaging for Supply) (Amendment) (No. 3) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations amend the Chemicals (Hazard Information and Packaging for Supply) Regulations 1994 (S.I. 1994/3247) (“the principal Regulations") as amended by the Chemicals (Hazard Information and Packaging for Supply) (Amendment) Regulations 1996 (S.I. 1996/1092). They partially implement Article 1(2) of Commission Directive 97/56/EC (O.J. No. L333, 4.12.1997, p.1) which amended for the sixteenth time Council Directive 76/769/EEC. These Regulations combined with the Dangerous Substances and Preparations (Safety) (Consolidation) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1999 (S.I. 1999/3193) fully implement Article 1(2) of Directive 97/56/EC.

items.title: The Design Right (Proceedings before Comptroller) (Amendment) Rules 1999
items.description: This version of the The Design Right (Proceedings before Comptroller) (Amendment) Rules 1999 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Registered Designs (Amendment) Rules 1999
items.description: This version of the The Registered Designs (Amendment) Rules 1999 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Patents (Amendment) (No. 2) Rules 1999
items.description: These Rules further amend the Patents Rules 1995 (S.I. 1995/2093) (which were previously amended by the Patents (Amendment) Rules 1999 (S.I. 1999/1092) and the Patents and Trade Marks (World Trade Organisation) Regulations 1999 (S.I. 1999/1899)) (“the Patents Rules”). In addition to minor and drafting amendments, these Rules—

items.title: The Personal and Occupational Pension Schemes (Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations amend a number of sets of regulations related to the Pension Schemes Act 1993 and the Pensions Act 1995.

items.title: The Betting and Gaming Duties Act 1981 (Bingo Prize Limit) Order 1999
items.description: This Order, which comes into force on 22nd December 1999, increases the money prize limit for bingo played on a small scale by way of commercial amusement to qualify for exemption from bingo duty. It increases the maximum amount permitted to be distributed as a money prize from £5 to £15.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Registration of Ships, and Tonnage) (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Merchant Shipping (Registration of Ships) Regulations 1993 (“the 1993 Regulations”). Their primary purpose is to require that the engine power recorded for fishing vessels is the maximum continuous engine power, or that power as permanently de-rated, as required by Council Regulation (EEC) No. 2930/86 of 22nd September 1986 (defining characteristics for fishing vessels) and Commission Regulation (EEC) No. 1381/87 of 20th May 1987 (establishing detailed rules concerning the marking and documentation of fishing vessels).

items.title: The Northern Ireland Act 1998 (Appointed Day) Order 1999
items.description: This Order appoints 2nd December 1999 as the day for the commencement of Parts II and III of the Northern Ireland Act 1998. Part II, which deals with the legislative powers of the Northern Ireland Assembly, and Part III, which deals with the Executive Authorities, implement, in those regards, the Belfast Agreement.

items.title: The Northern Ireland Act 1998 (Commencement No. 5) Order 1999
items.description: This Order brings into force on 2nd December 1999, provisions of the Northern Ireland Act 1998. By virtue of the Northern Ireland Act 1998 (Appointed Day) Order 1999, that day is the appointed day under section 3 of that Act when Parts II and III of the Act, which make provision for the legislative powers and executive authorities of the Northern Ireland Assembly, are commenced. This Order brings into force other provisions of the Act in connection with the devolution of power to the Assembly.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Radio) (Fishing Vessels) Regulations 1999
items.description: (This note does not form part of the Regulations)

items.title: The Education (Education Standards Etc. Grants) (England) Regulations 1999 (Amendment No. 2) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Education (Education Standards Etc. Grants) (England) Regulations 1999. They add seven new purposes for or in connection with which grants are payable. The new purposes relate to support for parent governor representatives on local authority committees dealing with education, the improvement of non-teaching areas of schools used by teachers, increased administrative support for small schools, support for city learning centres, support for Phase 1 of the DomEx project, research scholarships relating to the raising of standards and support for prospective non-statutory education action zones.

items.title: The Tax Credits (Payment by Employers) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations, which are made under section 6 of the Tax Credits Act 1999 and come into force on 6th April 2000, specify the circumstances in which certain employers shall make payments of working families' tax credit and disabled person’s tax credit (“tax credit”) to certain of their employees, and also provide the machinery for such payments and for recovery of sums provided by the Commissioners of Inland Revenue (“the Board”) for the purpose of such payments.

items.title: The Petty Sessions Areas (Amendment) Order 1999
items.description: This Order amends the list of petty sessions areas in the Petty Sessions Areas Order 1999 (which was the list of petty sessions areas in existence immediately before 27th September 1999) to take account of changes that were effected by Orders made before 27th September 1999, but coming into force on 1st January 2000.

items.title: The Contaminants in Food (Amendment) (England and Wales) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations, which apply to England and Wales, amend the Contaminants in Food Regulations 1997 by bringing up to date a reference to Commission Regulation (EC) No. 194/97 (as amended) setting maximum levels for certain contaminants in foodstuffs, so as to cover its further amendment by Commission Regulation (EC) No. 1566/1999 which altered the date by which the maximum aflatoxin limits laid down for nuts and dried fruit have to be reconsidered by the Standing Committee on Foodstuffs from 1st July 1999 to 1st July 2001 and similarly altered the date from which specified maximum limits will apply to cereals.

items.title: The A41 Trunk Road (Barnet) Red Route Traffic Order 1997 Variation Order 1999
items.description: This version of the The A41 Trunk Road (Barnet) Red Route Traffic Order 1997 Variation Order 1999 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A13 By-pass Trunk Road (Barking and Dagenham and Havering) Red Route (Clearway) Traffic Order 1999
items.description: This Order is based on a comprehensive model Order produced by the Traffic Director for London to assist understanding of the regulations, and to assist future consolidation of Orders relating to priority routes.

items.title: The A205 Trunk Road (Lewisham) Red Route Traffic Order 1998 Variation Order 1999
items.description: This version of the The A205 Trunk Road (Lewisham) Red Route Traffic Order 1998 Variation Order 1999 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Motor Vehicles (Approval) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Motor Vehicles (Approval) Regulations 1996. Schedule 1 is amended by the omission of paragraph 1(1)(b), thus removing the limitation on the total number of vehicles which can meet the requirements of paragraph 1 within a period of 5 calendar years, and by the insertion of paragraph 1B which defines a new vehicle class consisting of vehicles to which a new vehicle identification number is assigned by the Secretary of State under regulation 8. Consequential amendments are made to other provisions of the 1996 Regulations.

items.title: The Civil Aviation (Navigation Services Charges) (Fourth Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations amend the Civil Aviation (Navigation Services Charges) Regulations 1998 (“the principal Regulations”) as follows:

items.title: The Bank of England (Limit on Fiduciary Note Issue) Order 1999
items.description: Section 2 of the Currency Act 1983 provides that the amount of the Bank of England’s fiduciary note issue is not to exceed £13,500 million or the limit from time to time specified in a direction given by the Treasury. In the normal course, the limit specified in any such direction is not to exceed by more than 25 per cent the limit which was in force at the beginning of the period of two years ending with the day on which the direction is given. By this Order, the Treasury direct that the restriction on the size of any increase in the limit is not to apply for the period of six months beginning with the day on which the Order is made.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Sala Limited) Order 1999
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Sala Limited a licence on 7th October 1999 under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in the licence. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 7th December 1999. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Sala Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force on 5th January 2000.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Thus Limited) Order 1999
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Thus Limited a licence on 7th October 1999 under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in the licence. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 7th December 1999. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Thus Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force on 5th January 2000.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Metromedia Fiber Network UK Limited) Order 1999
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Metromedia Fiber Network UK Limited a licence on 7th October 1999 under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in the licence. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 7th December 1999. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Metromedia Fiber Network UK Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force on 5th January 2000.

items.title: The Ionising Radiations Regulations 1999 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations supersede and consolidate the Ionising Radiations Regulations 1985 and the Ionising Radiations (Outside Workers) Regulations 1993.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Rateflame Limited) Order 1999
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Rateflame Limited a licence on 1st June 1999 under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in the licence. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 7th December 1999. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Rateflame Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force on 5th January 2000.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Easynet Group plc) Order 1999
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Easynet Group plc a licence on 1st June 1999 under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in the licence. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 7th December 1999. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Easynet Group plc will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force on 5th January 2000.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Drive Memory Limited) Order 1999
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Drive Memory Limited a licence on 15th January 1999 under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in the licence. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 7th December 1999. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as a public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Drive Memory Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force on 5th January 2000.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Netkonect Communications Limited) Order 1999
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Netkonect Communications Limited a licence on 1st June 1999 under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in the licence. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 7th December 1999. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Netkonect Communications Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force on 5th January 2000.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (World-Link Inc) Order 1999
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to World-Link Inc a licence on 15th June 1999 under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in the licence. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 7th December 1999. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, World-Link Inc will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force on 5th January 2000.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (AT & T-Unisource Communications Services (UK) Limited) Order 1999
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to AT & T-Unisource Communications Services (UK) Limited a licence on 15th June 1999 under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in the licence. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 7th December 1999. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, AT & T-Unisource Communications Services (UK) Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force on 5th January 2000.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Flag Telecom Ireland Limited) Order 1999
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Flag Telecom Ireland Limited a licence on 8th July 1999 under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in the licence. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 7th December 1999. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Flag Telecom Ireland Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force on 5th January 2000.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (VIA Net Works UK Ltd) Order 1999
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to VIA Net Works UK Ltd a licence on 25th June 1999 under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in the licence. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 7th December 1999. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, VIA Net Works UK Ltd will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force on 5th January 2000.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (iaxis Limited) Order 1999
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to iaxis Limited a licence on 25th June 1999 under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in the licence. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 7th December 1999. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, iaxis Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force on 5th January 2000.

items.title: The Management of Health and Safety at Work Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations re-enact the Management of Health and Safety at Work Regulations 1992, with the following modifications—

items.title: The Wireless Telegraphy (Licence Charges) (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations substitute corrected National Grid References in Schedules 3, 4 and 5 to the Wireless Telegraphy (Licence Charges) Regulations 1999 (S.I. 1999/1774; “the 1999 Regulations”) (regulation 2(a), (c) and (d)). Such National Grid References are used for setting fees in relation to the Point to Point Fixed Links Licence, the On-Site Private Business Radio Licence and the Wide Area Private Business Radio Licence respectively.

items.title: The Environmental Protection (Controls on Injurious Substances) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations, which apply to Great Britain, give effect in part to European Parliament and Council Directive 94/60/EC amending for the fourteenth time Directive 76/769/EEC (OJ No. L262, 27.9.1976, p.201) on the approximation of the laws, regulations and administrative provisions of the Member States relating to restriction on the marketing and use of certain dangerous substances and preparations.

items.title: The London Cab (No. 2) Order 1999
items.description: This Order makes special provision for the extra charges payable for the hiring of a motor cab licensed in the Metropolitan Police District and the City of London in respect of all journeys beginning and ending there.

items.title: The Local Government (Best Value) Performance Plans and Reviews Order 1999
items.description: This Order specifies the elements which best value authorities must include in their performance plans and reviews prepared pursuant to the provisions of sections 5 and 6 of Part I of the Local Government Act 1999 (Best Value).

items.title: The Severn Bridges Tolls Order 1999
items.description: Section 9(2)(b) of the Severn Bridges Act 1992 (“the Act”) provides for the tolls for the use of the bridges to be fixed by annual orders made in December of each year. This Order fixes the tolls payable for use of the Severn Bridge and the Second Severn Crossing during the year 2000.

items.title: The A1 Trunk Road (Haringey) Red Route(Bus Priority) Experimental Traffic Order 1999
items.description: This version of the The A1 Trunk Road (Haringey) Red Route(Bus Priority) Experimental Traffic Order 1999 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Commonwealth Development Corporation Act 1999 (Appointed Day) Order 1999
items.description: This Order appoints 8th December 1999 as the day for registration pursuant to section 3(3) of the Commonwealth Development Corporation Act 1999. On the appointed day, the registrar of companies in England and Wales comes under a duty, by virtue of section 4 of that Act, to register the Commonwealth Development Corporation’s memorandum and articles of association. On registration, pursuant to section 5 of that Act, the Companies Act 1985 (1985 c. 6) and other legislation relating to companies will apply to the Commonwealth Development Corporation, with the modifications referred to in that section, and the Commonwealth Development Corporation Act 1978 (1978 c. 2) shall cease to have effect, subject to the savings, etc. referred to in that section.

items.title: The Local Government Pension Scheme (Management and Investment of Funds) (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These regulations amend the Local Government Pension Scheme (Management and Investment of Funds) Regulations 1998.

items.title: The National Health Service (General Medical Services) Amendment Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations further amend the National Health Service (General Medical Services) Regulations 1992 (“the 1992 Regulations”), which regulate the terms on which general medical services are provided under the National Health Service Act 1977.

items.title: The Civil Aviation (Route Charges for Navigation Services) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations revoke and replace the Civil Aviation (Route Charges for Navigation Services) Regulations 1997 (S.I. 1997/2920), as amended.

items.title: The Stamp Duty and Stamp Duty Reserve Tax (Open-ended Investment Companies) (Amendment No. 2) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations amend the Stamp Duty and Stamp Duty Reserve Tax (Open-ended Investment Companies) Regulations 1997 (S.I. 1997/1156) (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Stamp Duty and Stamp Duty Reserve Tax (Investment Exchanges and Clearing Houses) (OM London Exchange Limited) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations are made under sections 116 and 117 of the Finance Act 1991 (c. 31). Section 116 provides that a charge to stamp duty may be treated as not arising or as reduced in prescribed circumstances, and section 117 make similar provision with regard to stamp duty reserve tax.

items.title: The Distraint by Collectors (Fees, Costs and Charges) (Stamp Duty Penalties) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations are made under section 61(6) of the Taxes Management Act 1970 which applies in relation to stamp duty penalties by virtue of the Stamp Duty (Collection and Recovery of Penalties) Regulations 1999 (S.I. 1999/2537). The Regulations make provisions for fees, costs and charges where a collector of taxes distrains upon the goods and chattels of a person who is in default in respect of a stamp duty penalty. The Regulations mirror the Distraint by Collectors (Fees, Costs and Charges) Regulations 1994 (S.I. 1994/236) which make similar provisions in respect of such distraint upon the goods and chattels of a person who is in default of income tax, capital gains tax or corporation tax.

items.title: The Stamp Duty Reserve Tax (Amendment No. 2) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations amend the Stamp Duty Reserve Tax Regulations 1986 (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Payments to the Churches Conservation Trust Order 1999
items.description: This Order provides that for the period 1st April 2000 to 31st March 2003 sums up to a total of £3,783,000 may be paid to the Churches Conservation Trust, either out of the net proceeds of sale of redundant churches or by way of grant from the Church Commissioners.

items.title: The Social Fund Maternity and Funeral Expenses (General) Amendment Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Fund Maternity and Funeral Expenses (General) Regulations 1987 (S.I. 1987/481).

items.title: The General Optical Council (Rules relating to Injury or Disease of the Eye) Order of Council 1999
items.description: The Rules approved by this Order replace those approved by the General Optical Council (Rules relating to Injury or Disease of the Eye) Order of Council 1960. They provide that where it appears to a registered optician that a person consulting him is suffering from an injury or disease of the eye, he shall take the steps set out in rule 4 to refer that person to a registered medical practitioner for advice and treatment, except in the circumstances set out in rules 5 to 8. The power to specify such circumstances was extended by the amendment to the Opticians Act 1989 made by section 30 of the NHS (Primary Care) Act 1997, and these Rules give registered opticians the discretion to decide not to refer persons where in their judgement there is no justification to do so, or where it would be impracticable or inexpedient.

items.title: The Civil Aviation (Joint Financing) (Second Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations amend the Civil Aviation (Joint Financing) Regulations 1997 (“the principal Regulations”) as follows:

items.title: The Statistics of Trade (Customs and Excise) (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 1st January 2000, make one change to the Statistics of Trade (Customs and Excise) Regulations 1992 (S.I. 1992/2790) (“the 1992 Regulations").

items.title: The Town and Country Planning (Costs of Inquiries etc.) (Standard Daily Amount) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations, which apply to England and Wales, apply where the Secretary of State is authorised to recover costs borne by him in connection with unitary development plan, local plan or simplified planning zone inquiries or other hearings or examinations in public in respect of structure plans (“qualifying inquiries”).

items.title: The Landfill Tax (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 1st January 2000, amend the Landfill Tax Regulations 1996 (“the Regulations”) to provide a number of changes to the scheme whereby landfill site operators are entitled to credit based upon the contributions they give to approved bodies with objects concerned with the environment. The details of this scheme are to be found in Part VII of the Regulations “Credit: bodies concerned with the environment”.

items.title: The Greater London Authority Act 1999 (Commencement No. 1) Order 1999
items.description: This Order brings into force the provisions of the Greater London Authority Act 1999 specified in article 2 and for certain purposes only article 3 on 1st January 2000. The provisions specified in article 4 are brought into force on 1st April 2000.

items.title: The Greater London Authority Act 1999 (Consequential and Transitional Provisions) (Police) order 1999
items.description: This Order makes consequential and transitional provision in relation to the alteration of the boundary of the metropolitan police district effected as a result of section 323 of the Greater London Authority Act 1999. Section 323 is brought into force with effect from 1st April 2000 by The Greater London Authority Act 1999 (Commencement No. 1) Order 1999 (S.I. 1999/3271 (c. 87)). Article 2 provides that officers may be provided from the police areas of Essex, Hertfordshire and Surrey to the metropolitan police force from 1st January 2000 to 31st March 2000 in preparation for the boundary change.

items.title: The Scotland Act 1998 (Transitory and Transitional Provisions) (Finance) Amendment Order 1999
items.description: This Order amends of the Scotland Act 1998 (Transitory and Transitional Provisions) (Finance) Order 1999 (S.I. 1999/441) (“the principal Order”) which, amongst other things, makes temporary provision concerning the Auditor General for Scotland (“the Auditor General”) until provision is made by or under an Act of the Scottish Parliament.

items.title: The Local Government etc. (Scotland) Act 1994 (Exemption from Repeal) (Scotland) Order 1999
items.description: This Order exempts byelaws promoted or made by persons other than local authorities and relating to matters, which are reserved matters for purposes of the Scotland Act 1998, from ceasing to have effect on 31st December 1999 in terms of section 59(6)(a) of the Local Government etc. (Scotland) Act 1994.

items.title: The Non-Domestic Rating Contributions (England) (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: Under Part II of Schedule 8 to the Local Government Finance Act 1988, billing authorities are required to pay amounts (called non-domestic rating contributions) to the Secretary of State. Payments in respect of a provisional amount of the contributions are made during the financial year, final calculations and any adjustments of payments being made after the year ends.

items.title: The South Birmingham Mental Health National Health Service Trust (Transfers of Trust Property) Order 1999
items.description: This Order transfers on 30th December 1999 to the bodies specified in column (1) of the Schedule to the Order trust property held by the South Birmingham Mental Health National Health Service Trust. A copy of the schedule of trust property transferred to each body can be obtained from the Department of Health, Financial and Audit Support Branch, Quarry House, Quarry Hill, Leeds LS2 7UE.

items.title: The Town and Country Planning (Development Plan) (England) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations consolidate with amendments the Town and Country Planning (Development Plan) Regulations 1991 (S.I. 1991/2794) and subsequent amending Regulations (S.I. 1997/531 and S.I. 1999/981).

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Seamen’s Documents) (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Merchant Shipping (Seamen’s Documents) Regulations 1987 (“the principal Regulations”) to allow the Secretary of State to issue discharge books to persons who are British Citizens within the meaning of the British Nationality Act 1981 and have the right of abode within the United Kingdom who are or have been employed in ships other than United Kingdom ships.

items.title: The A40 Trunk Road (Western Avenue, London Borough of Ealing) (Prohibition of U-Turn) Order 1999
items.description: This version of the The A40 Trunk Road (Western Avenue, London Borough of Ealing) (Prohibition of U-Turn) Order 1999 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Sale of Solid Fuel Byelaws (Exemption from Repeal) (Scotland) Order 1999
items.description: This Order exempts the byelaws listed in the Schedule, which relate to matters which are reserved matters for the purposes of the Scotland Act 1998, from ceasing to have effect on 31st December 1999 under the provisions of section 59(6)(a) of the Local Government etc. (Scotland) Act 1994.

items.title: The Special Commissioners (Jurisdiction and Procedure) (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations amend the Special Commissioners (Jurisdiction and Procedure) Regulations 1994 (“the principal Regulations”) so as to make provision for appeals against amendments of self assessments.

items.title: The General Commissioners (Jurisdiction and Procedure) (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations amend the General Commissioners (Jurisdiction and Procedure) Regulations 1994 (“the principal Regulations”) so as to make provision for appeals against amendments by the Inland Revenue of self-assessments.

items.title: The Special Commissioners (Amendment of the Taxes Management Act 1970) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations make a consequential amendment to section 56A of the Taxes Management Act 1970 (appeals from decisions of the Special Commissioners) in the light of an amendment to the Special Commissioners (Jurisdiction and Procedure) Regulations 1994 (S.I. 1994/1811) (“the Special Commissioners Regulations”) made by the Special Commissioners (Jurisdiction and Procedure) (Amendment) Regulations 1999 (S.I. 1999/3292) (“the 1999 Regulations”).

items.title: The Medicines (Pharmacies) (Applications for Registration and Fees) Amendment Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Medicines (Pharmacies) (Applications for Registration and Fees) Regulations 1973 (“the principal Regulations ”). Regulation 2(a) increases the fees for registration of premises at which a retail pharmacy business is, or is to be, carried on from £135 to £139 and, where the premises are in Northern Ireland, from £73 to £75. Regulation 2(b) increases subsequent annual fees (retention fees) from £87 to £89 and, where the premises are in Northern Ireland, from £68 to £70. Regulation 2(c) increases the penalty for failure to pay retention fees (payable in circumstances specified in section 76(2) of the Medicines Act 1968) from £278 to £286 and, where the premises are in Northern Ireland, from £208 to £213.

items.title: The Lord Chancellor’s Advisory Committee on Legal Education and Conduct (Provisions on Abolition) Order 1999
items.description: This Order makes arrangements for the transfer, on the abolition of the Lord Chancellor’s Advisory Committee on Legal Education and Conduct (ACLEC) by section 35(1) of the Access to Justice Act 1999, of ACLEC’s property, rights and liabilities to the Lord Chancellor. It also makes arrangements for the preparation and audit of ACLEC'S final accounts.

items.title: The Education (Transition to New Framework) (New Schools, Groups and Miscellaneous) Regulations 1999 (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations amend the Education (Transition to New Framework) (New Schools, Groups and Miscellaneous) Regulations 1999.

items.title: The Public Record Office (Fees) (No. 2) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations revoke the Public Record Office (Fees) Regulations 1999 and prescribe a new range of fees to be charged for authentication of copies of records and for other services provided by the Public Record Office.

items.title: The Legal Advice and Assistance at Police Stations (Remuneration) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations allow a supplementary fee in respect of work done by duty solicitors or own solicitors at police stations on 31st December 1999 or 1st January 2000. The supplementary fee is equal to 50% of the basic fee, and is not taken into account in deciding whether the costs incurred are likely to exceed the limit prescribed by regulation 4(1)(a) of the Legal Advice and Assistance Regulations 1989 (at present £90).

items.title: The Kirkley Hall College, Ponteland, Northumberland (Dissolution) Order 1999
items.description: This Order dissolves with effect from 1st January 2000 the further education corporation established to conduct Kirkley Hall College, Ponteland, Northumberland. It provides for the transfer of its property, rights and liabilities to Northumberland College, and secures the rights of its employees by applying section 26(2) to (4) of the Further and Higher Education Act 1992.

items.title: The Public Lending Right Scheme 1982 (Commencement of Variation) (No. 2) Order 1999
items.description: This Order brings into force on 31st December 1999 a variation to the Public Lending Right Scheme 1982 made by the Secretary of State on 9th December 1999.

items.title: The Premium Savings Bonds (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations amend the Premium Savings Bonds Regulations 1972 to permit bonds to be purchased pursuant to powers of attorney. Regulation 4(2A) of the 1972 Regulations prohibits the purchase of bonds by any person on behalf of another person, subject to certain exceptions set out in Regulation 4(3). These Regulations create an additional exception under which, subject to the approval of the Director of Savings, bonds may be purchased on behalf of an eligible person by any other person acting in pursuance of a power of attorney created by the eligible person. For this purpose, “eligible person” is defined to mean two classes of person: first, anyone who may purchase bonds under Regulation 4(1) (ie any person who has attained the age of sixteen years and who is not under any legal disability otherwise than by reason of his age); secondly, any mentally disordered person (in whose name bonds could hitherto be purchased only by a receiver under the existing Regulation 4(3)(b)). The purchase of bonds in the name of a person under the age of sixteen years will continue to be possible only by a parent or other person acting in accordance with the existing Regulation 4(3)(a).

items.title: The Lloyd’s Underwriters (Special Reserve Funds) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations amend and modify Schedule 20 to the Finance Act 1993 (c. 34) (“Schedule 20” and “the Act” respectively). The principal effects of the changes are to make provision in consequence of a variation of the arrangements made by the Council of Lloyd’s for special reserve funds, under section 175(1) of the Act (regulation 3), and to make other modifications to Schedule 20 for cases where a Lloyd’s member ceases to carry on his underwriting business (regulations 4, 5 and 6).

items.title: The Welfare Reform and Pensions Act 1999 (Commencement No. 1) Order 1999
items.description: This Order provides for the coming into force of the following provisions of the Welfare Reform and Pensions Act 1999 (c. 30)–

items.title: The Passenger Transport Executives (Capital Finance) (Amendment) (England) Order 1999
items.description: Part IV of the Local Government and Housing Act 1989 (“the 1989 Act”) establishes a framework of capital finance controls for local authorities. The Passenger Transport Executives (Capital Finance) Order 1990 extends and adapts these controls so as to take account of the capital finance transactions of the Passenger Transport Executives each of which is responsible under the Transport Act 1962 (c. 37) to the Passenger Transport Authority (“PTA”) for its area.

items.title: The Registration of Births, Deaths and Marriages (Fees) Order 1999
items.description: This Order specifies the fees payable under the Acts relating to the registration of births, deaths and marriages and associated matters, from 1 April 2000.

items.title: The Maternity and Parental Leave etc. Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations, together with provisions inserted into the Employment Rights Act 1996 (“the 1996 Act") by the Employment Relations Act 1999, confer new rights to maternity and parental leave. The provisions relating to parental leave implement Council Directive 96/34/EC on the framework agreement on parental leave (OJ No.L145, 19.6.96, p.4).

items.title: The Road Traffic (Permitted Parking Area and Special Parking Area) (Borough of Medway) Order 1999
items.description: This Order designates the Borough of Medway, excluding that part of the M2 Motorway and its slip roads situated within the Borough, as both a permitted parking area and a special parking area in accordance with Schedule 3 to the Road Traffic Act 1991. It also applies with modifications various provisions of Part II of that Act to the designated area and modifies the Road Traffic Regulation Act 1984 in relation to the designated area.

items.title: The Road Traffic (Permitted Parking Area and Special Parking Area) (County of Kent) (Borough of Gravesham) Order 1999
items.description: This Order designates the Borough of Gravesham, excluding those sections of the M2 Motorway, A2 trunk road and the A289 road (Wainscott Bypass) and their on and off slip roads situated within the Borough, as both a permitted parking area and a special parking area in accordance with Schedule 3 to the Road Traffic Act 1991. It also applies with modifications various provisions of Part II of that Act to the designated area and modifies the Road Traffic Regulation Act 1984 in relation to the designated area.

items.title: The Hill Livestock (Compensatory Allowances) (Enforcement) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 16th December 1999, provide for enforcement of the rules of the United Kingdom’s Hill Livestock Compensatory Allowances Schemes in 2000 in England and Wales, and in Northern Ireland and Scotland where a farm includes land there as well as land in another of the United Kingdom’s four jurisdictions. They re-enact, with minor and drafting amendments, the enforcement provisions of the Hill Livestock (Compensatory Allowances) Regulations 1996, as amended. The rules of the Schemes for England and Wales, Scotland and Northern Ireland are laid down in separate regulations.

items.title: The Hill Livestock (Compensatory Allowances) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 16th December 1999, provide for the administration of the Hill Livestock Compensatory Allowances Scheme in England and Wales in 2000. They re-enact, with modifications, the Hill Livestock (Compensatory Allowances) Regulations 1996, as amended. In addition to minor and drafting amendments, they introduce a requirement that farmers claiming compensatory allowances must apply usual good farming practices on their land (regulation 6).

items.title: The Afghanistan (United Nations Sanctions) (Channel Islands) (Amendment) Order 1999
items.description: This Order, made under the United Nations Act 1946, corrects errors in S.I. 1999/3134 which gave effect in the Channel Islands to the imposition of restrictions pursuant to a decision of the Security Council of the United Nations in Resolution No. 1267 of 15th October 1999 on certain actions making available or otherwise transferring funds and financial resources to or for the benefit of the Taliban.

items.title: The Afghanistan (United Nations Sanctions) (Isle of Man) (Amendment) Order 1999
items.description: This Order, made under the United Nations Act 1946, corrects an error in S.I. 1999/3135 which gave effect in the Isle of Man to the imposition of restrictions pursuant to a decision of the Security Council of the United Nations in Resolution No. 1267 of 15th October 1999 on certain actions making available or otherwise transferring funds and financial resources to or for the benefit of the Taliban.

items.title: The Maximum Number of Stipendiary Magistrates Order 1999
items.description: This Order in Council provides for the maximum number of stipendiary magistrates (other than metropolitan stipendiary magistrates) appointed under the Justices of the Peace Act 1997 to be raised from 50 to 56.

items.title: The Scotland Act 1998 (Agency Arrangements) (Specification) (No. 2) Order 1999
items.description: This Order specifies functions of the Scottish Ministers for the purposes of section 93(1) of the Scotland Act 1998. Section 93(1) allows, amongst other things, the Scottish Ministers to make arrangements for any of their specified functions to be exercised on their behalf by a Minister of the Crown.

items.title: The Scotland Act 1998 (Transfer of Functions to the Scottish Ministers etc.) (No. 2) Order 1999
items.description: This Order, made under the Scotland Act 1998, provides for certain functions of a Minister of the Crown, so far as they are exercisable by him in or as regards Scotland, to be exercisable by the Scottish Ministers instead of by the Minister concerned.

items.title: The Holders of Hereditary Peerages (Extension of the Franchise) (Transitional Provisions) Order 1999
items.description: This Order makes transitional provision to enable the holders of hereditary peerages to whom section 3(1) of the House of Lords Act 1999 applies to vote at parliamentary elections. Section 3(1) does not apply to a peer to whom section 2 of that Act applies (section 3(2)).

items.title: The Transnational Information and Consultation of Employees Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations implement Council Directive 97/74/EC (O.J. L10, 16.1.98, p.22). That Directive extends to the United Kingdom Directive 94/45/EC on the establishment of a European Works Council or a procedure in Community-scale undertakings or Community-scale groups of undertakings for the purposes of informing and consulting employees (O.J. L254, 30.9.94, p.64).

items.title: The Income Support (General) and Jobseeker’s Allowance Amendment (No. 2) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations amend the Income Support (General) Regulations 1987 (S.I. 1987/1967) and the Jobseeker’s Allowance Regulations 1996 (S.I. 1996/207).

items.title: The Coroners (Amendment) Rules 1999
items.description: These Rules amend the Coroners Rules 1984 in two main respects. First, rule 4 provides for authorised advocates (as defined in section 119(1) of the Courts and Legal Services Act 1990) to be entitled to examine witnesses at an inquest on behalf of interested persons rather than only barristers and solicitors. Secondly, rules 9, 11 and 18 make provision in respect of an inquest which is resumed following an adjournment for a public inquiry under section 17A of the Coroners Act 1988 (as inserted by section 71 of the Access to Justice Act 1999). In particular, rule 11 makes provision for admitting the findings of the public inquiry as documentary evidence at the resumed inquest.

items.title: The Income Support (General) Amendment (No. 2) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations amend the Income Support (General) Regulations 1987 (S.I. 1987/1967) (“the Income Support Regulations”).

items.title: The Double Taxation Relief (Taxes on Income) (Foreign Interest and Dividends) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations replace the Double Taxation Relief (Taxes on Income) (Foreign Loan Interest) Regulations 1988 (S.I. 1988/88).

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (FLAG Atlantic UK Limited) Order 1999
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to FLAG Atlantic UK Limited a licence on 2nd November 1999 under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in the licence. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 15th December 1999. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, FLAG Atlantic UK Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force on 13th January 2000.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (E.V. Limited) Order 1999
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to E.V. Limited a licence on 18th October 1999 under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in the licence. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 15th December 1999. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, E.V. Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force on 13th January 2000.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Pangea Networks (UK) Limited) Order 1999
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Pangea Networks (UK) Limited a licence on 2nd November 1999 under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in the licence. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 15th December 1999. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Pangea Networks (UK) Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force on 13th January 2000.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Guernsey Telecoms) Order 1999
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Guernsey Telecoms a licence on 2nd November 1999 under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in the licence. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 15th December 1999. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Guernsey Telecoms will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force on 13th January 2000.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Norweb Telecom Limited) Order 1999
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Norweb Telecom Limited a licence on 18th October 1999 under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in the licence. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 15th December 1999. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Norweb Telecom Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force on 13th January 2000.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Racal Telecommunications Limited) Order 1999
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Racal Telecommunications Limited a licence on 18th October 1999 under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in the licence. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 15th December 1999. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Racal Telecommunications Limited will be a public telecommunications operator in respect of the telecommunication systems run under that licence when the Order comes into force on 13th January 2000.

items.title: The Travel Documents (Fees) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations prescribe fees in connection with applications to the Secretary of State for three types of Home Office travel document: Convention travel documents, documents of identity and certificates of identity.

items.title: The Combined Probation Areas (Bedfordshire) Order 1999
items.description: This Order amends the Combined Probation Areas Order 1986 (S.I. 1986/1713) to take account of the abolition of the petty sessional divisions of Ampthill, Bedford, Biggleswade, Dunstable, Leighton Buzzard and Luton and the creation of the new petty sessional divisions of Bedford and Mid Bedfordshire and Luton and South Bedfordshire. The changes to the petty sessional divisions in question are effected by the Petty Sessional Divisions (Bedfordshire) Order 1999 (S.I. 1999/150) which will come into force, subject to certain exceptions, on 1st January 2000.

items.title: The Combined Probation Areas (Staffordshire) Order 1999
items.description: This Order amends the Combined Probation Areas Order 1986 (S.I. 1986/1713) to take account of the combination of the petty sessional divisions of Mid Staffordshire and Rugeley to form the new petty sessional division of Mid Staffordshire and Rugeley, the combination of the petty sessional divisions of Cannock and Seisdon to form the new petty sessional division of Cannock and Seisdon, the combination of the petty sessional divisions of Lichfield and Tamworth to form the new petty sessional division of Lichfield and Tamworth and the combination of the petty sessional divisions of Newcastle-under-Lyme and Pirehill North and Staffordshire Moorlands to form the new petty sessional division of North Staffordshire. The changes to the petty sessional divisions in question are effected by the Petty Sessional Divisions (Staffordshire) Order 1999 (S.I. 1999/2399) which comes into force, subject to certain exceptions, on 1st January 2000.

items.title: The Combined Probation Areas (Kent) Order 1999
items.description: This Order amends the Combined Probation Areas Order 1986 (S.I. 1986/1713) to take account of the combination of the petty sessional divisions of Dartford and Gravesham to form the new petty sessional division of Dartford and Gravesham. The changes to the petty sessional divisions in question are effected by the Petty Sessional Divisions (Kent) Order 1999 (S.I. 1999/2105), which will come into force, subject to certain exceptions, on 1st January 2000.

items.title: The Combined Probation Areas (Wiltshire) Order 1999
items.description: This Order amends the Combined Probation Areas Order 1986 (S.I. 1986/1713) to take account of the combination of the petty sessional divisions of North Wiltshire and West Wiltshire to form the new petty sessional division of North West Wiltshire and the combination of the petty sessional divisions of Kennet and Salisbury to form the new petty sessional division of South East Wiltshire. The petty sessional division of Swindon is not affected by this Order. The changes to the petty sessional divisions in question are effected by the Petty Sessional Divisions (Wiltshire) Order 1999 (S.I. 1999/1500), as amended by the Petty Sessional Divisions (Wiltshire) (Amendment) Order 1999 (S.I. 1999/1850), which comes into force, subject to certain exceptions, on 1st January 2000.

items.title: The Access to Justice Act 1999 (Commencement No. 2 and Transitional Provisions) Order 1999
items.description: This Order brings the following provisions of the Access to Justice Act 1999 into force:

items.title: The Export of Goods (Control) (Amendment No. 2) Order 1999
items.description: This Order adds Eritrea and Ethiopia to the list of countries to which arms in transit through the United Kingdom may not be exported without a licence.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Carriage of Cargoes) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations consolidate the Merchant Shipping (Carriage of Cargoes) Regulations 1997 and the Merchant Shipping (Carriage of Cargoes) (Amendment) Regulations 1997, implementing Chapter VI of the International Convention for the Safety of Life at Sea 1974 (SOLAS) as amended. The Regulations also implement amendments adopted on 14th June 1996 by the Maritime Safety Committee of the International Maritime Organization at its 66th session by Resolution MSC.47(66).

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Seamen’s Wages and Accounts) (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations amend the Merchant Shipping (Seamen’s Wages and Accounts) Regulations 1972 and the Merchant Shipping (Seamen’s Wages and Accounts) (Fishing Vessels) Regulations 1972. The Regulations reduce (in both instruments) to £100 the amount that can be deducted from a seaman’s wages for the expense or loss caused to an employer by the seaman’s absence without leave. This brings the maximum deduction into line with a similar limit in section 70(3) of the Merchant Shipping Act 1995.

items.title: The Self-Governing Schools (Publication of Proposals for Discontinuance) (Scotland) Regulations 1999
The Beef Bones (Amendment) (England) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations amend the Beef Bones Regulations 1997 (S.I. 1997/2959) in so far as they extend to England. Those 1997 Regulations extend to the whole of Great Britain.

items.title: The Working Time Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations amend the Working Time Regulations 1998, which implement Council Directive 93/104/EC concerning certain aspects of the organisation of working time (O.J. No. L307, 13.12.93, p. 18).

items.title: The Employment Relations Act 1999 (Commencement No. 3 and Transitional Provision) Order 1999
items.description: This Order brings into force, on 17th December 1999, the provisions of the Employment Relations Act 1999 specified in Article 2 of the Order, which relate to the Secretary of State’s power to change the amount of certain awards of employment tribunals and other sums payable under employment legislation.

items.title: Employment Rights (Increase of Limits) Order 1999 (revoked)
items.description: This Order increases, from 1st February 2000, the limits applying to certain awards of employment tribunals, and other amounts payable under employment legislation, as specified in the Schedule to the Order.

items.title: The Greater London Authority Act 1999 (Commencement No. 2) Order 1999
items.description: This Order brings into force on 14th December 1999, in so far as not already in force, Part I (and accordingly Schedules 1 to 3) of the Greater London Authority Act 1999, which provide for establishment of the Greater London Authority.

items.title: The Legal Advice and Assistance (Scope) (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: This version of the The Legal Advice and Assistance (Scope) (Amendment) Regulations 1999 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Legal Aid (Prescribed Panels) (Amendment) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations amend the Legal Aid (Prescribed Panels) Regulations 1999 so as to designate the Family Franchise Panel and the Immigration Franchise Panel as prescribed panels for the purposes of section 32(7) of the Legal Aid Act 1988. They provide that an assisted person’s right to select an authorised litigator for the purposes of assistance by way of representation or representation in relation to family proceedings, and for the purposes of representation in relation to immigration proceedings, is limited to members of the respective panels.

items.title: The Non-Domestic Rating (Chargeable Amounts) (England) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations, which extend to England only, make provision for the period of five years beginning on 1st April 2000 in relation to non-domestic rates under Part III of the Local Government Finance Act 1988 (“the Act”), as to the chargeable amount for which a ratepayer is liable in certain circumstances. They also make provision as to the giving of certificates by valuation officers.

items.title: The Greater London Authority (Assembly Constituencies and Returning Officers) Order 1999
items.description: Article 2(1) of this Order provides for the names and areas of the 14 Assembly constituencies for the purposes of the election of the constituency members of the London Assembly, as specified in columns (1) and (2) of the Table. (The other 11 members of the London Assembly are to be members for the whole of Greater London. The London Assembly and the Mayor of London together constitute the Greater London Authority.)

items.description: This Order establishes the Peterlee Education Action Zone, which comprises the schools listed in Schedule 2. The Order also provides (in articles 4 and 5) for the membership of the Education Action Forum for the zone.

items.description: This Order brings into force on the dates provided for in the Order various provisions of the Northern Ireland Act 1998.

items.title: The Withernsea and Southern Holderness Rural Achievement Education Action Zone Order 1999
items.description: This Order establishes the Withernsea and Southern Holderness Rural Achievement Education Action Zone, which comprises the schools listed in Schedule 2. The Order also provides (in articles 4 and 5) for the membership of the Education Action Forum for the zone.

items.title: The Road Traffic (Permitted Parking Area and Special Parking Area) (County of Kent) (District of Thanet) Order 1999
items.description: This Order designates the District of Thanet, excluding the A299 road for the whole of its length in the District and the A253 road for part of its length in the District, as both a permitted parking area and a special parking area in accordance with Schedule 3 to the Road Traffic Act 1991. It also applies with modifications various provisions of Part II of that Act to the designated area and modifies the Road Traffic Regulation Act 1984 in relation to the designated area.

items.title: The Road Traffic (Permitted Parking Area and Special Parking Area) (County of Kent) (City of Canterbury) Order 1999
items.description: This Order designates the District of the City of Canterbury, excluding that section of the A2 and its on and off slip roads situated within the District, that section of the A299 and its on and off slip roads situated within the District and that section of the Broomfield Link Road situated within the District, as both a permitted parking area and a special parking area in accordance with Schedule 3 to the Road Traffic Act 1991. It also applies with modifications various provisions of Part II of that Act to the designated area and modifies the Road Traffic Regulation Act 1984 in relation to the designated area.

items.title: The Road Traffic (Permitted Parking Area and Special Parking Area) (County of Kent) (Borough of Swale) Order 1999
items.description: This Order designates the Borough of Swale, excluding that part of the M2 Motorway in the Borough, a section of the A2 trunk road and its slip roads in the Borough, a section of the A249 trunk road and its slip roads in the Borough and the A299 Clearway and its slip roads for its whole length in the Borough, as both a permitted parking area and a special parking area in accordance with Schedule 3 to the Road Traffic Act 1991. It also applies with modifications various provisions of Part II of that Act to the designated area and modifies the Road Traffic Regulation Act 1984 in relation to the designated area.

items.title: The Road Traffic (Permitted Parking Area and Special Parking Area) (County of Kent) (District of Sevenoaks) Order 1999
items.description: This Order designates the District of Sevenoaks, excluding the M20 Motorway, the M25 Motorway, the M26 Motorway and the A21 trunk road, together with their on and off slip roads, for the whole of their lengths in the District and a section of the A20 trunk road, together with its on and off slip roads, as both a permitted parking area and a special parking area in accordance with Schedule 3 to the Road Traffic Act 1991. It also applies with modifications various provisions of Part II of that Act to the designated area and modifies the Road Traffic Regulation Act 1984 in relation to the designated area.

items.title: The Road Traffic (Permitted Parking Area and Special Parking Area) (County of Kent) (Borough of Tunbridge Wells) Order 1999
items.description: This Order designates the Borough of Tunbridge Wells, excepting that section of the A21 trunk road and its slip roads situated within the Borough, as both a permitted parking area and a special parking area in accordance with Schedule 3 to the Road Traffic Act 1991. It also applies with modifications various provisions of Part II of that Act to the designated area and modifies the Road Traffic Regulation Act 1984 in relation to the designated area.

items.description: This Order establishes the Speke Garston Excellent Education Action Zone, which comprises the schools listed in Schedule 2. The Order also provides (in articles 4 and 5) for the membership of the Education Action Forum for the zone.

items.title: The Social Security Benefits Up-rating (No. 2) Order 1999
items.description: This Order is made under section 152(1) of the Social Security Administration Act 1992 (c. 5). That section enables the Secretary of State, on being satisfied that a mistake has occurred in the preparation of an order under section 150 of the Social Security Administration Act 1992, to vary the amount of any one of the sums specified in an enactment mentioned in subsection (1)(a) of that section.

items.title: The Building Regulations (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Building Regulations 1991 (S.I. 1991/2768) to substitute a new Part B in Schedule 1.

items.title: The Export of Goods (Control) (Amendment No. 6) Order 1999
items.description: This Order further amends the Export of Goods (Control) Order 1994 by

items.title: The Gloucester Education Achievement Zone Order 1999
items.description: This Order establishes the Gloucester Education Achievement Zone, which comprises the schools listed in Schedule 1. The Order also provides (in articles 4 and 5) for the membership of the Education Action Forum for the zone.

items.title: The Road Transport (Passenger Vehicles Cabotage) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations implement in Great Britain Council Regulation (EC) No. 12/98 of 11 December 1997 (“the EC Regulation") which lays down the conditions under which road passenger transport carriers of member States may operate cabotage transport operations.

items.title: The Camborne, Pool and Redruth Success Zone Education Action Zone Order 1999
items.description: This Order establishes the Camborne, Pool and Redruth Success Zone, which comprises the schools listed in Schedule 2. The Order also provides (in articles 4 and 5) for the membership of the Education Action Forum for the zone.

items.title: The Merger Reference (Universal Foods Corporation and Pointing Holdings Limited) (Interim Provision) (Revocation) Order 1999
items.description: This Order revokes the Merger Reference (Universal Foods Corporation and Pointing Holdings Limited) (Interim Provision) Order 1999. The earlier Order was made to prevent action which might prejudice the investigation by the Competition Commission of the merger between Universal Foods Corporation and Pointing Holdings Limited. The report of the Competition Commission on their investigation has been published by The Stationery Office (Cm 4544).

items.title: The A40 Trunk Road (Ealing and Hammersmith & Fulham) Red Route Experimental Traffic Order 1999 Experimental Variation Order 1999
items.description: This version of the The A40 Trunk Road (Ealing and Hammersmith & Fulham) Red Route Experimental Traffic Order 1999 Experimental Variation Order 1999 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The London Borough of Barnet (Trunk Roads) Red Route (Priority Traffic Lanes) Experimental Traffic Order 1999 Revocation Order 1999
items.description: This version of the The London Borough of Barnet (Trunk Roads) Red Route (Priority Traffic Lanes) Experimental Traffic Order 1999 Revocation Order 1999 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.description: This version of the The A23 Trunk Road (Croydon) Red Route Traffic Order 1999 Experimental Variation Order 1999 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A20 Trunk Road (Greenwich) Red Route Traffic Order 1996 Variation Order 1999
items.description: This version of the The A20 Trunk Road (Greenwich) Red Route Traffic Order 1996 Variation Order 1999 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Welfare Reform and Pensions Act 1999 (Commencement No. 2) Order 1999
items.description: This Order provides for the coming into force on the dates listed below of the following provisions of the Welfare Reform and Pensions Act 1999 (c. 30) relating to national insurance contributions:

items.title: The Food (Animal Products from Belgium) (Emergency Control) (England and Wales) Order 1999
items.description: This Order, which applies to England and Wales, revokes, and re-enacts with certain changes, the Food (Animals and Animal Products from Belgium) (Emergency Control) (England and Wales) (No. 3) Order 1999 (S.I. 1999/2798 “the revoked Order”) and implements in England and Wales, in relation to food, Commission Decision 1999/788/EC on protective measures with regard to contamination by dioxins of certain products of porcine and poultry origin intended for human or animal consumption (OJ No. L310, 4.12.1999, p.62).

items.title: The Animal Feedingstuffs from Belgium (Control) (England and Wales) (No. 4) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations, which apply to England and Wales, revoke, and re-enact with certain changes, the Animal Feedingstuffs from Belgium (Control) (England and Wales) (No. 3) Regulations 1999 (S.I. 1999/2799 “the revoked Regulations") and implement in England and Wales, in relation to products for animal feeding, Commission Decision 1999/788/EC on protective measures with regard to contamination by dioxins of certain products of porcine and poultry origin intended for human or animal consumption (OJ No. L310, 4.12.1999, p. 62).

items.title: Local Authorities (Capital Finance) (Amendment) (England) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Local Authorities (Capital Finance) Regulations 1997 by substituting a new regulation 40 for the existing text.

items.title: The Police (Secretary of State’s Objectives) (No. 3) Order 1999
items.description: This Order sets out the Secretary of State’s objective for the policing of the areas of all police authorities established under section 3 of the Police Act 1996. Section 6(2)(a) of the 1996 Act requires police authorities to have regard to these objectives in the discharge of their functions. Local policing objectives determined under section 7 of the 1996 Act must be consistent with these objectives. Section 8 of the 1996 Act requires police authorities to issue local policing plans which must give particulars of these objectives.

items.title: The Criminal Justice Act 1988 (Commencement No. 13) Order 1999
items.description: This Order brings into force the repeal of sections 39 and 40 of the Powers of Criminal Courts Act 1973, which is effected by section 170(2) of, and Schedule 16 to, the Criminal Justice Act 1988 (“the 1988 Act”). The repealed provisions relate to criminal bankruptcy orders. The power to make such orders was abolished on 3rd April 1989 by section 101 of the 1988 Act.

items.title: The Crime and Disorder Act 1998 (Commencement No. 6) Order 1999
items.description: This Order brings into force on 1st April 2000 sections 73 to 79 of the Crime and Disorder Act 1998, subject to minor transitional arrangements and savings. These provisions relate to detention and training orders. The Order also brings into force on that date a number of related minor provisions.

items.title: The Youth Justice and Criminal Evidence Act 1999 (Commencement No. 1) Order 1999
items.description: This Order brings into force section 67(2) of, and Schedule 5 to, the Youth Justice and Criminal Evidence Act 1999 on 1st January 2000. Schedule 5 contains pre-consolidation amendments relating to youth justice. The Order also brings into force a number of repeals of provisions in the 1999 Act which are consequential upon the introduction of detention and training orders under the Crime and Disorder Act 1998 (c. 37) on 1st April 2000.

items.title: The A102 Trunk Road (Greenwich) Red Route (Clearway) Traffic Order 1999
items.description: This Order is based on a comprehensive model Order produced by the Traffic Director for London to assist understanding of the regulations, and to assist future consolidation of Orders relating to priority routes.

items.title: The Plant Protection Products (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations, which apply in Great Britain, further amend the Plant Protection Products Regulations 1995 (S.I. 1995/887) as amended (“the principal Regulations”) which implement in Great Britain Council Directive 91/414/EEC concerning the placing of plant protection products on the market (OJ No. L230, 19.8.91, p. 1 as read with OJ No. L170, 25.6.92, p. 40) (“the Directive”) as amended.

items.title: The Health Development Agency (Establishment and Constitution) Order 1999
items.description: This Order provides for the establishment and constitution of a Special Health Authority, to be known as the Health Development Agency (“the Agency ”), to exercise on behalf of the Secretary of State such functions in connection with developing and ensuring high quality public health provision and health promotion and such other functions as he may direct it to perform.

items.title: The Health Development Agency Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations make provision concerning the membership and procedure of the Health Development Agency which is a Special Health Authority established under the National Health Service Act 1977 by the Health Development Agency (Establishment and Constitution) Order 1999.

items.title: The Greater London Authority Act 1999 (Commencement No. 3 and Transitional Finance Provisions) Order 1999
items.description: This Order brings into force specified provisions of the Greater London Authority Act 1999 relating to finance. Article 2 of this Order brings into force on the 12th January 2000 certain finance provisions in Chapters I to III and VI of Part III, subject to the transitional provisions set out in Schedule 1 to this Order, certain preparatory provisions in Part IV relating to the transition from London Regional Transport to Transport for London, preparations for Public-Private Partnership Agreements and GLA roads, preparatory provisions in Parts XI and XII. Article 3 brings into force certain finance related provisions in Chapters V and VI of Part III and Part XI, on 8th May 2000, when the GLA will come into existence. Article 4 brings into force the remaining provisions in Part III, Chapters I to III, V and VI, and further provisions in Part XI, on 3rd July 2000, when the GLA and functional bodies will assume most of their powers.

items.title: The Greater London Authority Act 1999 (Transitional and Consequential Finance Provisions) Order 1999
items.description: Article 2 of this Order adapts, in respect of the 2000/2001 financial year only, provisions in the Local Government Finance Act 1988 which govern the payment of revenue support grant, special grant, non-domestic rates and additional grant to local authorities. These adaptations take account of the Greater London Authority and its functional bodies.

items.title: The Local Authorities (Calculation of Council Tax Base) (Amendment–Greater London Authority) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations insert into the Local Authorities (Calculation of Council Tax Base) Regulations 1992 references to the provisions in the Greater London Authority Act 1999 which provide for the calculation of the tax base of the Greater London Authority, or of a part of the area of the Greater London Authority.

items.title: The Local Government Pension Scheme (Amendment etc.) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations affect the Local Government Pension Scheme Regulations 1997 (“the principal Regulations”) which comprise the Local Government Pension Scheme (“the Scheme”).

items.title: The Apportionment of Money in the National Lottery Distribution Fund Order 1999
items.description: This version of the The Apportionment of Money in the National Lottery Distribution Fund Order 1999 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Water Industry Act 1999 (Commencement No. 2) Order 1999
items.description: This Order commences the remaining provisions of the Water Industry Act 1999 (“the Act”) that were not brought into force by the Water Industry Act 1999 (Commencement No. 1) (Scotland) Order 1999 (S.I. 1999/133).

items.title: The Water Industry (Charges) (Vulnerable Groups) Regulations 1999
items.description: These Regulations define vulnerable groups who are to be given assistance with water and sewerage charges by water and sewerage undertakers. Regulation 2 requires undertakers' charges schemes to reflect provision for these groups and prescribes classes of persons who are eligible to apply for assistance. Regulation 3 states the nature of the assistance to be provided. Regulation 4 governs the method by which claimants are to establish their eligibility to assistance.

items.title: The Water Industry (Prescribed Conditions) Regulations 1999
items.description: Section 144A of the Water Industry Act 1991 enables a consumer to serve notice electing for charging for the water supply to his home by reference to volume. Section 144B of the Act restricts a water or sewerage undertaker from beginning to fix such charges by volume without the consumer’s consent or unless there has been a change of occupation.

items.title: The Pet Travel Scheme (Pilot Arrangements) (England) Order 1999
items.description: This Order, which extends to England, creates, in limited circumstances, an exemption from the requirements for pet cats and pet dogs imported into England to be placed in quarantine under the Rabies (Importation of Dogs, Cats and Other Mammals) Order 1974, S. I. 1974/2211. The Order does not extend to Wales or Scotland, but if an animal has been brought into England in accordance with this Order it can then be taken from England into Wales or Scotland.

items.title: The Workington Harbour Revision Order 1999
